dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Home Depot is reporting two purchases on a card ( ending in XXXX ) which I have never had in my possession : {$380.00} in XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/, & {$120.00} in a XXXX Home Depot on XX/XX/. I have advised Home Depot that I never had that card, & that I did not make those charges. 

Home Depot removed the {$120.00} from my statement but the {$380.00} charge is still showing. I have changed my account password to help prevent this type of fraud in the future.

Home Depot is now tracking a new card # XXXX on my account. I have not received the referenced card.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative: On XXXX XXXX someone went to Home Depot and made a purchase under my name of an amount of XXXX XXXX dont know how they did this because I had my credit card on me on this day and was out of the country. I called in may to discuss this matter in attempts to open up an investigation but they really didnt listen to what I had to say. After calling within XXXX weeks they sent me a letter saying their investigation was done and that I was responsible for paying that amount to them. So I went ahead and called again and told them it was not fair for me to get charged such a large amount for something that I didnt purchase. Therefore, I asked them to please open up again the investigation, in which they then told me they did open it up. However, I continued getting calls from Home Depot and text messages saying I had a missed payment. So I called again and I even told them I had proof I was not in the country when that purchase was made under my name so they gave me a fax number and I even had to take money out of my own pocket to pay to send the proof to them. I called a XXXX later and they told me they had not received anything to go ahead an fax them again so I went again and fax it and had to yet again take money out of my own pocket to pay again. I then waited a XXXX of days and I called and checked and they said no we dont have anything from you. This is really irresponsible on Home Depots part especially because they are such a big corporation and have continued to ignore me and wrongfully charge me for a fraud payment that they allowed to happen. Throughout this process I have felt extremely ignored and have just been forced to continue wasting my own money to continue to fight for this situation to be corrected. Money that I do not have to be wasting. I have done my part and paid to fax the proof that I was not in the country at the time of this purchase multiple times. They have not done anything and are continuing to not do anything to solve this problem. Due to this situation, my credit is now even being negatively affected. I would like this situation to be resolved without it becoming a legal situation. This situation has caused me a lot of financial stress and I would not be doing this whole situation or even spending my own money if I was responsible for this. With the concrete proof that I have of not being in the country during this purchase it should not be this difficult to understand and acknowledge that it was not me.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am filing a complaint regarding unresolved fraudulent charges on my Barclays JetBlue Credit Card, despite confirmed fraud and explicit instructions from the merchant ( XXXX XXXX ) to pursue a chargeback. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, two unauthorized transactions were charged to my Barclays JetBlue Credit Card : {$600.00} XXXX XXXX {$600.00} XXXX XXXX XXXX Confirmation # : XXXX On or about XX/XX/XXXX, my XXXX XXXX Bank account was hacked without my knowledge or authorization. I promptly reported the fraud to XXXX XXXX. JetBlue confirmed the unauthorized access, suspended my account, and blocked all access. 
A fraud case was opened by JetBlue ( Case # XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX ). 

On XX/XX/XXXX, a XXXX  customer service representative instructed me to file a dispute with Barclays Credit Card for services not rendered. I complied and filed the dispute as directed by the merchant. Barclays resolved the dispute unfavorably, and no funds were returned. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, another XXXX customer service agent explicitly instructed me to attempt to get a refund from Barclays Jetblue Credit card by filing a " chargeback '' with Barclays, confirming that XXXX would not return the funds directly. ( I have documentation/screenshots of this instruction. ) Despite : confirmed unauthorized account access, a documented fraud case opened by XXXX, and repeated instructions from the merchant to pursue a chargeback, Barclays has refused to reverse the fraudulent charges, leaving me financially responsible for funds stolen due to fraud. 

I have made continuous good-faith efforts to resolve this matter with both XXXX and Barclays since XX/XX/XXXX, with no resolution. I am requesting assistance from the Attorney Generals Office to ensure that consumer fraud protections are properly applied and that these unauthorized charges are reversed. 



The following documents are included in support of this complaint : -Original transaction receipts for the disputed XXXX charges XXXX XXXX Bank account history, including records identifying the individuals who utilized the stolen travel credits -Copies of written correspondence sent to both XXXX XXXX and Barclays Bank requesting a fair resolution

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Company : Barclays Bank Delaware Account Type : Credit Card Transaction Date : XX/XX/year> Amount : {$1500.00} Merchant : XXXX XXXX XXXX / XXXX XXXX Summary of Issue : I am disputing an unauthorized {$1500.00} charge posted on XX/XX/XXXX to my Barclays credit-card account by XXXX  XXXX  XXXX ( also operating as XXXX  XXXX ). The merchant never obtained my authorization for this transaction, and Barclays improperly denied my dispute even though the merchant produced no valid proof of consent or contract supporting the charge. 
Facts : XXXX XXXX XXXX had access to two of my credit-card numbers from prior transactions. They randomly charged one card on XX/XX/XXXX without my permission. 
The merchant submitted XXXX XXXX  screenshots to Barclays, but those communications do not contain any statement authorizing a {$1500.00} payment. They only show general correspondence about our accountnot authorization. 
For XXXX and XXXX, the merchant provided invoices and I paid voluntarily ( XXXX was billed correctly to a different card ). There was no invoice or written agreement for any XXXX charge. 
The XX/XX/XXXX transaction was therefore arbitrary and unauthorized. The merchant has never produced a signed contract, invoice, or written consent for it. 
Barclays denied my dispute even though Regulation Z ( 12 CFR 1026.12 ( b ) ) requires the issuer to demonstrate that the transaction was authorized. That burden of proof was never met.

Additionally, the company failed to deliver the agreed service of providing ten qualified leads within ninety ( 90 ) days. Only one lead was ever provided. After that failure, they claimed they would work for free, which is not measurable, enforceable, or an acceptable substitute for contractual performance.

Requested Resolution : I request that the CFPB review Barclays Bank Delawares handling of this billing dispute and require Barclays to : Reverse the {$1500.00} charge and all related interest or fees ; Acknowledge the transaction as unauthorized under Regulation Z ; and Ensure future compliance with dispute-resolution standards by requiring documentary proof of cardholder authorization before denying similar disputes.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
This is a complaint about Citis handling of my dispute with them. I placed a dispute with them within the allowed time window for a dispute to be made. The representative told me that I need to wait for citi to reach out if they needed more info. 

Citi failed to send me a letter requesting additional info on time, and closed out my dispute. I called to reopen it, and was told that it shouldnt be an issue since I didnt receive the letter notice. I provided them all of the relevant info about the claim, and it kept getting closed because too much time had passed. 

They retroactively added the letter in my documents tab on the online banking portal AFTER closing my dispute, backdating it to when it was supposed to be sent out as a way to cover their tracks. I want to stress that this letter did not exist in my documents tab prior to them closing my dispute the first time for failure to provide them with additional info. 

The dispute was filed on time, closed by Citi due to me not responding to a letter that they themselves failed to send out, and any attempt at reopening this dispute has been met with the same canned response saying too much time has passed. 

These are counterfeit goods. I cant get a refund from the merchant. I have every right to be able to chargeback this transaction.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
XXXX billed my Sears Mastercard credit card {$500.00} for airline tickets on XX/XX/XXXX. I did not make the charge ; I did not authorize the charge. I have never done any business with XXXX. This is a letter that I sent to Mastercard on XXXX XXXX XXXX regarding the ongoing lack of corrective action by Mastercard : " Attempting to resolve a fraudulent charge with Citi XXXX card caused the entire XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX to become an ongoing period of torment for me. Commencing with my first call to customer service back in XX/XX/XXXX and continuing through the following months, I have been plagued by automated phone calls, emails, text messages, and an occasional form letter telling me that I have an overdue bill payment and that the contact is being made in an attempt to collect a debt. NO ONE from Citi XXXX card has ever contacted me to explain how my credit card number was apparently fraudulently used to pay for airline tickets. My use of the term apparently is based upon what XXXX and Citi XXXX card have said as I have not seen anything that actually documents that my credit card number was used for the charges that I have been billed for or specifically where those charges were made. The destination of the fraudulently charged tickets was the XXXX XXXX, but where were the tickets purchased and who purchased them? 
Repeated written and called-in requests to speak to someone concerning the ongoing harassment and billing have been in vain. I was told that I had not responded to a message from XXXX XXXX that contained a statement for me to sign denying that I made the charge. That statement was sent by regular USPS and took weeks to get to me, arriving several days before the return by date. I sent the signed document to XXXX XXXX by Certified Mail and I have the return receipt as well as the USPS electronic tracking record for both that document with enclosures and a subsequent set of documents that I also sent to XXXX XXXX. The alleged debt started off as a fraudulent charge on my credit card that I reported back at the end of XXXX when I noticed a charge from XXXX in XXXX XXXX XXXX, UT that appeared on my online account before I had received a monthly statement. A second charge subsequently appeared. Then, a confusing series of additions and subtractions from my account were posted online. 
I initially called the number on the back of my then-Sears Mastercard to report the fraudulent charge. The person I spoke with had a very thick accent and there was a great of noise in the background wherever he was. I kept repeating that I could not understand him. The only thing that I could decipher was that the person said that he would report that my credit card was lost or stolen. I said that my card was not lost or stolen. He told me that he would report that it was stolen and I would get a new card. Thats when the Citi XXXX card ending in XXXX came into the maze. 
It seems that there is no way for a victimized customer to get a phone number or an email address that leads to an identifiable person within the CitiBank organization. The best that can be hoped for is a first name that might be a pseudonym and there is no number where the same person can be reached again. When the harassing calls to submit payment come in, they are automated and are actually a good example of entrapment as theres no actual person available to speak to, just options to pay ; no options to dispute the charge. 
In the law enforcement world, when there is a suspicion of an illegal act, such as stolen identity or a fraudulent billing, the investigation would concentrate on the incident and the perpetrator and would not concentrate on badgering or demeaning the victim ( s ) rather than identifying the perpetrator ( s ). Establishing when and where the original charges were made and the name of the person who made those charges would have been a proper opening of the investigation as would have been ascertaining from XXXX just exactly how the charges were made ; whether electronically or in person. How was the person who made the charges identified at that time? 
XXXX billed my credit card deliberately or mistakenly. Im sure that the charge was made during the commission of a criminal act. Someone, whether it was the person who made the charges or an accomplice, apparently took one or more flights to or from the XXXX XXXX on XXXX airline. It wasnt me. Citi XXXX card should be protecting me, their customer, rather than demanding that I pay for a charge that I didnt make with XXXX XXXX 
Please check my past use of the now-Citi XXXX XXXX, previously a Sears Mastercard , that I had since XXXX You will note that it was never used to pay for a flight. You will see that it was typically used to pay for antiques that were bought at auctions and the charges were usually paid off monthly. 
The fact that the fraudulently acquired airline tickets were used for travel to the XXXX XXXX and that other persons besides me were also fraudulently charged for tickets to the XXXX XXXX via XXXX strongly suggests that there is more than just credit card fraud taking place. The person that informed me of an identical situation involving fraudulent use of a MasterCard at XXXX for tickets to the XXXX XXXX had a XXXX MasterCard issued by XXXX XXXXAt some point, the original two ( or more ) charged tickets were temporarily taken off of my account. Then one reappeared. After spending a lot of time making phone calls trying to find out why there was a new charge on my account, a lady named XXXX told me that I had been credited for the two fraudulent charges, but then another charge came up in either XXXX or XXXX. She said that I was being billed again because I had not reported that charge as being fraudulent. I attempted to explain that I didnt report that charge because it was the same number as one of the previous charges that was supposedly being investigated, and, additionally, no one had notified me that there was another charge. It was a rebilling. If there was in fact a second charge ( or third, fourth or whatever charge as none of this has been logical ), why havent I gotten any of the fraud alerts that I used to get when I was attempting to use the card for my own purchases? 
It seems as if something either got diverted or overlooked during the merger/sale of Sears Mastercard tXXXX Citi XXXX XXXX. After having the Sears card since XXXX, I had gotten used to quality service and there was real customer service. That all went downhill in XXXX. 
Last week, I called several numbers listed online for Citi XXXX card and got transferred to other persons, some of whom spoke clearly, two did not. A lady named XXXX told me that I had to report the latest fraudulent charge in order for it to be investigated. She transferred me to someone that I could not understand who then transferred me to a gentleman named XXXX who was supposedly in the fraud department. No one gave me any of the phone numbers for the transferred calls, not that it would have made any difference as its impossible to do any follow-up when dealing through the phone maze and XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX why are there two different names? Why are there two different operations with different XXXX XXXX Why are there two account/case numbers ( XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ) for the same incident? Are different cases open for the old XXXX card and the new Citi XXXX card? I havent ever used the XXXX Citi XXXX card ; the charge on it was a rebill for the disputed charge ( s ) from the Sears Mastercard. ( This is all unnecessarily confusing, isnt it? Try being on my side of the incident. ) I have sent documents to both XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX and never received a follow-up response. Is the actual fraud unit different from those units? Do your departments communicate with each other? 
XXXX from the fraud section told me that the investigation was reopened now that I had reported it ( again ) and that the dunning calls would stop. The next day, the phone calls for payment ramped up. I called to opt out of getting the dunning calls for this matter, because I had not charged any airline tickets, and because the last listed ticket reference number was the same as the original number from XXXX. That number is XXXX XXXX. 
Whoever I spoke to relevant to opting out of the fraud calls said that if I didnt accept the dunning calls, no one could call me to inform me of the progress of the investigation or the settlement. She added that all communication would be henceforth be done by letter. I said that no one had yet called me with a progress report or answered any of my written questions either in writing or by phone and that I expected that opting out of phone calls would not affect that. She said that CitiBank had no way of calling me without using the automated system that I had just opted out of. (! ) That explanation seems to project the scent of something normally associated with bulls and pastures. Citi XXXX employees havent called over the last three months except to seek payment, never with updates ; how would things change? 
They did change - now Im getting dunning emails and the latest one had this address ( used in the letter heading above ) to write to and thats what this is all about. Past experience indicates that expecting an answer is futile, but I am again attempting to seek a proper resolution of this matter. Instead of CitiBank warning that calls may be recorded, customers should be cautioned to record the calls in order to document getting the run around. '' Mastercard keeps billing me for a fraudulent charge. The person named XXXX mentioned in the letter thought the whole thing was pretty funny as he laughed several times, but he apparently didn't do any follow up to correct the fraudulent charge. 

Mastercard 's customer service is useless to the customer, but benefits the criminal who uses the Mastercard fraudulently.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative: I am requesting claims and defenses under Federal law ( 15 USC 1666i ). This section specifies that the cardholder 's liability is limited to the outstanding balance minus the fraudulent transaction at the time the issuer is notified. I do not consent to any past or future payments being applied for the fraudulent charge. I hereby inform the card issuer and representative investigating the case, that I am withholding payment on the fraudulent charge ( s ). However, to be a loyal consumer I will continue the minimum payments under duress and fear that withheld payments will negatively impact my credit report and reputation. According to California Financial Code Section 2102, 17000 and Federal law principles. I reserve the right to refund. Consumers should be protected from financial loss and fraudulent transactions. The Fair Credit Billing Act safeguards consumers from fraudulent transactions or charges on their credit card accounts. I am requesting a full reversal of the fraudulent transaction XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Amex XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Amex XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX

At the end of XX/XX/XXXX, I contacted Amex again and was informed that, instead of depositing the refund into my checking account as requested, they mailed another check without my consent or any notification. 

To this day, I have not received the check and suspect it was never sent. 

What I Want from Amex : Immediate refund of {$1800.00} via direct deposit to my XXXX XXXX Checking Account. 
Proof of mailing for the check, or confirmation that it was never sent. 
Compensation for financial inconvenience caused by Amexs delays and errors. 
An explanation of why my refund method was changed without my consent. 
Amexs repeated mistakes have disrupted my financial planning, and I need a swift resolution.

narrative:

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I disputed a charge for a XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXr purchased on XX/XX/XXXX with Barclays Credit Card. After noticing that the icemaker was not functioning properly, I contacted XXXX, XXXX and Barclays ( Barclays Case # XXXX ) to report that the product was defective. After several visits to repair the icemaker, it remains defective ( see timeline below ). 

I received a letter from Barclays Billing Disputes Department on XX/XX/XXXX ( letter dated XX/XX/XXXX ). Barclays informed me in that letter that the disputed transaction in the amount of {$1200.00} has been reapplied to my account, stating that the merchant has not violated the terms and conditions of the sales agreement. HOWEVER, THAT WAS NOT THE BASIS OF MY DISPUTE. 

At the latest repair visit on XX/XX/XXXX, the service technician confirmed that neither the original icemaker nor the replacement icemaker was configured for my refrigerator/freezer. He told me that manufacturer practice was to exchange the appliance after 3 repair attempts but that they havent been honoring this practice because it is less expensive to keep sending him out repeatedly to repair. During that visit, the service technician ordered what he believes to be the correct icemaker for my appliance. The icemaker is on back order for several months ( audio of the entire conversation available upon your request ). 

Please see the timeline of events below with documentation attached. 

XX/XX/XXXX : XXXX delivered the XXXX Refrigerator/Freezer. The installation technician told me that I must flush 3 gallons through the water dispenser before the water is potable. In addition, he told me to wait up to 4 days for the XXXX to start fully functioning. There was no time to see if the icemaker was working until XXXX XXXX hour return policy expired. 

XX/XX/XXXX : After finding that the icemaker was not working after 4 days, I contacted the manufacturer for repair. The manufacturer told me that I must contact XXXX for service. XXXX provided me with a phone number for repair. I reached out to that number to schedule a repair. 

XX/XX/XXXX : XXXX sent a service technician to repair Icemaker ( Work Order # XXXX ). The service technician was unable to repair the icemaker and ordered a new Icemaker to install. 

XX/XX/XXXX : I purchased XXXX XXXX XXXX ( Order Number XXXX ). 

XX/XX/XXXX : XXXX informed me that parts for repair were shipped for Work Order # XXXX. 

XX/XX/XXXX : XXXX sent the same service technician but he was unable to repair. 

XX/XX/XXXX : XXXX sent the service technician for repair ( Work Order XXXX ) but he was unable to repair. 

XX/XX/XXXX : I received a letter from Barclays ( dated XX/XX/XXXX ) stating that The merchant has not violated the terms and conditions of the sales agreement, reversing the temporary credit. 

XX/XX/XXXX : In response I sent a fax to Barclays outlining my position with the same documentation as I am providing here. 

XX/XX/XXXX : XXXX sent the service technician to repair. the service technician confirmed that neither the original icemaker nor the replacement icemaker was configured for my refrigerator/freezer. He told me that manufacturer practice was to exchange the appliance after XXXX repair attempts but that they havent been honoring this practice because it is less expensive to keep sending him out repeatedly to repair. During that visit, the service technician ordered what he believes to be the correct icemaker for my appliance. The icemaker is on back order for several months ( audio of the entire conversation available upon your request ). 

XX/XX/XXXX : In a final attempt to discuss Barclays decision, I called Barclays and spoke with a dispute department agent XXXX. She refused to identify herself and hung up after XXXX minutes of discussion and on-hold.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative: In XX/XX/XXXX I received a bill from best buy charged to my best buy card in the amount of {$100.00}. I had no idea what the charge was for and filed a fraudulent charge complaint. The charge was removed from the card ( XXXX ). I called citibank who issues the cards and canceled the card believing the charge to be fraudulent and requested a replacement card. Citibank immediately put the {$100.00} charge onto the new card ( XXXX ). I NEVER received this card and believe it to be stolen as well. I immediately canceled the new card. I found out the charge was for an annual XXXX subscription and called XXXX to cancel the subscription. The XXXX statement shows a XXXX balance due because the detail portion shows a double billing of {$210.00} on the card ( XXXX ) and an " other '' payments or credit of {$210.00}. The next statement which should be XX/XX/XXXX but payment is due XXXX XX/XX/XXXX shows a purchase/other debits of {$100.00}. I received an email from best buy showing they had put the {$100.00} charge on the card ( XXXX ) on XXXX XX/XX/XXXX. This is fraud. The card was canceled in XX/XX/XXXX but best buy is claiming it wasn't canceled until XXXX of XXXX. This statement shows the charge was made on XXXX XX/XX/XXXX. This does not agree with the email I received.The next statement has a closing date of XXXX XX/XX/XXXX and shows a previous balance of {$100.00} and other credits of {$47.00}. The {$47.00} was the refund best buy refused to give me for the unused portion of my total tech which I had canceled. dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
DATE : XX/XX/XXXX ; credit card notified immediately to dispute ; credit card was closed. 

AMOUNT : {$910.00} Below are topline bullets of my case with Cit Security/Disputes team, a dispute spanning XXXX months : - I have never been to XXXX XXXX  - thats an undercover/fake name for an illegal business. 

- I never authorized a charge in the amount of {$910.00}. 

- This is theft - its intent to fraud - and there is evidence of the merchants illegal business operations. 

- Citis early fraud alert, a benefit for safety protection highlighted as part of this credit card, did not happen - no text, email, or call. The {$910.00} amount under the category of fast food should have been flagged immediately, as that is not common purchase behavior for me. 

- The card benefits advertised include coverage from theft, fraud, unauthorized charges, etc, and cases should be looked at carefully as they are all different. There is clear evidence of wrongdoing in mine. 

- There was no regard for my appeal in XXXX, despite my warning to Citi of their dealing with criminals and providing all the proof of the business arrests that took place. 
As of XX/XX/XXXX, Citi is now stating I am responsible for the charge, despite their teams telling me they were working through getting me the permanent credit after their last merchant response ( late XX/XX/XXXX and early XX/XX/XXXX ). 

- There has been misleading information shared by Citi. They shared that they heard from the merchant in XXXX, following my appeal. I later learned ( on XX/XX/XXXX ) that the merchant never responded to my appeal, and Citi still sided with the original fake response from XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX days before 21 merchant owners/managers/staff were arrested for fraud. According to the dispute process, the merchant should respond to my appeal and accusations, and they did not. 

- The case was submitted to the Federal Trade Commission in XXXX- this information was shared with Citi, and I was in touch with the Embassy and local police in XXXX to inquire about a report. Citi stated that a police report doesnt matter and they will not reopen the case. 

ACTION : Unauthorized credit card charge by a merchant who had 21 owners/staff arrested and taken into custody for committing foreign credit card fraud/scams/theft. Based on online research and investigation, the criminal business rotates under four names to try to hide their identity, and the merchant bank is generating documentation Ive never seen before in an attempt to provide false proof of purchase. The receipt is all in XXXX, but with XXXX, the receipt is not itemized with any description of goods - it states SERVICE FEE with one lump amount charged- it also does not include the merchant name, address, phone number/site, and it does not include my name or last four digits of the card used. 

XXXX months after this dispute was opened, the criminal merchants are getting favored vs. a XXXX year loyal customer because Citi Mastercard will not validate or further investigate/consider any of the criminal evidence and will only trust the illegal operation of this business. 

Per Citi Mastercard 's recommendation, when I immediately reported the charge, they suggested opening the dispute as security fraud in late XX/XX/XXXX- the credit card was shut down. Then weeks later, the credit card company billed me again for {$910.00} and stated it needed to be a billing dispute. It moved to a different team and the dispute process restarted. 

The merchant did not respond till the end of XXXX ( two days before their arrests on XX/XX/XXXX ) - they fabricated a receipt in their response. In my early XXXX appeal, with evidence of the arrests, criminal activity, and proof from other customers from all over the world experiencing the same situations by the same merchant, it went into another round of review to the merchant, according to Citi. 

During this time, I learned that despite the letters issued to me from Citi with updates claiming they are working through this with the " merchant and/or merchant bank '', the reality is they don't check with the merchant at all- they only work through the bank who is following the lead of the criminal owners. If they started with the merchant and reviewed the high level of detail I provided about this business, it would be clear that there are criminals behind the operation. Instead, there was no customer courtesy to investigate deeper - or suggest other steps to take - to help my case and clear this charge, but none of that was taken into consideration. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, according to Citi, the merchant responded to my appeal. No proof of their response was provided to me- only a letter that stated they heard back from the merchant and they'd continue the investigation. I then responded to Citi with even more evidence of the criminal activity I found online to help with my case. 

In late XXXX and early XXXX, I placed two calls to Citi to check the status - both calls were good news from the representatives. They stated that they heard from the merchant and that they just need a few days to close the case to issue the " permanent credit... and you have been a customer for over 20 years... not to worry '' - so, we had come to a good conclusion. Both reps reiterated that the permanent credit was in the works and just to give it another couple of weeks for the letter. 

The letter came XX/XX/XXXX - two months since they had allegedly heard from the merchant - stating I was responsible for this charge. I couldnt believe this was the outcome, despite all my proof and efforts. 

After some calls with Citi, refusing to reopen the case or consider my evidence, I called a Disputes representative again on XX/XX/XXXX to make sure I was tracking all of this information correctly before sending a long letter to the Executive Response Unit ( and for purposes of this report to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ). In that phone call, the representative confirmed they had only heard from the merchant ONCE- on XX/XX/XXXX. They never responded to my appeal where I accused them of criminal activity with PROOF of their arrests. It is unclear why Citi was deceitful to me stating they had heard from the merchant following my appeal- I asked why I was led to believe the merchant responded, but the representation could not answer the question. 

Here is what Citi did not do : - Credit card transaction was processed with no fraud safety alert text/email sent to verify the purchase under the category of " fast food restaurant '' for {$910.00}. I asked Citi Customer Service why this didnt happen, and no answer could be given as to why the fraud alert was not triggered. Never would I spend that amount at any business, and that should have been a red flag on Citibank to alert me based on my consumer purchasing behavior. In the past, I have received these alerts, but Citi did not this time, and they are aware they didnt. When you're advertising this benefit for credit card holders and your marketing includes this type of protection, it is unreasonable to make the consumer responsible for something that could have been avoided with their advertised security measures- this is something that Cit was sued for in XXXX for deceitful marketing, but they dont own up to their own faults in the process. 

- Given the larger criminal circumstances of this case, policies need to be reviewed that allow for the customer 's case to be further investigated in detail. Customer service was not the priority and my detailed appeal with substantial evidence of criminal activity was disregarded by ALL teams I spoke with - Security team, Disputes team- and Im now pleading with the Executive Response Unit to look at it closely. 

- Not one phone call or email acknowledgment of my communication of this case was done. No one reached out to ask questions. I also filed a complaint with the FTC, emailed the report number to Citi. No response. I alerted them that I was in touch with the Embassy in XXXX and with the local police in XXXX to try to provide any further documentation if helpful for my case- no response. There was zero guidance and zero efforts from the Citi Mastercard group to give the custom some reassurance. I called them multiple times each month to check in so that I could provide them with whatever was needed to help me, and I just kept being told to be patient while the process was in motion, but never did I speak or get a call from their disputes investigators for such a complicated case like this one.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
REBUTTAL TO CITI RESPONSE : FORMAL ASSERTION OF CLAIMS AND DEFENSES Case # XXXX I am formally rebutting Citis denial dated XX/XX/year>. Citibank is incorrectly attempting to adjudicate this matter under 12 CFR 1026.13 ( Billing Errors ) and internal XXXX timeframes. I am not reporting a billing error ; I am formally asserting my rights under 12 CFR 1026.12 ( c ) ( Claims and Defenses ). 

Under federal law, a consumers right to assert claims and defenses against a card issuer for defective or misrepresented goods is not subject to the 60-day billing error window or XXXX  120-day rule. This right exists for up to one year from the date of the first statement, provided the purchase exceeds {$50.00} and occurred in the consumer 's home state. 

1. Fact : Material Misrepresentation & Breach of Warranty Jackerys marketing ( Exhibit B ) and technical manuals ( Exhibit C ) explicitly promise " XXXX XXXX '' ( retention of 95 % charge for 1 year ) and a " 30-day backup '' capability. 

However, in the attached correspondence ( Exhibit A ), XXXX XXXX XXXX admits the unit has a " normal '' idle drain of XXXX per hour. 

The Mathematical Proof of Fraud : A XXXX hourly drain equals XXXX per day. On a XXXX unit, the battery is depleted in XXXX days while doing nothing. 

The Violation : This represents a 9,000 % deviation from the XXXX standby capability advertised. This XXXX " normal '' drain is documented in writing by the merchant but is undisclosed on their website and in their manual. 

XXXX. Justification for Total Recovery of {$12000.00} Citi is legally obligated to make the consumer whole. This total includes the {$2000.00} installation fee. Because the hardware is fundamentally defective and incapable of performing the " whole-home backup '' function as marketed, the professional installation is valueless. I have relied on the merchants marketing and their recommended installers to my financial detriment. 

3. Final Assertion I made a good-faith effort to resolve this with XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX following the XX/XX/XXXX professional installation. The merchants refusal to honor a refund for a product that fails to meet its own basic technical specifications is the exact scenario 12 CFR 1026.12 ( c ) was enacted to prevent. 

I expect Citibank to comply with federal regulations and immediately reverse the charges for this defective equipment and the associated installation costs.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am submitting this complaint regarding Citibanks handling of a billing dispute for a {$600.00} credit card charge dated XXXX XXXX, 2025. 

I disputed the charge on the basis that the services provided did not match what was represented and resulted in harm. As part of my dispute, I submitted color photographic evidence documenting the condition at issue. 

On or about XXXX XXXX, 2026, I received a denial letter stating that the documentation provided did not support my billing error claim. After receiving this denial, I contacted Citibank to confirm whether my submitted photos had been reviewed. During that call, I was informed that although I had submitted the images in color, they were appearing in black-and-white within Citibanks system. I was not notified of this technical issue prior to the initial denial. 

I was advised to resubmit the photographs in PDF format, which I did promptly. The dispute was re-reviewed, and on XXXX XXXX, 2026, I received a second denial letter stating that I received the services for which I contracted and that Citibank had exhausted all dispute resolution options. 

My concern is not only the outcome, but whether a reasonable and thorough investigation was conducted. The initial review appears to have been made without properly viewing the full photographic evidence due to a technical formatting issue. Additionally, the denial letters do not explain what documentation the merchant provided, what specific standard was applied, or how my submitted evidence was evaluated in reaching the conclusion.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am filing this complaint regarding the handling of multiple fraud and billing dispute claims on my Citi card account. Over the past several months, my account has experienced significant issues involving unauthorized activity, incorrect charges, and merchants continuing to process transactions after my account had already been deactivated. 
Despite repeatedly providing documentation, receipts, account records, and written proof supporting my claims, I have not received consistent or accurate assistance from Citi. I have spoken with numerous representatives across both the fraud department and billing disputes department, and I have repeatedly been given conflicting information regarding how these claims should be handled. 
At various times, Citi representatives instructed me to proceed through fraud because the charges involved incorrect amounts or transactions processed after account deactivation. Other representatives later stated the matters should instead be handled as billing disputes. I also contacted Citi XXXX, which appeared to focus on only one merchant while many other unresolved transactions remained outstanding. 
This has resulted in an ongoing cycle of contradictory guidance, repeated submissions of the same documentation, and significant delays in resolving my claims. I was repeatedly assured that actions were being taken on my account ; however, on XX/XX/year>, I learned that certain actions I had been told were completed had not actually been carried out. 
In addition, I continue receiving dispute determination letters that directly contradict both the evidence provided and prior communications from Citi representatives. Some letters state that merchants would not issue refunds, despite the fact that several disputes involve charges that were either incorrect in amount and/or processed after my account had already been deactivated. In other situations, merchants had already acknowledged issues related to the transactions. 
I have spent countless hours attempting to resolve these matters through Citi directly, including repeated phone calls, resubmissions of documentation, and communications with multiple departments. I have also experienced calls being disconnected while attempting to obtain assistance. 
For reference, I have retained supporting documentation, receipts, emails, account records, and recordings of recent phone conversations reflecting the handling of my case and the representations made to me by Citi representatives. 
I am requesting that Citi conduct a complete and thorough review of my account, including all prior communications, notes, submitted evidence, and dispute history. I am also requesting immediate corrective action regarding the mishandling, delays, contradictory determinations, and failure to properly investigate these claims. 
I am concerned that the delays, inconsistent information, and improper handling of these matters may negatively impact my rights and the timelines associated with billing dispute and fraud investigations under applicable consumer protection regulations and card network rules. 
I respectfully request prompt assistance and resolution regarding these ongoing issues

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
TO : Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) DATE : XX/XX/XXXX COMPANY : American Express ISSUE : Predatory Debt Resurrection, Retaliation, and Negligent Investigation I. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY I am filing this complaint to document a pattern of Unfair, Deceptive, and Abusive Acts or Practices ( UDAAP ) by American Express. Following my initial regulatory inquiries, the bank engaged in Actual Retaliation by re-categorizing a {$78000.00} construction purchase as a " Cash Advance '' to strip me of Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) protections. Furthermore, the bank has conducted a " sham '' investigation, ignoring conclusive evidence of criminal misdemeanors, manufacturer warranty exclusions, and the existence of signed legal releases that nullify the merchant 's claims. 

II. THE RETALIATION TIMELINE & " DEBT RESURRECTION '' XX/XX/XXXX : I filed a formal notice with the CFPB regarding merchant fraud. 

XX/XX/XXXX : Within XXXX hours, Amex " resurrected '' a previously settled debt of {$76000.00} and applied the predatory " XXXX XXXX '' ( XXXX XXXX ) label. 

The Motive : By re-labeling a " Purchase '' as " Cash, '' Amex is attempting to evade its statutory liability for merchant fraud under FCBA Section 170. This action was taken specifically to punish me for my regulatory filing. 

XXXX. MATHEMATICAL PROOF OF DECEPTIVE ACCOUNTING ( THE " SHAVE-OFF '' ) A forensic audit of my account ( performed by a household member with a corporate VP/Auditor background ) reveals that Amex " shaved off XXXX {$2300.00} from my total settlement payment of {$78000.00}. 

The Trap : Amex diverted my payment to cover minor, non-disputed purchases first. This was a calculated maneuver to leave the $ XXXX GVD debt isolated on the ledger, allowing them to trigger the retaliatory " Cash Advance '' re-categorization and high interest penalties.

IV. THE " KNOWLEDGE LINK '' & NOTICE FRAUD ( EXHIBIT T ) On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a physical packet from Amex at my home in XXXXXXXX XXXX 

The Proof : This packet contained a " Proof of Service '' showing the merchants lawyer, XXXX XXXX, was mailing legal notices to a vacant house in XXXXXXXX XXXX. 

The Fraud : This proves the merchant possessed my Texas address and was communicating with me there, yet intentionally served an empty house to prevent me from receiving legal notice. Amex possessed these conflicting documents and failed to flag this as a " Notice Fraud '' violation. 

V. THE " WAIVER PARADOX '' & TITLE OBSTRUCTION EVIDENCE On XX/XX/XXXX, I uploaded evidence that Amex explicitly ignored. Most notably : Unconditional Waiver and Release ( Civil Code 8134 ) : I provided XXXX ( XXXX ) signed Unconditional Waivers where XXXX 's CEO, XXXX XXXX, legally released all claims to {$140000.00} of the project funds. Crucially, the merchant filed their lawsuit on XX/XX/XXXX, yet signed these Unconditional Waivers on XX/XX/XXXX. This means that even after initiating litigation for these funds, the merchant legally released their claim to them. 

XXXX XXXX Waivers ( Evidence of Title Hostage ) : I have obtained waivers proving the supplies were paid for in full. Crucially, I have written proof that merchant XXXX XXXX instructed the supplier NOT to share these waivers with the owners. This intentional obstruction of property title is a tool of coercion and constitutes Financial Elder Abuse. 

Manufacturer Determination ( XXXX XXXX # XXXX ) : The manufacturer verified that the installation failed to meet standards ( " Reverse Lap '' and " Z-flashing '' breaches ) and is excluded from warranty. 

" XXXX '' XXXX XXXX : XXXX claims " XXXX years in business '' on the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX In my XX/XX/XXXX evidence upload, I notified Amex that the manufacturer was placed on " Actual Notice '' of this fraud as early as XX/XX/XXXX. Furthermore, I am providing this Bureau with copies of formal notices sent to XXXX XXXX 's General Counsel ( XXXX XXXX ) on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX. Despite these notices, the manufacturer has maintained a " Calculated Silence '' for over XXXX days, allowing the merchant to continue using falsified credentials. 

VI. INTEREST RATE FRAUD ( XXXX % vs. XXXX % ) & XXXX XXXX American Express ignored evidence that the merchant 's claim is mathematically and legally fraudulent : Interest Fraud : The merchant 's own contract ( p. XXXX ) caps interest at XXXX %. The lawsuit papers accepted by Amex claim XXXX %. This is a blatant, documented overcharge. 

XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX & XXXX XXXX, XXXX & XXXX ) : I provided proof that the contract was executed by an unregistered salesperson ( XXXX XXXX ). Under California law, a contract signed by an unregistered individual is void. Furthermore, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ( b ) mandates the total disgorgement ( refund ) of all compensation paid to a contractor operating in violation of these licensing laws. 

XXXX. FINANCIAL ELDER ABUSE ( CA WELF. & INST. CODE XXXX ) The merchant has engaged in Title Obstruction ( via the XXXX waiver instruction ), predatory interest overstatements, and coercion to target a senior homeowner. By providing a " XXXX XXXX '' platform to collect this illegal debt, Amex is facilitating Financial Elder Abuse. 

XXXX. XXXX XXXX American Express accepted a statutorily premature " Claim of XXXX '' as its sole justification for denying a dispute while demonstrably ignoring conclusive technical evidence, manufacturer determinations, and the mandatory disgorgement requirements of XXXX & XXXX XXXX. Crucially, the bank ignored that this project no longer constitutes a " XXXX XXXX '' but rather " Property Damage '' as evidenced by the documented leaks, forensic moisture readings ( XXXX  % ), and the manufacturer 's warranty exclusion. I request that the CFPB audit the retaliatory XX/XX/XXXX re-categorization and hold American Express accountable for its willful failure to distinguish between a legitimate consumer debt and a merchants attempt to collect for the documented destruction of a primary residence. 
Sincerely, XXXX XXXX XXXX

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I had recently moved to XXXX, XXXX XXXX and work at the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. On XX/XX/year>, after having dinner with my family at XXXX XXXX in XXXX, we were walking out of the restaurant and a man approached us in a suit and name tag. He said he worked for XXXX XXXX and asked if I had signed up for my 20 % off coupon. I said I wasnt interested but he was being persistent and said it would only take a few minutes. He led me to the XXXX XXXX XXXX row of ATMs and said I could sign up on the ATM, and I just needed to use my bank card ( in XXXX XXXX many different types of transactions are done through the ATM so this did not seem unusual to me ). 

I inserted my XXXX XXXX  XXXX debit card and logged into the ATM. Another man approached and was trying to distract me. I started to get the sense that something was not right and I tried to retrieve my ATM card but it was stuck in the machine. I took out my citibank credit card and tried to dislodge my ATM card with it but it did not work ( I did not insert it fully into the ATM machine ). The second man walked away and the first man was pretending to assist me. I told him that I worked for the XXXX XXXX and was calling the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX to assist he then fled. 

Knowing that my bank card was stuck in the machine, I immediately deactivated it through the app. Subsequently, a number of charges were blocked totaling close to {$7000.00}. Unfortunately, I did not cancel my citibank card because since it didnt go into the ATM machine, I didnt think it was compromised. I only realized it was compromised after I got an alert from Citibank for potential fraud ( charge for {$2500.00} for XXXX XXXX ). Unfortunately, their first transaction of {$1700.00} for XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX went through. This does not appear to be a legitimate company and I did not authorize the transaction. I disputed this claim through the fraud department and was told since I was still in possession of the card it was not fraud? Clearly this is a skimming scam and I did not authorize the purchase. Had Citibank performed even a cursory review, they would have realized this was fraud. 

Based on the advice of a very helpful Citibank customer service rep, I then tried to dispute the claim with Citibank as a charge dispute ( instead of fraud which it is ), and then denied the dispute again on XX/XX/year> - stating That once the credit card number is given to merchant and a transaction has taken place, the merchant terms and conditions apply.

Again, this was FRAUD and was subject to a skimming scam from a reputable ATM machine. I never gave my credit card information up to engage in a transaction with XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX

I then filed a dispute with Barclays. The dispute ( XXXX XXXX. XXXX ) was denied on the basis that the ticket was non-refundable. 
This reason is not applicable. The flight was cancelled by the airline, and the service was not provided. dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
My XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX was charged several hundred dollars for a transaction I did not authorize. I submitted a dispute with Barclays Disputes Department, but they determined that the transaction was valid, claiming that I still had possession of my card when the transaction occured. Based on this, they closed the case without further investigation. I strongly believe their determination was incorrect and failed to meet the investigative standards required under U.S. consumer protection laws. I formally requested Barclays to reopen the dispute. 

Why Their Investigation Was Flawed : Failure to Provide Adequate Evidence Barclays claimed the transaction was valid because my card was in my possession. This reasoning is insufficient, as fraudulent transactions can still occur even under these circumstances. A thorough investigation should have gone beyond this basic assumption. Their approach failed to meet the standards set by the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ), which requires credit card issuers to conduct a comprehensive review of disputed charges.

Violation of Consumer Rights Under the FCBA The FCBA ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ) and Regulation Z ( 12 C.F.R. 1026.13 ) provide consumers the right to dispute unauthorized or incorrect transactions. Barclays was obligated to conduct a thorough investigation and consider all relevant evidence. From their response, it was evident they failed to meet these requirements, as critical details about the transaction were ignored.

Failure to Provide Supporting Evidence Barclays is required under the Electronic Fund Transfer Act ( EFTA ) and Regulation E to provide evidence supporting the validity of the transaction. I specifically requested the following, which they ignored : A copy of the transaction receipt, including a description of the services or products provided.

Documentation showing my authenticated signature authorizing the transaction.

Logs and records detailing the transaction 's origin, including IP addresses, geolocation, and device information.

Without this evidence, their conclusion lacked credibility.

Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) Guidelines The CFPB mandates that financial institutions protect consumer rights and conduct proper investigations into disputes. Barclays superficial review and failure to address my specific concerns violated these guidelines.

Repeated Inadequate Responses Despite my formal request to reopen the dispute and address my detailed concerns, Barclays ignored my points and repeatedly sent the same response. This dismissive approach was unacceptable and highlighted their failure to fulfill their obligations. By refusing to investigate thoroughly, they effectively supported and enabled fraudulent activity.

In Summary : Barclays handling of my dispute failed to meet the standards required under U.S. consumer protection laws. I urged them to reopen the case, conduct a thorough investigation, and provide the evidence I requested. However, they ignored my request, showing a disregard for their responsibilities and my consumer rights.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
On XX/XX/year>, I purchased a set of appliancesincluding a XXXX  XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX using my Citi credit card ( ending in XXXX ). The full charge of {$19000.00} was posted to my account under XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX 

The cooktop was delivered on XX/XX/year>, and during installation, a subcontracted gas technician sent by the merchant dropped a metal object on the unit, leaving a visible scratch on the stainless steel face. I contacted the merchant the same day to report the damage. 

Despite multiple follow-ups, the merchant refused to take responsibility, stating only that the product was delivered as agreed. No offer of repair, replacement, or refund was made. I then filed a formal dispute with Citi. 

Citi initially issued a conditional credit, but later reversed it after receiving what they described as a merchant response. I asked Citi on multiple occasions to provide a copy of that response or at least describe the supporting documentation provided by the merchant. I received no documentationonly the blanket statement that services were rendered as described. 

That phrase is meaningless in this context. The dispute was not about whether the product was delivered, but about the condition of the product at delivery, which was damaged by the merchants own installer. The merchant never contested the damage, never responded to my documentation, and offered no inspection or follow-up. Yet Citi treated this vague denial as sufficient grounds to close the dispute in the merchants favor. 

Whats even more troubling is that when I submitted surveillance video clips and photos of the damage, Citi refused to accept them, stating that their system could not process the file types ( XXXX and XXXX ). I asked how to submit acceptable evidence, and no alternative was provided. Citi made a decision without reviewing the central visual evidence, then provided no explanation as to what documentation they relied on from the merchant to justify their decision. 

This is not only unreasonableit undermines the very foundation of the dispute process. I have : A detailed timeline of events. 

A purchase confirmation email showing the cooktops price and brand. 

Video and photo evidence showing the technician and the moment he bent to retrieve a dropped tool. 

A statement from a technician confirming the units front panel can not be repaired and would require full replacement. 

Meanwhile, the only documentation Citi appears to have relied on is a one-line claim by the merchant that the services were rendered. Thats not evidence. Thats a generic statement used to deny responsibility. 

I am asking the CFPB to review how Citi handled this dispute, specifically : Why they closed the case without reviewing the cardholders evidence ; What specific documentation was submitted by the merchant and used to support the denial ; And why Citi is allowed to resolve disputes in favor of merchants without transparency, while refusing to accept or even view clear documentation from their own customer. 

This is not how fair resolution should work. I have fulfilled every requirement and acted in good faith. I am asking that the dispute be reopened, the decision reversed, or Citi be held accountable for failing to apply its purchase protection policies appropriately.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am filing this complaint against Barclays for failing to properly investigate and process my credit card disputes with case no ( XXXX ) XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX involving purchases from XXXX XXXX / XXXX. 

The disputed transactions were for in-game digital currency called XXXX XXXX. XXXX XXXX are used inside the X-Clash game to upgrade castles and heroes. The merchant advertises the game as a competitive XXXX XXXX environment. My dispute is not simply that the XXXX XXXX failed to appear in an account ledger. My dispute is that the virtual goods were sold for use in a materially misrepresented service environment. 

The merchant marketed and sold participation in a fair, competitive PvP game. My evidence shows that the environment was not fair, organic, or as advertised. It was allegedly influenced by undisclosed automated bots, artificial engagement, and paid actors used to create artificial competition and spending pressure. I provided screenshots and logs supporting this claim, including statements from supposed players asking whether they were being paid by developers to hype up the server before later abandoning it. 

This materially changed the value and utility of the XXXX XXXX. XXXX Diamonds in a legitimate PvP environment are not the same product or service as XXXX XXXX used in an artificially manipulated game environment. The merchants anticipated defense that the XXXX XXXX were technically credited ignores the actual dispute : the goods and services were not as described and were sold for use in a misrepresented service ecosystem. 

In addition, the merchants own Terms of XXXX prohibit bots and unauthorized methods in the name of fair play, while the merchant failed to disclose its own alleged use of artificial engagement, bots, or paid actors. The merchants XXXX XXXX XXXX also acknowledges abnormal mechanics and unauthorized methods, supporting my position that the service environment was compromised. 

As of XX/XX/year>, the merchant revoked my account access entirely while retaining my funds. Even if the merchant claims the XXXX XXXX were initially delivered, the merchant later removed my ability to access or use the account and purchased goods. This creates a separate basis for dispute : service not rendered / ongoing service interrupted. 

Barclays XXXX Barclays appears to have mishandled the dispute by focusing only on whether the digital currency was technically credited, rather than investigating whether the goods and services were delivered as agreed or as described. 

Under Regulation Z, billing-error protections cover disputes involving goods or services that were not accepted or not delivered as agreed, including delivery of services different from what was agreed. A consumer also is not required to first resolve the dispute with the merchant before giving a billing-error notice to the creditor under XXXX ( a ) ( XXXX ). 

Barclays was required to conduct a reasonable investigation. Regulation Z states that if a creditor determines no billing error occurred, it must conduct a reasonable investigation and explain its reasons ; for nondelivery of property or services, the creditor may not deny the claim unless it determines the property or services were actually delivered, mailed, or sent as agreed. 

Barclays approach appears inconsistent with Mastercards dispute framework as well. XXXX XXXX XXXX recognizes disputes where goods or services were either not as described or defective, including where goods and services did not conform to their description or where the merchant did not honor terms and conditions. XXXX also identifies digital goods as goods stored, delivered, and used electronically, including games, game pieces, and software. 

The dispute should therefore have been evaluated under the proper framework for Goods or Services Not as Described, Defective/Not as Described, and/or Goods or Services Not Provided / interruption of ongoing service, rather than reduced to the narrow question of whether Red Diamonds appeared in the account. 

This issue is further supported by the fact that another major card issuer, XXXX XXXX, reviewed materially similar evidence involving the same merchant and resolved the dispute in my favor. I understand that XXXX Ones decision is not binding on Barclays, but it demonstrates that my claim is bona fide, supported by evidence, and capable of being processed under card-network dispute rules. Barclays should not dismiss the claim using a superficial digital currency was delivered theory while ignoring the misrepresented service environment and subsequent account revocation. 

Consumer protections involved I believe Barclays failed to comply with the spirit and requirements of the following protections : XXXX. Regulation Z / Fair Credit Billing Act billing-error procedures under XXXX C.F.R. XXXX, because Barclays did not reasonably investigate whether the purchased goods and services were delivered as agreed. 
XXXX. XXXX XXXX. XXXX ( c ), which allows a cardholder to assert claims and defenses against the card issuer when the merchant fails to resolve a dispute involving property or services purchased with a credit card. 
XXXX. XXXX dispute workflows, including Mastercards framework for goods or services not as described, defective, not provided, and digital goods disputes. 
XXXX. UDAAP concerns, because Barclays handling of the dispute appears unfair and potentially deceptive if it accepted my dispute evidence but then evaluated the claim under the wrong theory, effectively denying consumer dispute rights by ignoring the substance of the merchant misrepresentation. 

Requested resolution I request that Barclays : XXXX. Reopen and reprocess all disputed transactions involving XXXX XXXX / XXXX. 
XXXX. Evaluate the disputes under the proper chargeback basis : Goods or Services Not as Described, Defective/Not as Described, and/or Goods or Services Not Provided / service interruption, rather than merely asking whether XXXX XXXX were credited XXXX 
XXXX. Permanently credit all disputed amounts, including any related interest, fees, or charges. 
XXXX. Provide a written explanation identifying the exact XXXX dispute reason code or workflow Barclays used. 
XXXX. Provide copies of any merchant representment evidence Barclays relied upon. 
XXXX. Confirm whether Barclays submitted the disputes through XXXX and XXXX Barclays pursued XXXX or arbitration if the merchant/acquirer rejected the chargeback. 
XXXX. Remove or suppress any negative credit reporting, delinquency, fees, interest, or adverse account treatment connected to the disputed transactions. 
XXXX. Confirm that Barclays has preserved and reviewed all attached evidence, including Capital Ones favorable outcome, merchant PvP advertising, account revocation evidence, merchant statements, player/bot logs, and the merchants own Terms of Service and Fair Operation Statement. 

Closing statement This is not a case where I simply received digital currency and later changed my mind. The XXXX XXXX  were purchased for use in a PvP game environment that was materially misrepresented. The merchant advertised a fair competitive environment, allegedly used undisclosed artificial engagement and paid actors, and later revoked access to the account and purchased goods while retaining my funds. 

Barclays should not allow the merchant to avoid chargeback liability by claiming that virtual currency was technically credited while ignoring the misrepresented service environment and total loss of access. I respectfully request that the CFPB require Barclays to conduct a proper investigation and provide a full written response.

The young man said not to remove the substance for XXXX hours, so I was not able to see the work that was done. ( Later-on, when I removed the foam or substance, the scratches were still there indicating no work had been performed ) If had tried to pay the young man with XXXX credit cards, expecting a charge of {$280.00}. When I got home, I checked my email. There was no invoice or receipt or business card. Instead, there were multiple fraud alerts from XXXX and XXXX. Some were in the amount of {$1000.00} and XXXX was {$2000.00}. My CITI Custom cash had a charge of {$1000.00}, which was an amount I did not authorize and would not have paid to someone who randomly came up to me in a parking lot. Since the scratches were still there, I later went to a professional shop and was told, it would take XXXX days to complete and would require {$510.00} for parts, {$680.00} for body labor for XXXX hours, {$410.00} for paint labor for XXXX hours, and paint supplies for {$270.00} for XXXX hours, and with sales tax of {$56.00}, which came to a total of {$1900.00}. I immediately contacted my credit card bank, Citi Bank, and they said that a charge of {$9.00} had been made to my account and then immediately afterward a charge of {$140.00} was made. It is now XX/XX/XXXX, I have spoken with XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX, all from the Security Dept at Citi card Bank. I call every week and get the same runaround. This was done by someone who is scamming people and obviously getting away with it. I just want my account credited with the {$140.00} that is due to me.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Citi Bank : Complaint Against : Citibank ( Credit Card Services ) Issue : Incorrect denial of unauthorized transaction dispute I am filing this complaint regarding Citibanks refusal to properly handle and reimburse an unauthorized credit card charge. 

Charges of approximately {$600.00} from ClassPass were made on my credit card without my authorization. The transaction was initiated by a minor who used my card without my knowledge or permission. I did not consent to the creation of any account, enrollment in any trial, or authorization of any charges. 

Upon discovering the charge : The account was immediately cancelled No services, classes, or benefits were used I promptly notified Citibank and disputed the charge Citibank denied my dispute. However, this is not a billing disagreement or dissatisfaction with services. This is a clear case of repeat unauthorized transactions. 

Under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ), I am not liable for unauthorized charges. A minor does not have legal authority to bind me to a contract or authorize use of my credit card. Additionally, I received no benefit from the transaction. 

Citibank appears to have misclassified my dispute and failed to conduct a reasonable investigation into the unauthorized nature of the charge. 

I am requesting : Immediate reversal of all charges from Classpass Correction of the dispute classification to unauthorized transaction Written confirmation of the outcome If necessary, I am prepared to provide documentation supporting my claim, including proof of cancellation and lack of usage.

I request that Citibank comply with its obligations under federal law and resolve this matter promptly.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am submitting this complaint regarding American Expresss handling of multiple credit card disputes involving merchandise that I did not receive.

I made two separate purchases with XXXX using my American Express card. In both instances, the merchandise was never delivered to me, and I did not receive the goods or any benefit from the transactions. I timely disputed both charges as goods not received.

Disputed Charge # 1 Merchant : XXXX XXXX Transaction Date : XX/XX/XXXX Amount : {$290.00} Merchant Address : XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX Cardmember : XXXX XXXX Dispute Reference Number : XXXX Dispute Received : XX/XX/XXXX Resolution Date : XX/XX/XXXX Status : Closed ( Denied ) Disputed Charge # 2 Merchant : XXXX XXXX XXXX Date : XX/XX/XXXX Amount : {$330.00} Cardmember : XXXX XXXX Dispute Reference Number : XXXX Dispute Received : XX/XX/XXXX Resolution Date : XX/XX/XXXX Status : Closed ( Denied ) American Express initially issued provisional credits for these disputes but later reversed them after accepting documentation from the merchant that does not substantiate delivery. The merchant provided only carrier tracking information a copy of my purchase and a XXXX  XXXX  photo. That photo does not show my residence, does not display my address, does not show the package in my possession or that of any authorized recipient, and contains no identifying information tying the package to my orders. The image could depict any package belonging to any person. Tracking data alone does not prove receipt.

Despite the absence of proof of delivery, American Express has denied these disputes repeatedly. I have reopened the disputes four separate times, and this complaint follows the most recent denial. At no point has the merchant provided evidence demonstrating that the merchandise was delivered to me.

Because I did not receive the merchandise, the merchant did not fulfill the transactions. Charging me under these circumstances forces me to pay for goods I never received and improperly shifts delivery risk to the consumer without proof. I believe American Express failed to properly apply its dispute resolution standards and buyer protection obligations.

I am requesting that the CFPB review American Expresss handling of these disputes and require American Express to : Correct my account by issuing
permanent credits for the disputed charges, and Ensure compliance with credit card dispute obligations and cardholder protection standards.

I have retained all supporting documentation, including dispute correspondence, carrier delivery images, and account statements, and can provide them upon request.

Thank you for your review of this matter.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I have an XXXX XXXX credit card through American Express which I opened in XXXX. I quickly stopped using this payment method due to high interest rates and at this point its been years since I made a purchase intentionally on this card. 

On XX/XX/XXXX I was charged for XXXX purchases on XXXX which I did not authorize. I made those purchases with a different payment method, but when that payment method failed to process XXXX apparently took the liberty of creating and charging a backup payment method on my behalf without my knowledge or consent. I was never notified that another payment method would be charged. I never authorized a backup payment method, and I reached out to XXXX as soon as I noticed those charges to have them reversed and remove that payment method. I was explicitly told that those charges would be reversed by XXXX at that time and billed to another payment method and that my American Express card would not be charged again. I was told they would take care of the whole thing and that they were sorry for the inconvenience. 

Fast forward to today, and I get a call from American Express claiming I still owe them $ XXXX. The initial purchases that were charged to that account totaled about {$60.00}. I refuse to pay those fees under any circumstances, respectfully. Theyve been charging me late fees for months on transactions I was told would be reversed months ago. If you look at the whole of my credit history youd see a perfect on time payment history. Literally 100 % on every card except this one And the reason why that is appears to be an intentional effort on XXXX behalf to charge these American Express XXXX XXXX cards. I guess one or both of them receive some kind of financial gain from those transactions. Every time I make a purchase on XXXX I have to go into my list of payment methods and change the payment method to my debit card despite my debit card being my primary payment method. Their system automatically tried to charge those cards for some reason even when you explicitly tell it not to. Ive had to cancel several orders over the years for the same reason. The only difference in this case is that I did not notice the transaction right away and was not in time to cancel it when I did. I no longer have access to the email account I signed up for the card with. Its an old email I do not use. 

Funny thing is, when I called American Express to dispute the charges the representative I spoke to knew about this issue already and told me theyve had lots of people calling about XXXX charging their XXXX business card without their knowledge. American Express is fully aware of this issue and so is XXXX, yet they both continue to do it anyway. American Express benefits from cases like this because most people will just pay the fraudulent charges to avoid hurting their credit score I guess. I am not sure, however, what XXXX has to gain. But you have to assume theres some kind of deal going on between them, otherwise their logo wouldnt be on my Amex card. 

I was told there is nothing either of them can do because XXXX  days has now elapsed and they are unable to dispute the transaction. Which is XXXX, because I already disputed those transactions months ago with XXXX and was told XXXX would take care of it. I was lied to and mislead into waiting until the window for a dispute had closed. 

I think any rational, decent human being with the power to reverse these charges would do so in a heartbeat. I am not asking for free stuff. Charge my debit card for the items, I dont care. I am asking for late fees and interest to be removed because I did not authorize those transactions and my account history clearly shows that this is not an account I ever intentionally use. 

I asked to speak to a supervisor with Amex hoping they would hear the details of my case and realize it was wrong to charge those late fees. He was a bit rude and didnt do anything for me except tell me I was XXXX basically because it had been more than 60 days. 

Tell me, if someone steals your grandmas credit card and buys XXXX dollars worth of stuff, but she doesnt notice until XXXX  days after the last transaction, would you tell her, Oh well, sorry maam, looks like you owe us {$60000.00} plus interest!. Really??? 

I got ta believe American Express is a better company than this.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative: I am filing a complaint regarding another billing dispute involving Citibank and XXXX for merchandise I did not receive, totaling {$820.00}. This dispute involves an order from XXXX XXXX The merchant claims the package was delivered ; however, the only evidence provided is a photograph showing the package left unattended in the lobby of my apartment building, which is a shared, unsecured public space. There was no signature confirmation, no hand delivery, and no proof that I personally received or took possession of the merchandise. I never received this order. Citibank initially issued a provisional credit while investigating. After XXXX submitted the lobby delivery photo, Citibank reversed the credit and held me responsible for the charge, treating delivery to a building lobby as equivalent to delivery to the cardholder. I dispute this conclusion. Delivery to a shared lobby without signature does not establish receipt by the consumer. The merchant selected an unsecured delivery method for high-value merchandise and assumed the risk of loss prior to receipt. I did not authorize delivery to a public lobby and did not receive the merchandise. Citibank advised me to contact the merchant directly. XXXX has refused to refund or replace the order, leaving me responsible for merchandise I never received. This appears to be a repeated failure to properly assess non-receipt disputes where no proof of receipt exists.
