dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Below are the details of a purchase I made using my American Express card in XX/XX/year> for compounded medication sold by a telehealth company called SlimDownRx. The pharmacy they used to fill the order is called XXXX XXXX, based in XXXX XXXX, FL. 

This medication was compounded illegally, as per the Dept of Health in the state of FL. The pharmacy does not have a sterile compounding license, only a pharmacy license. Therefore the medication they sold me is counterfeit and can not be used, again per the state of Florida. American Express has failed to protect my rights as a consumer. I filed a dispute and a second dispute. Despite submitting numerous documents showing that I was sold goods that were not as described, American Express sided with SlimDownRx, the merchant, citing their terms of service as the reason why I can not receive a refund for my fraudulent medication. Below are the details of what American Express received : Merchant : XXXX Amount : {$810.00} Date : XX/XX/year> Dispute Ref # : XXXX Dear American Express , Thank you so much for your help reclaiming {$810.00} from XXXX for goods and services that are not as described by XXXX. The Florida Department of Health has advised me to make my bank aware of the below facts involving this claim : Overview : This chargeback concerns fraudulent sale of counterfeit or illegal merchandise. 
The disputed charges are for prescription medication dispensed by a compounding pharmacy that I recently learned did not have a sterile compounding license to prepare this type of medication I learned this because they are now subject to Florida Department of Health disciplinary proceedings for this conduct.

Since the Department of Health complaint became public, the compounding pharmacy and the doctor who prescribed me the medication have claimed that they acquired the medication from a third party that did have a sterile compounding license, but they have refused to give me any information about the identify of that alleged third party. 
They have given names of third parties to other patients, but when contacted, those third parties have denied having prepared the medication. 
They have stated that XXXX XXXX provided central filled medication as a third party ; XXXX XXXX has refuted this claim ( see attached document ), stating that they only filled an order of XXXX vials of medication in the month of XXXX, which was after I received my order. 
The product was illegally manufactured and illegally shipped to me. I purchased it based on the doctors misrepresentations about its sources. 

XXXX XXXX has since relinquished its license and is no longer compounding medication due to the ongoing invenstigation. 

Please see the attached documentation outlining the Dept of Health 's recommendation not to use this medication, as well as other supporting documents. 

Thank you, XXXX XXXX

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative: My XXXX XXXX  issued by Citibank came up missing around XX/XX/XXXX. There was a charge made to my account on XX/XX/XXXX at XXXX in XXXX, XXXX  which is several hours away from my home. I got a fraud alert asking me to verify if the charge of XXXX was fraud and I replied with XXXX, indicatinf that it was fraud and began to look for my card. At that time I realized my credit card was missing and I looked at the text again and realized they were askiing me to confirm if the charge was valid or not and I had replied Yes, like the charge was valid, thinking I was replying yes that the charge was fraudulent. I immediately reach out to Citibank to advise them of this mistake and even advised the representative that I was speaking with at the time of the mistake and they advised me not to worry that would take care of it. They advised me that a police report was not needed but I did go ahead and file XXXX. I got a call from an XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX advising that they needed me to call them back to discuss the claim and I called back about an hour later to indicate that I got the message and was reaching back out. The agent explained to me that the agent had decided the claim against me and they were holding me responsible but at the time the reason why they were holding me responsible was not noted, but since I was still disputing the charge he would reopen the case, which I assume he did. I then got XXXX letters the next day, XXXX stating that they had been trying to reach me to discuss the case and had been unable to reach me and that I had 10 days to reply to the request or they would make a decision with the information they had. They only tried to reach out to me once, and they call my home number instead of my cell phone number which was listed as my primary number and they only tried once. Additionally they did not even give me 10 days like the letter said because I replied to the voicemail about an hour later and they had already denied my claim. I also got another letter dated the same date as the letter advising that I had 10 days to reply. saying that my claim had been denied and I was being held responsible. WHy send me a letter saying I had 10 days and not give me 10 days, I explained this and esclated to make a complaint with a supervisor and she told me that the case had been reopened and could take XXXX days to re review. I got another letter dated for today XX/XX/XXXX advising that all my account with Citibank had been closed after a security review and everyother account I had with Citibank had been closed as well, not just this one. Then I get another email with a letter saying that my dispute again had been denied. Its fine that they closed the accounts and is their right to do so but its not ok to hold me responsible for valid fraud charges. I even filed a police report to verify that this was fraud

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am filing a complaint regarding American Expresss handling of a dispute involving misrepresentation of goods at time of sale. 

I purchased a vehicle advertised as equipped with air conditioning, including the statement A/C DOES NOT COOL, which represents that an air conditioning system was installed but non-functional Upon mechanical inspection after delivery, it was discovered that the vehicle had no air conditioning system installed at all. 

This is not a defect or late-reported damage. It is a material misrepresentation of the goods at the time of sale. 

American Express has repeatedly denied my dispute based on the merchants internal reporting policy requiring issues to be reported within 2 days. This policy does not apply to misrepresentation at time of sale, where the goods delivered differ materially from what was advertised. 

Additionally, American Express stated that the merchant offered compensation which I declined. This is factually incorrect. No email, documentation, or communication exists in which the merchant offered any form of compensation. The documented email correspondence shows that no compensation was ever offered. 

The merchant instead requested documentation and a repair estimate, received all requested materials, and then denied the claim based on shifting reasons ( timing and cost ). 

I provided structured documentation, including a timeline of correspondence, demonstrating these facts. 

I am requesting that American Express review this dispute under goods not as described, rather than relying on merchant policy or inaccurate representations.

I called Barclays again yesterday to follow up and they said XXXX confirms the purchase was made by me. 

I believe someone hacked into my XXXX account and made the purchase under my name with the card I had previously used in XXXX of XXXX.

narrative:

( " Citi '' ), located at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX, regarding their failure to adequately investigate and resolve a billing dispute involving deceptive practices related to the misrepresentation of digital goods and services. XXXX handling of this matter constitutes a violation of Georgias Fair Business Practices Act ( O.C.G.A. 10-1-393 ( b ) ( 2 ), ( 5 ), ( 7 ) ) as well as federal consumer protection standards under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ), 15 U.S.C. 1666.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
On XX/XX/year>, I promptly notified Barclays Bank of Delaware about unauthorized charges on my Frontier Airlines account. Despite reporting these fraudulent transactions and initiating an investigation, the resolution process has been wholly inadequate. I would like to draw attention to several critical points regarding the inefficiencies and negligence in addressing this matter : 1. Lack of thorough investigation : Barclays was informed that unauthorized charges appeared on my credit card statement, specifically from Uber, which I had not authorized. The charges were linked to rides in the XXXX XXXX, not ordered by me, and not linked to my Uber account. Although I specifically requested Barclays to obtain a copy of my Uber account to cross-verify and dispute any rides not associated with my account, no such investigation was conducted. Instead, Barclays sent me a letter dated XX/XX/year> addressing only a small fraction of the disputed charges and stated, We have determined the activity is valid based on your previous purchase history with the merchant. I contacted Barclays and provided evidence that while I do visit the XXXX XXXX, I was not in the country at the time that many of these charges were made and that if I had ordered the rides, the rides, and charges would be linked to my Uber account. For example, I provided Barclays a copy of the tracking information of my passport showing that my passport was sent to the US Department of State for renewal on XX/XX/year>, specifically drawing their attention to charges on XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX from Uber for rides in the XXXX XXXX. I explained that I was in the US and without a valid passport as proof that these charges were not mine and again requested that Barclays obtain a copy of my Uber account and ask Uber to provide information about the account that initiated these rides in the XXXX XXXX. 
2. Inadequate response and failure to provide evidence : Barclays did not investigate this matter. To my knowledge, they have not contacted the merchant, Uber, to obtain a copy of my account which would show that these charges were not made or authorized by me. Additionally, Barclays has not provided me with information regarding the investigation or any evidence obtained in the investigation which led them to determine that these charges are valid. 
3. Discrepancies in account management : When I reported the charges on XX/XX/year>, I was told a credit would be placed on my account for these charges and an investigation would be conducted to determine where the unauthorized charges came from. My credit card was in my possession ( it was not lost or stolen ) so the account had to be cancelled. Despite canceling my credit card and receiving a replacement card ( which I never activated ) an automatic debit from my checking was made on XX/XX/XXXX for {$720.00}. I immediately called Barclays to report that the auto debit was withdrawn in error as Barclays informed me that they were placing a credit on my account for the fraudulent charges pending this investigation. The customer service agent I spoke to advised they would be sending me a refund check for the auto-debit withdrawal and that I would receive the check within 7-10 business days. After 2 weeks, I had not received the reimbursement check, so I contacted customer service again. At this time, I was advised a check was not mailed. Additionally, my account balance showed my account was past due for {$720.00} ( the statement balance which was debited from my bank account on XX/XX/XXXX ) The agent advised this was an accounting error on Barclays and that my account would reflect the payment and the late fees would be removed. I again asked when they would be issuing the refund check for the fraudulent charges that was auto-debited on XX/XX/XXXX and again was told that I would receive a check in 7-10 days. Barclays never issued the refund check ; they did not fix the late fees or the interest charges on my account but instead determined all the charges to be valid without conducting any investigation into this matter. The failure to rectify these errors in the balance and the recurring portrayal of my account as past due has been a consistent and concerning issue and it is having a negative impact on my credit score. 
4. Refund and resolution : Although Barclays acknowledged an erroneous auto debit and promised a refund, to date, no reimbursement has been issued. Moreover, there has been a lack of transparency in sharing the merchant 's response, necessary evidence, or the investigative process. 
5. Non-compliance with federal consumer protection laws : I must emphasize that as a consumer, I am entitled to protection under federal credit card fraud laws, particularly the Fair Credit Billing Act. It mandates a 90-day period for investigating and providing a written explanation detailing the supporting evidence of why I am responsible for contested charges. This regulation has not been adhered to by Barclays. 
6. I have provided documented proof, such as the USPS tracking information of my passport, a photograph of the envelope mailed to the Department of State, and my Uber account history, all of which unequivocally confirm my claims of non-authorization and account discrepancies. I must highlight my frustration in the repeated lack of adequate customer service support and the continuous disregard for a comprehensive investigation into this matter. This ongoing negligence has led to financial implications as Barclays is showing my account as past due, they are charging interest and late fees on an erroneous balance that was paid in full on XX/XX/XXXX, and it is having an adverse impact on my credit score. 
This letter serves as a formal notice that in parallel to my communication with Barclays, I have reported this matter to XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX credit bureaus. I have provided the bureaus with comprehensive evidence to support my claims and actions. Additionally, I have requested a police report in an attempt to obtain the account holder 's information of who requested the rides and paid for the Uber services with my credit card as Barclays has failed to do so. 
It is imperative that Barclays Bank of Delaware  promptly conducts a thorough and fair investigation into the unauthorized charges, reimburse me for the charges that were debited from my checking account, and rectify the account discrepancies, including the removal of late fees and removal of the past due notices from my credit report including restoring my credit score. I provided Barclays with the following information and received no response : Document 1 : Account Statement XXXX Document 2 : Account Statement XXXX Document 3 : Account Statement XXXX Document 4 : Screenshot of auto debit withdrawal from my checking account on XX/XX/year> for {$720.00} for the statement balance. 
Document 5 : USPS Tracking Information of Passport ( proof that I was not in the XXXX XXXX ) Document 6 : Photograph of Mailed Envelope containing passport mailed on XX/XX/year> ) Document 7 : Uber Account History showing that the disputed charges are not listed in my Uber account. 
Document 8 : Phone Call Screen Shot ( XXXX ) showing the duration of the call to customer service. 
Document 9 : Phone Call Screen Shot ( XXXX ) showing the duration of the call to customer service. 

Despite hours on the phone with customer service and evidence supporting these charges were not made with my Uber account, Barclays has not responded nor have they corrected their accounting error to reflect that I closed the account on XXXX the balance was paid in full on XX/XX/year> - and there should be no interest or late fees given the balance was paid in full and the account was closed, but they continue to compound interest and late fees monthly.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I have had an XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX through XXXX for more than XXXX  years. It was my very first credit card, and I have never had a problem. I always pay my statements on time. In fact I paid my account off, almost in full, this past spring. 

From approximately XXXX XXXX XXXX fraudulent charges were somehow made with my account, totaling more than {$2500.00}. The charges are all made in the XXXX, XXXX XXXX  area. I am a resident of XXXX XXXX and was home during this time period. I have never set foot in any of these establishments. XXXX never notified me that there was suspicious activity on my card. I saw the problem when I was checking my account online. 

When I first called XXXX the evening of XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, I was assured there would be an investigation into my case and not to worry about the charges. I was told to destroy the old card and was sent a new one. 

At the end of XXXX I received a note in the mail that attempts had been made to reach me for more information and because I hadn't replied the case had been closed. However, no attempts were made to reach me for more information, either by phone or email. 

I called back on XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, and was assured that my case would be reopened. 

Yesterday, XX/XX/XXXX, I received a letter in the mail from XXXX saying again that my case had been dismissed. XXXX says that an XXXX chip was used to make all of the fraudulent charges and because an XXXX chip can not be duplicated and the card was in my possession, the charges are not criminal. 

I don't know anything about XXXX chips. What I do know is that I was not in XXXX  on the dates of these charges. I have never shopped at or dined at any of the establishments in question. 

In fact, there is a XX/XX/2025 that I did legitimately make in XXXX XXXX XXXX {$140.00} for tickets to a show at the XXXX XXXX on XXXX XXXX. How could I make this charge at the theater box office and then the same day make XXXX purchases in XXXX at XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX XXXX. 

I called XXXX back yesterday evening after reading this final letter. I've been told that my case is reopened, but I lack confidence that I will be happy with the result. I am upset and very scared that I will be held responsible for these charges that I did not make.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement

When we arrived to the hotel 6 month later on XX/XX/XXXX, we were told by the hotel that all 3 rooms were sold out. With no where to go or turn because we are now stranded at a hotel in XXXX, we were instead put in 3 single bed bedrooms for XXXX adults, including XXXX men, my brothers. Unsatisfied, we checked out early and spent double our vacation budget to move my family to another hotel in XXXX. 

I was instructed by XXXX to dispute the charge with my credit card provider BarClayUS because we ordered 3 queen bedrooms and were not given what we pre-booked 6 months before our vacation. When disputing the charge I provided the credit card company with the original receipt of the booking, the receipt of the second hotel we moved ( with assistance from XXXX ), photos of out early check-out forms, and photos of my brother sleeping on a cot. This was NOT the 4.5 star experience we paid for, and this was NOT what we ordered. 

However, my credit company will not budge. They are aware we did nothing wrong. They are aware we did not purchase the rooms we were put in. And they are aware that we checked out early. I have been fighting this case now going on 10 months this year. What are my rights?

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:

Due to immigration concerns, I called back the next day ( XX/XX/XXXX ) and canceled the entire package approximately 15 hours after booking. Despite this, Priceline refused to issue a full refund and has since claimed that the hotel portion is non-refundable, which contradicts the terms communicated at the time of booking.

On the same day I canceled, I also contacted my credit card issuer, Barclays, to dispute the charge before it was even posted to my account. Despite this timely notice, Barclays failed to block the charge and only later issued a partial and temporary refund. I believe both companiesPriceline and Barclaysfailed to act in good faith and ignored their obligations to protect me as a consumer. I am requesting CFPBs assistance in holding both companies accountable and ensuring the full removal of these charges.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Re : Dispute XXXX CITI Credit Card XXXX XXXX Dear XXXX, We upgraded our XXXX XXXX concert tickets to the XXXX XXXX XXXX which included access to the XXXX XXXX. This XXXX XXXX was granted from XXXX XXXX XXXX as shown on XXXX XXXX instructions. We would not have had access to this XXXX had we not purchased this upgrade with the tickets we had. These are all facts that can not be disputed by the merchant. A large part of our purchase decision was based on having access to the XXXX XXXX from XXXX to XXXX, which is a longer timer period than the other items included in this XXXX XXXX upgrade ( more than 50 % more time, which is why I am requesting a 50 % refund ). We knew about XXXX XXXX access before we purchased this upgrade. 

This XXXX was a temporary tent in a parking lot outside the venue, with port-a-potties. There was not enough seating for everyone present and half the limited number of chairs were in the sun. This was not a relaxing room it was a tent in a parking lot with no air conditioning. These photos were taken before about XXXX people showed up, which was more than triple the amount of seating of XXXX chairs. How can this be considered a lounge if you cant even sit down and relax? This is truly false advertising. 

A lounge is defined as a room in a usually public building or vehicle often combining lounging, smoking, and toilet facilities according to XXXX XXXX. XXXX defines it as a place for sitting, waiting, smoking, etc., especially a large public room, as in a hotel, theater, or air terminal, often with adjoining washrooms. XXXX defines it as a comfortable room where people can spend time while they are waiting in an airport or other public place. 

If you perform a XXXX image search for XXXX  XXXX absolutely NONE of the images resemble what we paid for. I implore you to do the same image search and compare images. 

As you can see, this was not what we purchased. We purchased an experience with XXXX hours of XXXX XXXXXXXX access. This was not a room, it was a outside tent. This was not comfortable ; it was outside in the heat with not enough chairs. This was not labeled as a XXXX  XXXX- It was mentioned multiple times as a XXXX XXXXXXXX. To even get to this location, we had to exit the venue ( which has no in and out privileges ) and walk down to the middle of a parking lot! 

As half the time period of this experience was clearly not as labeled, I need a 50 % refund. This is reasonable. I have enclosed photos. 

This is misleading and deceptive advertising. This simply was NOT a VIP XXXX. For this reason, we deserve a 50 % refund. A tent in a parking lot outside the concert venue with port-a-potties is not a lounge.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am submitting this complaint regarding American Expresss refusal to properly investigate unauthorized transactions on my credit card account ending in XXXX. 

On XXXX XXXX, 2026, several transactions appeared on my account that I did not authorize, including a charge of {$8400.00} with TAP XXXX XXXX, as well as smaller charges of {$23.00} with Dollar Tree and {$55.00} with XXXX XXXX. I promptly contacted American Express on XXXX XXXX, 2026 to report these charges as unauthorized and requested a fraud investigation. 

American Express issued a temporary credit of {$8800.00} while they investigated the dispute. However, on XXXX XXXX, 2026, the company reversed the credit and claimed that the transactions were made by me. In their written response to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, American Express stated that they reviewed transaction data and other information and concluded that the charges were valid. 

Despite making this claim, American Express has refused to provide any documentation or evidence supporting their conclusion. They did not provide the airline reservation details, the passenger name associated with the ticket, the travel itinerary, the email address used to make the booking, the IP address used for the transaction, or any authentication records demonstrating that I authorized the purchase. 

In fact, in their written response they explicitly state that they do not have additional documentation to provide beyond what was used in their review. This raises serious concerns about the adequacy and fairness of their investigation. If American Express determined that I made the purchase, they should be able to produce documentation demonstrating that the ticket or reservation was associated with me. 

Holding a consumer responsible for a charge exceeding {$8000.00} without providing any evidence that the consumer authorized the transaction does not constitute a reasonable investigation under the Fair Credit Billing Act. 

Additionally, American Express reported the account as delinquent during the dispute process, which negatively affected my credit profile. Reporting derogatory information while a legitimate fraud dispute is unresolved further compounds the harm caused by this situation. 

I respectfully request that the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau require American Express to : 1. Reopen the investigation into the disputed transactions.

2. Provide the documentation relied upon when concluding that I authorized the TAP XXXX XXXX purchase and other charges. 
3. Remove the charges from my account if American Express can not produce evidence demonstrating that I authorized the transactions. 
4. Correct any negative credit reporting related to this dispute. 

Consumers should not be held responsible for large transactions when the creditor can not provide documentation demonstrating that the charges were authorized.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
On XX/XX/2023 I used my AmEx card to purchase items online from XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX totaling {$680.00}. On XX/XX/2023, I received a package from XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX, but it did not contain the items I ordered. Instead, it contained a lot of packing material, brown packing paper, and a cheap, used, plastic bathtub. I never received the items I ordered. 

I immediately phoned XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX 's customer service number, but it was out of order due to the business closing down. I contacted my mail carrier 's corporate number, and was only provided tracking information. The mail agent said the package was uninsured, and they could not help me as it did not qualify for a claim. They suggested I contact my bank. I then called AmEx for assistance. 

An agent helped me open a dispute for the charge the same day ( XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX days after the original charge ). I later found out, after receiving a denial for my initial dispute, that the agent selected the wrong dispute reason. She selected " not satisfied with quality of goods/services '' instead of " goods services not received. '' Another agent helped me re-file with the appropriate reason, since I never received the items. I continued to receive XXXX denial letters from Amex, over the last 4 months. Every time I called in, a different agent from a higher escalation team would reassure me that this would be taken care of, as I should not be responsible for goods not received. Each agent promised the dispute would get escalated to a higher tier, and reviewed by a human, instead of a computer. 

I have provided photos of the package and bathtub I received INSTEAD of the baby stroller and gift items I ordered, receipts, unanswered emails to XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, a police report, and every other piece of documentation I could to verify I never received my goods. And I keep getting auto denials citing " all sales were final after XX/XX/XXXX, please contact the merchant. '' I understand that all sales are final, and do not find this reason applicable to my dispute. And as I have told agents several times, I still have no method of contacting the merchant, who has closed down. 

Because AmEx has wasted 4 months of time, between XXXX and XX/XX/2023, intentionally misinformed me of my rights as a consumer, and provided false assurances during several RECORDED phone calls that the charge would be removed, I missed important deadlines and other levels of recourse with filing claims for compensation from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX restructuring firm, insurances, etc. They are holding me liable for charges that I should not be responsible for. I need help getting this {$680.00} charge removed from my statement balance. 

I trusted AmEx and their reputation for consumer protection. I elected to use my card for the assurances and protections they claim to provide. Disputes are in place for this very reason, non receipt of goods, and I meet all of the requirements to be eligible for this courtesy. The repeated dispute denial reason that " all sales are final '' DO NOT apply to this situation, as I am not attempting to return something, I am reporting that I NEVER RECEIVED my items. They have failed me as a loyal customer.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am filing this complaint regarding Citibanks repeated failure to protect my credit card account from unauthorized recurring charges, despite multiple corrective actions taken by me and explicit instructions provided to Citibank. 

Citibank allowed more than {$2500.00} in recurring charges to post beyond my credit limit, creating balances that I did not authorize and could not control. These charges were largely subscription and automatic renewal fees, not merchandise or one-time purchases. 

After discovering this, I locked my credit card specifically to stop recurring charges. Despite the card being locked, Citibank continued to allow recurring charges to post, defeating the purpose of the lock and rendering it meaningless. 

I then changed my card number multiple times, yet the same merchants continued to successfully charge my account. This indicates Citibank permitted account updater or similar services to pass new card numbers to merchants without my consent. 

Compounding this issue, I am unable to reliably download or access statements from Citibanks website, preventing me from timely identifying, documenting, and disputing these charges. 

I disputed several charges and explicitly instructed Citibank to stop allowing these recurring transactions. Despite this, the charges continued, and balances increased. Some merchantssuch as XXXXprocessed automatic renewals after I had explicitly declined them, and Citibank still approved the transactions. 

Citibanks actions ( or inaction ) have caused : Unauthorized balances Improper credit utilization Loss of control over my account Significant time spent disputing charges that should never have been approved

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I was the victim of a scam XXXX  card transaction on XX/XX/year> in the amount of {$1000.00}. I contacted Citi within XXXX hours of that transaction to report the fraudulent charge, dispute the transaction, and cancel my XXXX  card. I was not contacted by Citi again until XXXX when I received a letter stating that they closed the dispute and I was responsible. Citi had never asked me for information prior to that letter. I immediately contacted Citi upon receipt of that letter on XX/XX/year> and provided all information requested of me between XXXX XXXX. 
On XX/XX/year>, I received an email from Citi that it had asked the merchant to provide a response to my dispute. I emailed and requested that I be provided a copy of any information received from the merchant. When the merchant responded on XX/XX/year>, I again requested a copy of that information. Instead, Citi closed the dispute again, stating that the merchant provided a policy that stated the charge was nonrefundable. Because it was a scam charge, no policy was provided to me, and likely does not exist. I asked for the merchant 's information again on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX. Citi has refused to provide me any information relating to my dispute or its investigation. It also did not consider that I never actually received a service relating to the {$1000.00}, and had to pay for the service in full ( receipts were provided to Citi and are attached here ). 
Citi 's investigation process is prejudicial to cardholders/consumers because it provides XXXX transparency, fails to promptly communicate or notify the cardholder of any activity or insight into its decision-making, and accepts an alleged response from a scammer as a basis to close a dispute and make its customer responsible for a significant charge. Essentially, Citi 's dispute process sets the cardholder up for failure when submitting a transaction dispute involving a scam charge. All written communications with Citi and relevant submissions are attached here.

XXXX tactic-we asked Barclay to STOP payment immediately on XX/XX/XXXX they refused - started dispute. Standard practice. No diagnostic tool used prior to replacing transmission. See invoice XXXX XXXX XXXX. Note labor description : Found downshift still harsh. Vehicle may need computer??

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
XXXX XXXX: Received shipment from XXXX XXXX XXXX missing XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX items that were supposed to be inside according to XXXX XXXX  shipment confirmation. Box was slightly crushed and had been retaped (with a clear plastic tape over the original paper tape which was ripped). The value of the missing items (amount charged) was XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX customer service and explained the situation. Agent told me that because I received the package, I needed to file dispute with my credit card company. She advised she could only help if I did not receive the package at all.XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX (Barclays Bank) XXXX XXXX  and opened dispute XXXX XXXX  for the missing items over the phone.XXXX XXXX Received Letter from Barclays Bank advising they received a dispute for XXXX, which was no the item in question, advising they are reversing the credit (this was for the one item I did receive in the package, which I returned as it did not fit  it was never meant to be part of the initial dispute)XXXX XXXX I sent fax requesting to reopen the dispute, further explaining and providing screen shots of which items in the order confirmation were missing (which I had already described over the phone when opening the dispute with the agent on XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Barclays sent letter XXXX XXXX XXXX advising I had provided insufficient proof that the merchant has violated the invoice/contracts terms and conditions.XXXX XXXX After receiving another letter advising I had provided insufficient proof of the missing items, I again clarified which items were in scope of the dispute and explained that they should be able to see from the picture of the box on my doorstep, which they provided as evidence of the delivery, that it was bent up and re-taped. I again asked for the dispute XXXX XXXX to be reopened.XXXX XXXX Faxed another letter to XXXX XXXX XXXX  restating the issues and noting from a conversation with the Barclays dispute agent, she advised the case reason was disagreement with the quality of the merchandise which was incorrect and I never stated any issue with quality of merchandise. She advised me to reopen the dispute to have this reviewed. I never received any response to this concernXXXX XXXX: Received phone call back from manager based on my request, however he confirmed this cannot be reopened due to the time that has passed. 

There were many phone calls to discuss my concerns with the dispute department inbetween here, which Barclays will have on file, however the theme was that I had provided insufficient proof of missing items  to which I advised that I cannot prove I did NOT receive something. The only thing I could have done was video tape myself opening the box, which I would have done if I had known there would be missing items. I should not have to pay for items I did not receive.

Furthermore, Barclays made it difficult to process the dispute, as they did not give the option to file the claim online or attach documentation there. All correspondence had to be via fax which virtually no one uses in XXXX, and I had to pay to use a fax service each time. Furthermore, some of the letters from them were posted in my online account, but the later ones were not, so I had to wait over a week for them to come in the mail. Finally, I could not speak with a manager when I called, and it took almost a week for them to call me back. When they did, they did not help at all. Everyone just told me there is nothing they could do.'

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
We, XXXX XXXX XXXX ( my maiden name was XXXX XXXX XXXX when my CFPB account was created ) and XXXX XXXX, made a vacation rental reservation on XX/XX/XXXX, with XXXX XXXX for {$1400.00} in XXXX XXXX and used our Credit Master Card Barclays to pay for this purchase. This was to be our Christmas vacation and our Honeymoon. When we arrived in XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX, we contacted the rental property owner, XXXX, at ( the phone number +**-*-**-**-**-**- ) given to us by XXXX to get the keys to the rental property. 

We could not locate the property, so we pulled over and found a gentleman who could speak XXXX. We asked him to use his cell phone to call XXXX ( she doesn't speak XXXX ) to let her know we'd arrived and were ready to check-in. This was when we discovered that she did not rent out the property for XXXX and no longer has the rental available. XXXX refused to speak with us directly, only talking to the translator, leaving us stranded. 

This was not only our Christmas vacation but also our Honeymoon. We recently got married on XX/XX/XXXX. You could imagine the emotional distress we were undergoing at this time. Not only did we not have Wi-Fi service, but we could not call off the island with our cell phones because we needed international calling plans. 

The lovely gentleman who was our translator assisted us with locating other accommodations that evening, and we had to pay nearly the same amount as we had previously paid XXXX. 

This was our first time renting from XXXX ; we never intended to cancel our stay at the property since it was our vacation/honeymoon. We contacted our credit card company, Barclays Card, the same evening on XX/XX/XXXX ; we had to go to a random hotel to borrow their hotel phone and call out to inform Barclays Card of our incident. We asked Barclays Card to refund ( credit ) our transaction amount of {$1400.00} since we did not get the rental apartment we paid for. We gave Barclays Card XXXX 's phone number so they could speak to her directly regarding our incident that day. We also called Barclays Card in mid. XXXX to check in on the status of our transaction dispute and gave Barclays Card the property owner 's name and phone number again so they may contact her. 

Anytime Barclays Card called us, if we missed the call, XXXX XXXX ( wife ) would call back and always speak with a different agent. We were told everything was under control and would get resolved. We received a missed call on XX/XX/XXXX, regarding our dispute when XXXX XXXX ( wife ) called back, spoke to a different agent, and was told we would receive a decision. 

Although we appreciated the temporary credit from Barclays Card on XX/XX/XXXX, while they're still investigating, they issued their final decision to charge us back {$1400.00} plus interest and service charges on or after XX/XX/XXXX, which we felt was unacceptable. 

We received the dispute packet from Barclays Card on XX/XX/XXXX, along with their letter to us dated XX/XX/XXXX ; in the packet, there was no tangible proof of communication between Barclays Card and the rental property owner or between XXXX and the rental property owner, XXXX, as part of their investigation or of their findings. 

XXXX, on the other hand, sent a dispute to Barclays Card, which consisted of our rental confirmation ( XXXX  XXXX ) and ( XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX, and XXXX XXXX ) claiming we canceled the services, which is untrue ; the unreadable screen of cancelation and cancelation rules claimed the apartment was available to us the entire time and that we canceled on them without filling out proper documentation promptly. This is a blatant lie ; we didn't contact XXXX because we were stranded in XXXX XXXX, in the middle of nowhere, looking for our rental apartment. Since we did not intend to cancel, we didn't need to fill out any cancelation documents. So, the cancellation screen from XXXX on our account was left blank. It was our first time doing business with XXXX ; we never expected a horrible experience like this! We are both seniors ; we're in our early XXXX, law-abiding senior citizens, and we're not in the business of scamming financial institutions or vacation rental businesses. We believe we're being accused of something that we did not do, which is unacceptable and is causing us extreme emotional distress. If anything, we think we're victims of a possible scam of XXXX and the rental property owner in XXXX XXXX. This is the first time we've done business with XXXX. Someone has our money, and we never received the rental apartment we had paid for. We also feel Barclays Card took XXXX 's side, and this is why they did not credit us back the money we spent on the vacation rental that we didn't get. 

Since our case is not settled, we continue to suffer from daily emotional distress. We strongly believe XXXX and Barclays Card owe us a complete refund of {$1400.00} with interest and service charges reversed because XXXX did not have a proper agreement with the rental property owner, XXXX. XXXX of them was dishonest, and we suffered the consequences. 

According to Barclays ' letter regarding our dispute ( dated XX/XX/XXXX ), we did not receive the packet until XX/XX/XXXX. The letter said we can further pursue our claim by providing our response with any additional information within XXXX days from this letter, dated XX/XX/XXXX. Remember that we did not receive the packet until XX/XX/XXXX, so I understood that XXXX days from XX/XX/XXXX, should have been XX/XX/XXXX, according to my calendar. 

My husband and I sent a reply packet via XXXX mail on XX/XX/XXXX. I tracked my certified mail packet to Barclays card and saw they received our packet on XX/XX/XXXX. 

I have not heard back from Barclays Card after they received our dispute reply packet, so I decided to call Barclays Card today, Thursday, XX/XX/XXXX. The customer service agent from the Barclays Card Dispute department was insulting when I asked him the status of my dispute ; instead of answering, he scolded me for taking too long to send back my reply dispute. I was in shock that he had treated a long-time customer so unprofessionally. I explained to him that although they dated their letter to me as XX/XX/XXXX, their packet didn't arrive until XXXX days later, XX/XX/XXXX. So I counted XXXX days from XX/XX/XXXX to send my reply, which I sent on XX/XX/XXXX, which was XXXX days. I also told the agent I sent it via XXXX mail ; I have no control over how fast the mail travels to the Barclays Card address. 

The agent continued to berate me over how long I responded to them. I was frustrated and told him I couldn't believe he was scolding me and that I was a valuable customer of Barclays Card. I told him I just wanted closure on my dispute, so did they decide to close my case already? He admitted that Barclays chose to close my dispute case and that they would mail me a letter. 

I was distraught and disappointed with how Barclays Card treated me. If I didn't call them today, were they still planning on sending me a letter to inform me that they closed my dispute case and I'm out of luck on getting my money back?

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Best Buy Dispute On XX/XX/XXXX, I placed an order for pickup at the Best Buy store in XXXX XXXX XXXX, where the XXXX XXXX XXXX was listed as in stock. While driving to the store, a Best Buy employee informed me via phone that the item they had at the store was an open box. I declined the open box item, and the employee offered to change my order from pickup to shipping, assuring me it would arrive in XXXX days. I accepted the change to home delivery. 

The store employee changed the order from pickup to shipping with a delivery date of XX/XX/XXXX. Given the high value of the item, I took the day off from work on XX/XX/XXXX to be present for the delivery. The package arrived at XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, upon opening the package, I discovered that it contained a XXXX XXXX  and battery among other XXXX camera components, but the XXXX camera itself was missing. I immediately photographed the contents and took the box to the XXXX, NJ Best Buy. 

At the XXXX Best Buy, I spoke to the operations XXXX present on XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX XXXX, explaining the situation. He called the XXXX XXXX XXXX store to check if they could find a new camera for me to pick up but noted they only still had the open box camera available. XXXX advised the XXXX XXXX XXXX store to update the item 's status to open box since it was still listed as new on their website. The intention of this call was to see if I could still get a new camera at Best Buy. 

XXXX XXXX then requested that I call Best Buy Customer Service from my phone on speakerphone. My phone records show we called XXXX at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX. The customer service representative understood the story regarding the package and issued a refund with email confirmation by XXXX XXXX, all of which were conducted on speakerphone with XXXX XXXX present. The refund amount was {$4100.00}, the full cost of the camera. 

After the refund was completed, XXXX asked the customer service representative what to do with the package. The representative stated that the transaction is completed at XXXX XXXX of refund, and the package did not need to be returned to the return center since the transaction is closed at this point. She even mentioned it could be discarded. XXXX was still concerned that he could not keep the package at the store or throw it away and requested a return slip for shipment to the return center. The representative then sent a return slip to my email to give to XXXX. I forwarded this slip to XXXX and subsequently left the store to purchase the desired camera at Unique Photo in XXXX, NJ, using a different credit card ( XXXX XXXX ). 

Of note, I have been a Best Buy customer for more than 20 years and a Best Buy VISA Citibank card holder since about XXXX. I always try to buy products from Best Buy to support the company and use their card to get reward zone points. For the last XXXX years, I have paid for their membership program and before that, I have always been a Reward Zone member. 

XX/XX/XXXX The package that I left at the store on XX/XX/XXXX, was delivered to my house by XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX. Inside, there was a letter from Best Buy stating the return was rejected due to missing major items and my credit card had been charged again. I immediately contacted XXXX. The Best Buy customer representative reviewed my record, assured me of another refund, and apologized for the inconvenience, promising resolution within XXXX hours and an email confirmation. Note that the package did not need to be returned according to the communications between the XXXX with Best Buy customer service on XX/XX/XXXX and the package was sent back to Best Buy by XXXX XXXX ( Operations XXXX ) and not myself. I left the open package at the store. 

After hanging up, I contacted XXXX ( Best Buy VISA Citibank Customer Service ) to explain the situation of the return package and the charge being posted back to the credit card after it had been refunded. I was advised that if no refund appeared by Tuesday, XX/XX/XXXX, I should call again and consider filing a dispute with the credit card. 

XX/XX/XXXX I called Best Buy Customer Service to inquire about my refund. They informed me that Best Buy would not issue a refund again, flagging the return as fraudulent because the package contained a XXXX  controller and battery instead of the camera. I explained we all knew the contents of the package at the time of the return/refund and the package was sent back by the store XXXX despite instructions not to. They suggested visiting the store and speaking with XXXX about the situation. I mentioned that my demanding schedule as a physician would make this a challenge to coordinate. 

I visited the XXXX NJ store and spoke to a XXXX named XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ext XXXX ) since XXXX was not working that day. He reviewed the case, noting the mistake made by returning the package after the refund was issued. He explained that sending back the package was unnecessary after the refund given that the transaction is considered as CLOSED, but XXXX was trying to " do the right thing. '' He confirmed that Best Buy flagged the return as fraudulent due to incorrect components returned. Best Buys XXXX XXXX also believes that the package was sent by me instead of the store XXXX given that the return label provided to XXXX XXXX had my name printed on it. 

XXXX XXXX spoke to another supervisor on the phone. They say that they will try to investigate internally but that the best course of action is for me to file a dispute with the credit card company just as the credit card representative had suggested if a refund was not given to me by XX/XX/XXXX. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I began the dispute process and submitted all the documentation I had available at the time. This included the photo of the delivered package, proof of the refund at the store, proof of the order initiated as a pickup but changed to delivery, etc. 

XX/XX/XXXX : The dispute result was sent electronically by the Best Buy Citi company to the online credit card website dated XX/XX/XXXX. I did not see the notification until XX/XX/XXXX while at work in the hospital. The notification states that they determined there was no error on the charge because The merchants cancellation/Return Policy was not followed and Merchant has denied our request for credit on your behalf. Merchants arent required to accept return or cancellation requests... Ultimately, it is the responsibility of the customer to be completely aware of what they are purchasing and the merchants return policy. 

These statements are completely false. Firstly, the return was completed and accepted by the Best Buy store on XX/XX/XXXX in the presence of an operations XXXX and a customer service representative. The package was already opened, and all parties knew that the reason for the return was that the camera was missing. The return policy was honored by the merchant in this situation and refund given to me at that time. That should have been the end of this. Secondly, Best Buy ACCEPTED the return and provided a refund in person. Both statements provided to me are untrue since the return and refund was completed at the store following their own procedures and policies. 

Since XX/XX/XXXX, I have contacted the Credit Card company multiple times and spoken to the managers at the store to get my money back. There is no way to directly contact the Dispute Department to discuss all the evidence and materials that I had uploaded for the dispute. I feel that all that evidence has been ignored. 

This situation has caused stress, frustration, and considerable time spent on an issue thought to have been resolved on XX/XX/XXXX.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I purchased lunch at the airport Subway restaurant in XXXX  and XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX using my Barclays XXXX XXXX. My order included a turkey sandwich and bag of chips and the total was around {$20.00}. I did not keep the receipt. When I checked my credit card statement, I had been charged {$120.00}, so {$100.00} more than I authorized. 
I immediately contacted the credit card company ( Barclays XXXX XXXX ) and they said I needed to try to work with the vendor to get the situation resolved. I assumed ( based on experiences with other credit card companies ) that this charge was on hold and that I did not owe the balance due until the dispute was resolved. Apparently that was not the case so my bill became overdue and as soon as I realized this, I went ahead and paid the full amount due of {$120.00}, 2 days after the due date ( XX/XX/XXXX ) even though I didn't agree with that amount. 
Apparently the company then assessed a late fee and interest charges to my account which I was unaware of, because I wasn't expecting any charges to be made to that account that month since I had paid the amount due ( once again, even though I didn't agree with it ). 
After 2 months of late fees, I realized I had been charged and was now past due on the account. I called the company and was referred to the Dispute department who assured me they would help me resolve all of these issues, all the way back to the initial incorrect charge. My case ID was XXXX. I was told they would also refund my late fees, and I would end up with a credit on the account for the {$100.00} I was overcharged by the initial vendor. 
Fast forward another month, my XXXX statement not only did NOT have the late fees refunded, it actually charged me an ADDITIONAL late fee. At this point, my account is past due by 3 months of late fees because I was falsely promised that the late fees would be removed. I previously had a credit score in the XXXX and it has now dropped to XXXX assuming because of this mess. 
I just got off the phone with this company and after being transferred to XXXX different departments, they say they did nothing to resolve my initial dispute ( didn't reach out to the vendor for proof of transaction after I told them multiple times that I don't have a receipt ). After about an hour on the phone, I finally got someone's manager in the " late fee '' department ( XXXX, Employee ID XXXX ) to say he'll refund the late fees in XXXX business days. We'll see if that actually happens. 
I'm extremely frustrated and want to report this credit card company for not protecting their consumer against a fraudulent transaction in the first place and then furthering the pain with all these late fees and LYING about advocating on my behalf against a fraudulent vendor as well as LYING about refunding me late fees as I was told in XXXX. As soon as the late fees are refunded, I'm closing my account and would advise others not to use this company.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
On XX/XX/XXXX American Express denied my dispute for an unauthorized recurring charge from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX for the amount of {$3700.00}. The original purchase with the merchant was done entirely over the phone with a sales representative calling from the XXXX in XX/XX/XXXX. I agreed to pay for 1 year membership, that was it. I never agreed to any written Terms & Conditions, auto-renewal, recurring billing, or subscription contract. I was never provided nor did I accept the contract the merchant is now claiming governs the account. On XX/XX/XXXX such merchant charged my amex card for {$3700.00} for a subscription based service that I didn't agree to. 

The merchant claims they emailed and mailed me the Terms & Conditions, but I never received any such communications. The transaction was completed by phone, and no digital contract, email, signature, or click-to-accept process was ever provided. 

I asked American Express to obtain proof that : I agreed to the Terms & Conditions I agreed to auto-renewal or recurring billing I was provided the Terms at enrollment I received a renewal notice before the new charge None of this proof was provided to me. 

Additionally, the merchant failed to deliver a key contracted service : a guaranteed pro-bono placement included in the paid program for the period of XX/XX/XXXX to XX/XX/XXXX. This constitutes a breach of contract. 

I am requesting the CFPB review Amexs dispute-handling process, require Amex to obtain proper documentation from the merchant, and reconsider this charge as unauthorized recurring billing and services not provided.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
My friend, XXXX XXXX and I, made a purchase with a merchant. My purchase was for {$27000.00} and XXXX 's was for {$40000.00}. The principal objective of the purchase was for his company to build us e-commerce stores. We were responsible for some work on the project- namely optimizing our credit profiles to obtain credit from banks to fund the e-commerce store and his team was responsible for building the stores. Despite XXXX and I successfully upholding our end of the bargin, he took our money did not complete the project by the deadlines set out, and his team refused to show us the work that had been done. We filed chargebacks for no services received with a number of the banks and were successful in a majority of those chargebacks. AMEX refused our chargeback on the grounds that their cardmember agreement prohibits the use of the card for an investment/security and that this was an investment/security, not the purchase of a service. 

We don't believe this is a security, as determined by the XXXX test - the accepted legal framework for determining whether something is an investment contract/security or not. But first, I would like to understand how the cardmember agreement explicitly prohibits the use of the AMEX card for investments. The relevant section of the cardmember agreement says : Eligible Purchases Eligible purchases are purchases for goods and services minus returns and other credits. 
Eligible purchases do NOT include : fees or interest charges, purchases of traveler 's checks, purchases or reloading of prepaid cards, purchases of any cash equivalents, or personto-person transactions. Additional terms and restrictions apply. 

Since there is no mention of invesments or securities in the eligible purchases section of the agreement, where are these additional terms and restrictions? AMEX must show us that we agreed to not use our card for the purchase of investment contracts. 

Even if they are able to show us that we were bound to not use the card for investment contracts, we believe this was a service contract. AMEX must demonstrate to us how our contract- which is entitled a contract for services - passes all prongs of the XXXX XXXX. 

The XXXX Test : " The test [ for an investment contract ] is whether the scheme involves an investment of money in a common enterprise with profits to come solely from the efforts of others. '' We believe our contract fails to meet two prongs of the XXXX test : Those of a common enterprise, and that the profits come solely from the efforts of others. 

A common enterprise is typically determined by whether there is " horizontal commonality '' which generally requires that investors are told that their funds are pooled together. Horizontal commonality is the tying of each individual investors fortunes to the fortunes of the other investors through the pooling of assets, usually combined with the pro-rata distribution of profits. In the SG case, participants were told that their funds were to be pooled in a single account. We were not. Moreover, we were each told we would be getting different e-commerce stores. In some cases, participants might share an e-commerce store. In my case and in XXXX 's case, we were told we would be getting separate stores and there was no mention of our funds being pooled together. 

On the requirement about profits coming solely from the efforts of others, we were both involved in the work after paying the merchant the fee. We were asked to optimize our credit profiles and spend hours meeting with banks to obtain credit that we would use to fund the stores. Each of us allocated more than XXXX hours of work to this process. So the effort was not solely on the part of the merchant- we were very involved in the work. 

AMEX needs to show us exactly how an investment contract violates the cardmember agreement and if they are able to do so, they need to show us exactly how this is an investment contract. They have not done so despite us asking for more than four months. The only argument I have gotten from AMEX as to why this is an investment contract is that the merchant has classified itself on the AMEX system as an investment manager/asset manager. But we don't believe this is a good argument, as we had no idea how the merchant classified itself in the AMEX system. An investment manager could sell all kinds of products and services that are clearly not investments. It's really about the specific purchase we made. That purchase was for predominantly for the creation of an e-commerce store.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
American Express Credit Card is violating my rights as well as their own terms and conditions. 
The scenario is as follows. I made two purchases for the same dollar amount from the same merchant XXXX. Both charges are for the same item in different sizes. I returned BOTH and have 2 separate receipts for each item returned where the merchant promised me a refund. 
The credits however failed to materialize on my Amex credit card account for both charges, where only ONE of the charges was actually credited to my account not two. I proceeded to dispute the case with my bank, American Express, and for months on end they have refused to credit me despite me providing PROOF of both returns. 
This isnt a case of he said she said, its a clear cut case where I do not have the merchandise since the merchant already ACCEPTED it back and promised a refund. 

I tried to go to the merchant which is a huge company and the clerks do not have the power to help me since they already processed the refund on their end. Additionally the merchant later on states that I return too many items and they do not want to help me further to get the money back from Amex. They are not required to accept my return but they ARE required to refund me in the event that they do accept the return as in this case so where is my money. 

Regardless, Amex is the bank and they are the ones who have access to my money which is rightfully owed to me as proven by my receipts. 
Amex however, refuses to give me my money back dragging this along for months spitting back generic robotic communications as to why they will not refund me. Sometimes they even brush me off saying that I already was refunded. This is absurd as I explained to them so clearly that I was refunded only once but not twice as I am supposed to be. 
Again this is a violation of my rights as I provided clear proof from the merchant that owes me the money based on returning both items. 

( I might add that the worst part of this ordeal is how Amex completely disregards me as a customer dragging this on for months and not even communicating with me. Rather they just close the case after several weeks of no communication with a generic response often robotic ). 

The amount in question is {$3400.00} This is an ongoing case since XX/XX/2023. 

I have attached both receipts showing 2 separate invoices for credit. I am due still one more credit.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I purchased from XXXX a large table that has rolling massaging function on XX/XX/year>. I purchased package protection in the amount of {$10.00} to ensure that I will be getting this package securely via in person pick up or with signature. This package protection ensures protection of this shipment, including easy return, any potential loss or damage to the item. The company uses XXXX per their policy and that was the service I agreed to. However, they changed to XXXX shipping and excluded any secured delivery signature requirement. The item is supposed to be delivered to me, based on tracking information, on XX/XX/XXXX. I never received the item. 

I called XXXX to inquire about the item and delivery. They claimed that the item is delivered to front door, with no signature requirement. I called XXXX and they claimed that the item is delivered with an image of the product left at a different front door, likely in another building within the community that I live in. I looked at the image and saw that it does not resemble my unit front door, with the door fixture showing as a different type of lockpad for entering. The image is extremely blurry as well to even know which unit it was delivered to. There are many similar buildings with the same unit number, and that it can be easily delivered to a different building of the same unit. But the fact that the front door fixture is completely different, that in itself should be sufficient proof that the item is not delivered to the correct address. 

I quickly called XXXX to ask them where exactly was the item delivered and to assist me with locating the package. They said I must reach out to the merchant to do so, as the recipient I have no right to open investigation. I contact XXXX and they said they will look into it, but I should ask around to see if any neighbors or building management may have collected it. I've looked and asked around the best that I can due to most buildings having their separate security system which I am unable to enter. I've waited over XXXX weeks, and when no response from both Merchant and XXXX, I reached out to my credit card company to ask them to assist with resolving this issue. 

Credit card company issued by Citi Bank was used in this purchase, in the amount of XXXX. They preliminarily issued a credit in that amount while pending investigation. The concluded on XX/XX/XXXX hat since XXXX had a delivery confirmation of the package at a delivered location, that it is considered delivered to me. I reached out to Citi XX/XX/XXXX to reiterate that the item was not delivered to my front door. The apartments in all the communities are similar but the confirmation delivery image of the door has a large black lock that looks nothing like my front door and keypad. 

They concluded that because an image is attached to a delivery confirmation, that in itself is sufficient and refused to investigate that it was in fact not delivered to me, and they disregarded the fact that I had purchased extra secure delivery, to make sure the item would have some kind of signature requirement, or allowing me to pick up at the local store upon arrival. Citi claims the case was concluded and that there is no need to further investigate this issue, despite item is not delivered to my front door. I am now at a loss of the full retail value of the item, and not having received the item. I am not sure what else I can do, as my plead for assistance to XXXX, XXXX, and Citi bank, is not leading to any resolution. I am not sure why I was given the option to purchase package protection, insurance, and secure delivery to prevent loss or damage, but when an issue had occurred, all parties ignored my plead to even investigate the most basic evidence. 

Please reach out to me at your convenience, I would like to speak with a representative who may be able to assist me with this claim.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
XXXX XX/XX/2023, my credit monitoring service sent me an alert - that a charge had appeared on a card I had not used in a while ( a Citibank card ending in XXXX that i had not used in a year ). I went to the account and saw a charge of {$160.00} for XXXX XXXX made on XX/XX/XXXX that was showing up on my statement. 

XXXX immediately called Citibank and reported this as fraud. The person I spoke to confirmed that I had not made the charge, nor had I authorized anyone to, and then said she was opening a dispute. I asked that my card number be changed because me card was compromised and she said something to the effect " of course, I'm surprised that didn't pop up before ''. 

XXXX went to check on this in the online dispute center a bit later, and noticed two weird things : XXXX ) I had both a purchase dispute and a fraud dispute open for the {$160.00} charge, both noted as reported by me on XXXX ; XXXX ) XXXXwo additional transactions were under the " fraud dispute '' section listed as " transaction did not post '', one by the same vendor for the same amount but attempted XXXX, and XXXX by " XXXX XXXX XXXX '' for {$170.00} on XXXX. Both were listed as being disputed by me on XXXX, which I didn't ( I wasn't even aware of them ). 

XXXX XX/XX/XXXX, I called Citibank about this. I was told that a mistake had been made on their end, and that they would work it out and I needed to " watch for the letters ''. I was also given XXXX dispute numbers as reference : Fraud dispute - XXXX Purchase dispute - XXXX -On XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX, I checked the online dispute center ( having heard nothing ) and saw that the purchase dispute was active, that the " merchant had responded '', and the fraud dispute for the {$160.00} charge was gone ( the fraud disputes for the transactions that hadn't posted were still there ). I called the fraud department to re-report ; they acknowledge that their notes confirmed that I had reported this charge as fraud, and that they would put *more* notes in. I asked that this please be confirmed with me in writing ; I was told no. I then closed my account ( I hadn't activated the card with the new number ) because I was not confident in the way they were handling this. 

XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX, I checked the online dispute center instead. I saw a notification that I was responsible for the charge, that the conditional credit would be reversed, and saw that all the fraud disputes had been erased. I called billing disputes to relay the investigation and was told : XXXX ) Again, that their notes showed that I had reported the charge as fradulent. 
XXXX ) That the purchase dispute investigators had been corresponding with me ( they haven't, I double checked ). 
XXXX ) That again this was fine, I had to just respond to a letter that they had sent me confirming that I hadn't made the charge. 
XXXX ) That, contrary to # XXXX, she couldn't find the fraudulent charge in their records. Yikes. 

I noted that this was violating my fraud protections under the XXXX that limit my liability to {$50.00} for fraudulent charges. I again asked for written confirmation that I had reported the charge as fraud on XX/XX/XXXX, and even said check the recording to confirm. I got a noncommital answer but I was told that, again, the report of fraud was going " in the notes ''. I reiterated my request that I get written confirmation of what they were saying was in their notes. I'm not optimistic as she was noncommittal and said she didn't understand why I had gotten nothing in writing so far. 

Whether intentionally or due to incompetence, Citibank is violating my rights under the XXXX. I am liable for up to {$50.00}, not {$160.00}. 

I have attached screenshots of the dispute portal showing the fraud disputes ( now erased from the portal ) and the purchase dispute that was erroneously opened being decided in the merchant 's favor.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am submitting this complaint regarding Citis handling of a disputed charge with XXXX XXXXXXXX in the amount of {$400.00}. 

I timely filed a dispute for this charge, and Citi confirmed that a conditional credit would be issued and resolved within the standard 60-day dispute period. Instead of resolving the matter, Citi later reopened the dispute in late XXXX without a clear explanation. 

Subsequently, Citi sent me the following written notice : When resolving your dispute, we credited your account for {$310.00} in excess. To correct this, we are debiting your account in the amount of {$310.00}. This adjustment will appear on your subsequent billing statement. We hope this information is helpful. We apologize for any inconvenience this may have caused. 

The {$310.00} referenced in this letter is not related to the {$400.00} dispute. That amount pertains to a separate transaction ( XX/XX/year> ) with the same merchant that was refunded directly by the merchant, outside of and unrelated to the disputed charge. It appears the dispute investigator incorrectly associated this unrelated refund with the active dispute, resulting in an improper debit to my account.

Since receiving this notice, I have contacted Citi at least ten ( 10 ) times to resolve this issue. Each call results in the same outcome : I am told that an investigation, audit, or back-office review will be initiated and that I should expect a response within 710 days. I have never received any follow-up, written response, or resolution. 

In several instances, I have been disconnected while on hold, further preventing resolution. Despite repeated attempts, I have been unable to reach anyone who can correct the error or provide documentation explaining Citis actions. 

As it stands, Citi has improperly debited {$310.00} from my account, and I am owed this amount. I am requesting CFPB assistance to ensure that : The unrelated {$310.00} refund is correctly separated from the {$400.00} dispute, The improperly debited {$310.00} is returned to my account, and Citi provides a clear, written explanation of how this error occurred and how it will be prevented in the future. 

Thank you for your assistance in resolving this matter.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement

The dispute involves XXXX large transactions of {$1500.00} and {$300.00} dated XX/XX/XXXX and XXXX, 2025, for purchasing a XXXX XXXX console and white-glove delivery service from XXXX, XXXX online merchant. Upon unboxing the item myself, I discovered that the back of the unit was severely damaged, appearing nearly cracked in half, consistent with damage from improper handling, possibly by a forklift. I have photographic evidence of this damage. Given the condition of the packaging and delivery method, I have reason to believe that XXXX was aware of the damage prior to delivery and intentionally arranged for a quick drop-off without setup to avoid immediate discovery. Despite repeated outreach efforts, XXXX has not responded to my emails. This is especially troubling because they advertise an easy and simple return policy. Their website touts product warranties and dedicated customer support, neither of which has been upheld. I provided clear photographic documentation of the damage and explained the situation thoroughly, yet received no acknowledgment or resolution. I attempted to initiate a transaction dispute through Barclays ' online portal. There was no option to upload supporting documentation, such as the photos of the damaged product or copies of my email correspondence with XXXX. The dispute case appears to have been dismissed or marked unresolved without a thorough review or follow-up. Barclays ' lack of a robust dispute resolution process fails to protect consumers from merchants engaging in deceptive practices. It is unacceptable that Barclays allows a merchant like XXXX to continue charging customers for undelivered services and damaged goods, and offers no effective recourse through its dispute system. I have been a longstanding and responsible Barclays cardholder, consistently paying my bills on time. This is now the second time I have experienced a failure in the dispute process, and as a result, I intend to close my account once the current balance is paid in full. I will also no longer consider Barclays for future credit or financial services. I paid for both a product and a premium delivery service that were not delivered as described. The item received is heavily damaged and effectively unusable. The lack of support from both XXXX and Barclays has left me without resolution and at risk of financial loss. The item is also too heavy and structurally compromised to move or return without assistance.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:

I filed a dispute with American Express with documentation that included evidence of the shipping weights. Despite this, American Express ' response was that I was responsible due to the empty box, and that if I had any proof I had shipped the phone, I should provide it. 

I reopened the dispute with American Express and once again provided documentation of the shipping weights. This time, American Express response was that I was responsible because the shipping weight was heavier than XXXX expected. This is in direct contradiction to the first response that it was an empty box! 

I reopened the dispute once again pointing out that these XXXX responses were in direct contradiction. American Express responded that I was responsible unless I had proof that XXXX had promised a refund ( alluding to the initial call I had with XXXX ). 

American Express did not investigate my dispute in good faith, and keeps changing what proof I need to provide, and has put the onus on me as a single customer to prove that I had returned the phone despite XXXX being a XXXX XXXXXXXX company that has the ability to file a claim with XXXX, and also the ability to track/brick stolen phones.
