dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am filing this complaint regarding American Expresss denial of my fraud claim involving a {$4000.00} XXXX XXXX transaction that was authorized only because I was deceived by individuals impersonating XXXX customer support during a XXXX XXXX sale. 

On XX/XX/year>, I listed a chair for sale on XXXX XXXX and was contacted by a person claiming to be interested in purchasing it. The buyer stated that payment would be sent through XXXX. I then received what appeared to be an official XXXX email stating that the payment had been sent but that I needed to upgrade my account from a personal account to a business account in order to receive the funds. 

Following these instructions, I was contacted by individuals posing as XXXX customer support using a fake XXXX support number and fraudulent email communications designed to appear legitimate. These individuals instructed me to authorize a {$4000.00} American Express XXXX XXXX transaction, falsely claiming that this was required to complete the account upgrade and receive the buyers payment.

To make the scheme appear legitimate, the scammers remained on the phone with me while instructing me to call American Express to authorize the XXXX XXXX transaction. They also used XXXX and multiple phone calls to impersonate XXXX representatives. In addition, I have voicemail evidence showing that the same individual was simultaneously using similar fraudulent statements with another victim.

Relying on these false representations and believing I was interacting with legitimate XXXX support, I authorized the {$4000.00} XXXX XXXX transaction. 

As soon as I discovered that I had been defrauded, I immediately reported the matter to American Express and submitted supporting evidence, including : fraudulent emails impersonating XXXX, text messages, voicemail recordings, documentation of the impersonation scheme. 

American Express reviewed the claim and denied reimbursement on the basis that the transaction was authorized, without adequately addressing the fact that my authorization was obtained through impersonation, deception, and fraudulent misrepresentation. 

Although I physically authorized the transaction, that authorization was induced through fraud. American Express has failed to meaningfully consider the evidence demonstrating that I was the victim of an imposter scam in which fraudulent actors posed as XXXX representatives and deceived me into authorizing the transaction under false pretenses. 

I am requesting that American Express : Reopen and fully investigate the {$4000.00} Split Pay transaction Provide the records and basis relied upon in denying my claim Reimburse the {$4000.00} loss resulting from this fraudulent scheme I have also filed reports with local law enforcement and have preserved the evidence related to the fraudulent communications and impersonation. I am requesting regulatory assistance because American Express denied my claim solely because the transaction appeared authorized, despite substantial evidence that the authorization was obtained through fraud.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am filing this complaint against American Express for failing to properly investigate a billing dispute under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) regarding a charge of {$1200.00} from the merchant " XXXX XXXX '' in XX/XX/2025. 

Amex has sided with the merchant despite clear evidence of material misrepresentation and non-delivery of services. 

1. The " Bait and Switch '' ( Non-Delivery of Services ) : In XX/XX/2025, I purchased a 6-month advertising contract for the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX which included an agreement for approximately 10 leads per month. 

Breach of Contract : Less than three weeks into this 6-month agreement, XXXX announced they were terminating this specific product model in my market to replace it with a different model ( " Flex '' ), unilaterally voiding the 6-month term I had just purchased. 

Non-Delivery : During the brief three weeks the contract was active, XXXX failed to deliver the agreed service. Despite the target of 10 leads/month, I received XXXX leads. They collected {$1200.00} upfront for a 6-month service that was going to cost {$1200.00} per month they knew they were canceling, delivered nothing, and then refused to refund me.

2. Rebuttal to Merchants Defense : In an attached email dated XX/XX/2025, the merchant representative ( XXXX XXXX ) admits to " Flex changes '' occurring during my contract but falsely claims I am requesting a refund based on " campaign performance. '' This is a diversion tactic. " Performance '' implies the service was delivered but ineffective. That is not the case here. I received XXXX leads ( complete non-delivery ), and the merchant cancelled the 6-month contract I paid for to switch to a new platform.

I am not disputing because the ads " didn't work '' ; I am disputing because the service I bought was not delivered and was discontinued by the merchant immediately after taking my money.

3. Amexs Procedural Failure : American Express failed to review this evidence. Even worse, they mishandled the dispute process : Amex initially closed my dispute with the notation " Closed per Cardmember Request. '' This is false. I never requested to close the dispute.

This internal error forced me to restart the process by months. The entire process took nearly 7 months for them to decline my dispute.

When finally reviewed, Amex rubber-stamped the merchant 's " performance '' excuse without acknowledging the breach of the 6-month term or the zero leads delivered.

Under the FCBA, I am not liable for services " not accepted by the consumer or not delivered to the consumer in accordance with the agreement. '' Amex is attempting to enforce a debt for a voided, discontinued service.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Hello, I am writing about an unauthorized transaction that Citi has acknowledged as unauthorized, but refuses to refund the charges for. It is a rather complicated situation on which I need your assistance. 

Transactions : XX/XX/year> Charge {$980.00} from XXXX XXXX XXXX XX/XX/year> Charge {$840.00} from XXXX XXXX XXXX XX/XX/year> Credit {$840.00} from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX charged me {$980.00} for a full week stay when checking in, but I only stayed one night, so I was due a refund of {$840.00}. As you can see, they did give me a credit of {$840.00}, but only after an unauthorized charge of the same amount was made to my credit card. 

I disputed the {$920.00} and it was confirmed the credit was for the unstayed nights. This was also confirmed by the account statement from XXXX XXXX XXXX. 

However, XXXX XXXX XXXX would not credit back the unauthorized charge of {$840.00}. So I called XXXX XXXX XXXX again and finally spoke with someone who was willing and able to look up the charge. ( I called XXXX XXXX XXXX at least a dozen times trying to resolve the issue before and during the disputes entered with Citi. ) That person said the {$840.00} was a charge from a third party website and the person on the phone had no idea how whoever made the charge would have gotten my credit card information. ( However, the charge on the credit card statement shows it came from XXXX XXXX XXXX. ) ( I also escalated the issue with XXXX XXXX  who told me the locations are individually owned and they could do nothing to help. ) So I filled the {$840.00} charge as a fraudulent charge with Citi. After first assuming I had been credited the {$840.00}, Citi finally understood this was a new separate charge. So after I confirmed the charge was unauthorized, Citi canceled my old card and issued a new one. 

There had not been any other unauthorized charges, but in their letter ( see attachment ) Citi stated they replaced my credit card to prevent further unauthorized activity. So Citi acknowledged the charge was unauthorized. 

The letter went on to say Citi forwarded my information concerning the unauthorized charge made with the card ending in XXXX to our Security Department for further review. Again, acknowledging the charge was unauthorized. 

As can be seen from the credit card statement, I was already in XXXX Washington XXXX / Idaho by the time the {$840.00} charge was made, so I was nowhere near XXXX, nor would I have had any need or interest in staying at the XXXX XXXX XXXX. Further evidence the charge was fraudulent. 

I also filed a report about this unauthorized charge of {$840.00} with the XXXX  XXXX Police Department. 

However, Citi has for an unexplained reason decided I am responsible for this unauthorized charge, even though the law says I am only responsible for the first {$50.00}. I sent a letter to Citi to remove the {$840.00} charge from my account, but I have not received any response. 

Please help me get this situation resolved. 

Thank you! 

Attachments : 1 - Credit card statement showing the transactions listed above.

2 - My letter to Citi explaining the three transactions for the {$980.00} dispute.

3 - My letter to Citi explaining the transactions for the {$840.00} dispute.

4 - Account statement showing the credit was against the original charge of {$980.00}.

5 - Citi letter acknowledging the charge is unauthorized, but putting the charge back on my statement.

6 - My letter to Citi asking for them to remove the charge from my account.

7 - The XXXX XXXX  Police Department report.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am writing to formally document and escalate an issue regarding a hotel booking made through the American Express travel portal for the {$300.00} hotel credit. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I booked a hotel stay through the American Express credit card portal specifically to use my {$300.00} annual hotel credit before the end of the year. The booking was completed through the American Express website, which reasonably led me to believe that the stay qualified for the promotion. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I reviewed my online statement and noticed that the full amount of the hotel stay of {$440.00} was charged to my account and that no portion of the {$300.00} credit had been applied. I immediately contacted American Express and was informed that the hotel was not part of the promotion. I questioned why a non-qualifying hotel was available for booking through the portal without any clear indication that it did not qualify for the credit. At that time, I requested that a ticket or claim be opened regarding this issue. 

After receiving no response or follow-up, I called again on XX/XX/XXXX, and spoke with three different customer service representatives, including a supervisor. Each advised me that nothing could be done. Notably, I was also told that many other cardmembers have experienced the same issue, which strongly suggests a systemic problem with how qualifying hotels are presented on the American Express portal. 

If multiple customers are encountering this same issue, the portal is misleading and not user-friendly. Customers reasonably expect that booking a hotel through the American Express site, especially when attempting to use a time-sensitive benefitwould clearly indicate whether the booking qualifies for the associated credit. The current setup creates confusion and leads customers to incur charges they believed would be offset by a promised benefit. 

I am requesting a formal review of this matter, including reconsideration of the {$300.00} hotel credit for this booking, and an explanation of how American Express intends to address what appears to be a widespread and misleading issue within its booking platform. 

Thank you for your time and attention. I look forward to your response..

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I was charged {$220.00} for a one-night stay at a hotel in XXXX. I did not receive the service that I paid for because the hotel had XXXX XXXX, so I couldn't stay there. I left the hotel without checking in and spent the night at a different hotel. The hotel desk manager refused to issue a refund. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I disputed the charge with Citi. On XX/XX/XXXX, the merchant responded to the dispute and falsely claimed that I was seeking a " free night stay. '' The case number on the merchant 's response form didn't even match my dispute case number. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, Citi 's disputes team notified me via email that they found me responsible for the charge. The same day, I appealed Citi 's decision and provided detailed information refuting the merchant 's response, including XXXX  receipts, a receipt for the other hotel I stayed at, and photos of XXXX XXXX XXXX on the hotel mattress. On XX/XX/XXXX, I called Citi to confirm receipt of these documents and to reiterate that the merchant 's response contained false information. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, the disputes team notified me via email that they had again found me responsible for the charge because I didn't provide " a credit slip or chargeback approval letter from the merchant to prove they agree you are due credit for this charge. '' If the merchant agreed that I was due credit for the charge, I would not have needed to file a dispute in the first place, so it was not reasonable for Citi to mandate a credit slip to process this chargeback. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I called Citi, and a representative said she'd ask for the dispute to be reopened. She offered me a partial credit of about {$100.00} ( less than half of the amount disputed ) for being a longtime customer. I thanked her and said I'd still like the dispute reopened. I never received the credit that I was offered. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, after receiving no follow-up from Citi, XXXX called again, and a different representative said my dispute was denied because it was miscategorized as Goods/ Services Not as Expected rather than Goods/ Services Not Received. After recategorizing it, he told me the dispute would be reviewed again. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, the disputes team notified me a third time via email that they still found me responsible for the charge because they needed " documentation showing the merchant agreed to credit your account. '' Again, this was not reasonable documentation to require, given the circumstances. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I called Citi and explained that the request for a credit slip was unreasonable. The representative said Citi would " make this right '' for me by issuing a credit for the full amount of my disputed charge, which would post to my account in 3 to 5 days. I never received this credit. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I called Citi again to ask about the status of my credit. The representative that I spoke with said there was no record in my account that anyone had agreed to give me a credit. She told me the dispute was open again and I should hear back in, once again, 3 to 5 days. 

I did not receive the service that I paid for on XX/XX/XXXX, and I have now been disputing this charge for over two months. I have sent Citi extensive documentation via email and have spoken to representatives five times. Citi keeps finding me responsible for the charge for reasons that don't make sense, and a Citi representative outright lied to me about resolving the situation with a refund.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Dear Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ), I am writing to bring to your attention a serious matter concerning the unprofessional behavior I have experienced with my credit card provider, CitiBank. As a long-time customer for over seven years, I am deeply disappointed by the level of service I have encountered, particularly regarding a recent dispute I had with a merchant. 

On XX/XX/2024, I made a purchase through XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX website for a pair of sneakers. After waiting several days for my order, I discovered that it had been canceled without my knowledge, and I was never issued a refund. After trying to resolve the issue directly with Citibank for nearly two months, I learned that they had closed the case in favor of the merchant, despite my clear evidence that my order was canceled. 

CitiBank allegedly sent a notice regarding this decision through the app 's notice section ; however, I never received any such notification. Additionally, I submitted all proof demonstrating that the order was indeed canceled and that I was entitled to a refund. Despite this, CitiBanks dispute department failed to take my side or handle the situation appropriately, siding instead with the merchant.

I have uncovered issues with CitiBanks dispute handling that extend beyond my individual case ; many other customers have voiced similar complaints concerning their disputes with merchants. This pattern of unprofessionalism and poor service is unacceptable. The dispute department appears to lack the necessary competence and commitment to fairly resolve customer issues. 

I am requesting the CFPB 's assistance in addressing this matter. It is crucial that consumers are treated with respect and that credit card companies like CitiBank are held accountable for their actions. We deserve a banking experience that prioritizes customer service and transparency. 

Thank you for your attention to this serious concern. I look forward to your response and any actions you can take to improve the situation for myself and other consumers facing similar challenges. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Re : American Express Unauthorized charges, suspected systems tampering, and failure to resolve billing errors Complainant : XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX/ XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Date XXXX XX/XX/XXXX Company complained about : American Express ( and American Express National Bank ) Card ( s ) : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX card ( Acct. ending XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ) and XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX card ( for reference ) Point of contact at AMEX ( investigation ) : AMEX Fraud Department Agent XXXX, ID # XXXX ( call on or about XX/XX/XXXX ) Summary of the complaint I am reporting continued unauthorized charges and apparent back-office manipulation on my American Express XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX account after it was paid in full as of the XX/XX/XXXX. I did not use this XXXX XXXX card after that date because I switched my spending to my XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX card. Nevertheless, charges appeared on the XXXX XXXX account in XXXX, including a large, same-day refund and adjustment of {$19000.00} and a XX/XX/XXXX final XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX absolutely did not occur before the card was unknowingly to me closed. I need AMEX to document who/what triggered that cancellation and when minimum-payment autopay was enabled on the account, neither of which I authorized or set. 

I believe AMEXs systems and/or account linkages were compromised or tampered with, and that some transactions were mis-attributed from my XXXX card to the XXXX XXXX account. I have evidence and am requesting a full investigation and correction. 

Timeline & facts XX/XX/XXXX XXXX XXXX account ( XXXX XXXX XXXX  ) paid in full. 

XXXX I stopped using the XXXX XXXX  card and used only my HXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX card for purchases. 

After which point the account reflects transactions made through out XXXX and other destinations which I unequivically know were not legitimate. The account was also somehow switched to an autopay bank draw for the minimum payment amount, while racking up large interest fees without being shown on my online account for this entire time period. Only after I noticed this autopayment deduction from my bank did I submit a complaint with AMEX, and only then did XXXX XXXX Card account show up on my online account porfolio. ( specific statements available ) My XXXX XXXX account reflects multiple unexplainable charges including A {$19000.00} charge that shows a same-day refund followed by a back-office adjustment. 
When contacting AMEX about this they claimed I wasn't supposed to see that, and they were sorry. 

At this point I stopped using all my AMEX accounts, opened internal investigations seeking to solve this situation, and to calculate how much money had been missappropriated. 

A XX/XX/XXXX charge at XXXX XXXX which is a hardware store in XXXX that did not occur was the final transaction on the account before it was closed without my knowledge autorization or notice. 

The XXXX XXXX card status indicates cancellation in XX/XX/XXXX ; I did not initiate this and request AMEXs records of how, when, and by whom the cancellation occurred. 

XXXX issues I could not see the XXXX XXXX account online for an extended period and only recently gained access after AMEX began looking into these issues. 

XX/XX/XXXX I spoke with AMEX Fraud ( Agent XXXX, ID # XXXX ) and formally disputed all XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXransactions. 

Legal issues and consumer rights implicated Truth in Lending Act / Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) and Regulation AMEX must investigate and correct billing errors and unauthorized use ; any related finance charges/fees must be removed during and after correction. They have failed to do so. 

I have been unable to adequately resolve these issues with AMEX, every time I call their fraud resolution department I am speaking with a different agent that has no authority or ability to actually solve these issues, while most of them are remote help desk agents in the located in the XXXX. 

Requests to the CFPB Please require American Express to : Recognize and treat as unauthorized every XXXX XXXX transaction after XX/XX/XXXX and reverse them, including all related interest and fees. 

Provide complete transaction-level data for the disputed items, including : Merchant IDs, processing/acquirer data, card-present/EMV flags, authorization logs, IP/device tokens XXXX and internal notes or adjustment codes so that the transactions validity can be reviewed. 

Produce the account-maintenance audit trail showing : Who/what canceled the XXXX XXXX card in XX/XX/XXXX, precise timestamps, contact channel ( phone/web/app ), and any associated verifications ; When and how minimum-payment autopay was enabled or altered ( IP/device, channel, user, and consent records ). 

Correct any credit reporting tied to the disputed balance/late payments and confirm corrections to all relevant CRAs in writing. 

Provide a written determination and full remediation within the regulatory time limits, including a clear explanation of findings regarding potential back-office/system tampering or mis-attribution between my XXXX and XXXX XXXX accounts. 


Additionally your system will not allow me to upload the files larger than XXXX which are associated with this complaint.. Please respond with a method to upload the documents of the accounts which are larger than XXXX

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Ive tried to cancel my XXXX subscription for around 4 years and they keep charging my card ( {$35.00} annually every year totalling {$140.00} ). I have disputed with my Citi credit card multiple times and they fabricate documentation ti justify charges as if Im receiving the notices and that I need to reach to them to cancel. 

As I explained to Citi and XXXX, I did not have access to my email address for my university account since I XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  ). Thus, I could not have had access to the notification for the subscription renewal only after seeing the charge on my credit card. Everytime this happened I tried disputing charges ( a clear signal I did not want this ) and they kept charging me. This current year was the final straw. 

During last years dispute, I even went ahead and told Citi to just cancel my card number, give me a new one to see the final charge from XXXX. To my amazement this year, the charge same back again. How is it possible they could continue charging my Citi card without an active credit card on file? The card if not mistaken was even expired including my drivers license.Hence, XXXX didnt even have valid informaiton of me to charge nor provide me this service subscription which they gladly kept charging for throughout the years. 

Then during the dispute, its seems like XXXX autogenerated documentation to deceptively create the impression I had received notice of the charge which is not true. The documentation clearly shows a card which is no longer valid this year yet the charge went through successfully. I requested proof I had received the email or accepted the renewal and they just closed my ticket, said they refunded the card ( which they dont have latest on file! ), nor did I hear back on my support ticket ( never receive notice the support ticket was closed ) nor did i get any money back! 

I consider XXXX practices to be clear UDAP violations ( Unfair, Deceptive, or Abusive Acts or Practices ).

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I noticed a charge of {$96.00} from the vendor XXXX XXXX XXXX which posted near the end of XXXX on my Advantage Airline credit card managed by Barclays. I have never interacted with XXXX XXXX XXXX, and in fact didn't know the company existed until the fraudulent charge appeared. I called Mastercard number on the back of my card and the gentleman suggested they issue a new card, which happened. 

On the next bill, the charge remained, so I called the company again and followed up with a letter, attached. 

The charge remained, so I called again. This time they said they would review the dispute. The woman also said " the chip was present '' and that " since the other charges that day were not disputed '' that I must have made the charge. In fact, there are no other charges that day listed. I never had any dealings with the vendor. So, to me, this charge was just added to my account without justification or evidence that I made the charge. 

After this call, my account now showed a second XXXX XXXX XXXX  charge for the same amount. I called again and was told the 2nd charge was just an accounting error, and would be removed ( it was ), but the original charge was NOT removed. 

I have not heard anything more from Barclay 's Mastercard and the charge remains. 

In summary, I called multiple times and sent the attached letter via surface mail. I also called after this letter and they again said they would review my case. In the meanwhile, I paid all but the disputed charge. However, the charge remains and I am now being asked to pay a minimum fee and interest has begun to accumulate. I have never received anything in writing. 

I appreciate your help. I have never had a credit card company refuse to remove a fraudulent charge before, so I am somewhat at a loss here. I don't know when the next charge will magically appear, so I have locked my card for the time being and paid all but the disputed charge.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
SUBJECT : FORMAL DISPUTE UNDER THE FAIR CREDIT BILLING ACT ( FCBA ) UNAUTHORIZED CHARGES To Whom It May Concern, I am writing to formally appeal the denial of my fraud claim dated XX/XX/year>. I am exercising my rights under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) and Regulation Z ( 12 CFR 1026.13 ) to dispute {$3300.00} in unauthorized charges appearing on my account.

I am disputing the banks determination that these charges are valid based on the following facts : Unauthorized Activation : On Tuesday, XX/XX/year>, my credit card was activated by an unauthorized party in Florida. I did not authorize this activation, nor was I in the state of Florida on that date.

Geographic Impossibility : The fraudulent transactions occurred between XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/year>, in Florida. During this entire period, I was physically present in New York. I have attached documentation proving my presence in New York.

Physical Possession of Card : Their " Bot '' determination that a " physical card '' was used, is proof of cloning or skimming. I have been in continuous physical possession of my legitimate card in New York at all times.

Attempted Fraud while Locked : An unauthorized online attempt at XXXX occurred while the card was locked around XX/XX/XXXX. This further proves the account was compromised by a third party.

Timely Reporting : I contacted the bank immediately on Saturday, XX/XX/year>, to report the fraud and secure the account. 

Phone call from Barclay : On XX/XX/year>, Barclays own fraud department contacted me via phone to alert me that an unauthorized individual was attempting to access my account over the phone. This proves the bank had internal knowledge that my account credentials were compromised. Despite their own records of an attempted account takeover on XX/XX/XXXX, they are erroneously holding me liable for the Florida charges from earlier that month. ''

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I purchased an XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX from Best Buy using my Best Buy Credit Card issued by Citi. Shortly after purchase, the PC experienced serious and recurring hardware failures, including motherboard-related issues that made the system unreliable and not fit for its intended use. 
I worked in good faith with the manufacturer through the warranty and XXXX process. My PC was held by XXXX  for several weeks without a technician assigned, with no clear repair timeline, and without visibility into repair status. XXXX  ultimately refused to authorize a refund or provide any durable remedy, stated that replaced components would not receive extended warranty coverage, and acknowledged that post-warranty failures would not be guaranteed coverage. 
Although the PC was eventually returned to me, the underlying defect was not permanently resolved, and the repair does not provide reasonable assurance of long-term reliability given the history of failures. 
I disputed the charge with Citi, as the issuer and servicer of my Best Buy Credit Card. Citi closed and refused to reopen my dispute solely because Best Buys 15-day return policy had expired. Citi did not properly investigate the defective merchandise or the manufacturers failure to provide a lasting remedy and instead relied on a store return policy.

This is not a case of buyers remorse or a voluntary return. It is a dispute involving defective merchandise that was not fit for ordinary use. Citis denial failed to comply with the Fair Credit Billing Act by not conducting a meaningful investigation into the defect.

I am requesting that Citi be required to reopen and properly investigate this billing dispute and provide appropriate relief for defective merchandise.

Thank you for your time and attention.

Initially, I paid to avoid ruining my credit while I disputed canceling my membership with Barclays Bank. This dispute lasted over XXXX years, and during that time, I ended up paying over {$2000.00}. Eventually, I got frustrated and quit paying because this dispute was not getting resolved. In XX/XX/XXXX, I received a summons to go to court.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
To Whom It XXXX Concern, I am writing to dispute a charge on my Barclays credit card for a rental car from Enterprise. On XX/XX/2022, I rented a XXXX from the Enterprise location in XXXX XXXX, NJ. 
I called XXXX the day the car displayed brakes needed to be checked and to stop driving. The manager said that they would give me a refund for the rental back on to my credit card, but they never did. I have called XXXX several times since then, but I have not been able to get a refund. 
I am requesting a full refund for the rental charges. I am also requesting a refund for any late fees or other charges that have been incurred. I believe that XXXX is responsible for this problem because they rented me a car that was not safe to drive. 
I have attached a copy of the summary of charges from XXXX. I have also attached a copy of the receipt for the rental. 
I would appreciate it if you would investigate this matter and issue me a full refund. I have been a customer of Barclays for many years and I have never had a problem before. I am disappointed that XXXX has not been willing to resolve this issue. 
Thank you for your time and attention to this matter.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am writing to formally lodge a complaint against American Express ( Amex ) regarding their failure to honor a request to block transactions from a specific merchant, XXXX, which has resulted in an unauthorized charge on my account. 
On XX/XX/year>, I explicitly requested that Amex block all future transactions from XXXX. I also called their customer service and was assured that my token would be reset, preventing the merchant from charging my account in the future. The representative on the call unambiguously stated that no future transactions from XXXX would be authorized or posted to my account under any circumstances. 
However, on XX/XX/year>, XXXX posted a charge of {$270.00} to my account, directly contradicting the assurances given by Amex. When I contacted Amex about this unauthorized transaction, they are now attempting to justify their failure by stating that recurring charges were not included in the block, despite having assured me otherwise.

This is a clear case of negligence, misrepresentation, and unfair financial practices on Amexs part. Not only did they fail to honor their commitment, but their misleading communication has now resulted in financial harm to me. I have taken all reasonable steps to prevent this charge, and the responsibility lies entirely with Amex for their failure to properly execute the block as promised.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Subject : Dispute of Wayfair patiocover purchases and Citibanks failure to properly investigate or resolve, despite clear evidence of nondelivery and repeated attempts to cooperate. 

Account holder : XXXX XXXX Account number : XXXX Dispute ID : XXXX / XXXX Merchant : Wayfair.com Purchase date : XX/XX/year> Dispute communications : XXXX XXXX Current status : Cardholder found liable, despite evidence and repeated submissions ; requester denied ability to provide unreasonable proof. 

XXXX ) Summary of the factual timeline XX/XX/year> : Purchased XXXX patio covers from XXXX. 

XX/XX/year> : Received email confirming delivery of XXXX of the XXXX shipments. Photo evidence showed delivery to a residence that was not mine. 

Background context : In my neighborhood, multiple residences share the same street number ; misdeliveries are common. This was relevant to why the delivery could go to the wrong house. 

Shortly after XX/XX/XXXX : Contacted Wayfair via chat to report the misdelivery and request cancellation of the other pending delivery. Wayfair required a police report alleging theft to issue a refund, despite photo evidence showing wrongaddress delivery. 

XX/XX/year> : Second delivery was made, again to a different residence, not mine. Raised to Wayfair ; they again insisted on a police report as the only avenue for refund. 

Subsequent action : Unable to falsify a police report; refused to perjure. Turned to Citibank to dispute the charges. 

XX/XX/year> : Received notice that the dispute was closed and found in my responsibility. Called Citibank immediately ; representative assured me it could be reopened and asked me not to worry or make a payment, saying additional information would be requested if needed. 

XXXX XXXX XXXX : Near payment date, called again on XX/XX/year> ; representative stated the investigation was pending and I should submit documentation. I submitted the requested documentation to the provided email address. 

XX/XX/year> : Called again to confirm receipt of documents. Told the case had been closed because I failed to submit information by XX/XX/year>, a deadline I had not been clearly informed about despite my prior inquiries. Representative promised to reopen and confirmed receipt of XXXX of XXXX files, advising that .jpg was invalid and to resubmit as .pdf. 

Resubmission : Converted the remaining images from .jpg to .pdf and sent again as instructed. 

XX/XX/year> : Received notice that I was still responsible for the charges because I failed to provide proof of cancellation, a reason that conflicted with my actual claim of nondelivery. 

XX/XX/year>, call : Spoke to a representative, then supervisor XXXX ( ID XXXX ). Told I needed a police report, mirroring Wayfairs unreasonable demand, despite photographic proof of misdelivery. Supervisors justification shifted from lack of police report to lack of proof of cancellation ; then he stated Citibank would do nothing further. 

Supervisors acknowledgment : He confirmed on the recorded line that I was being directed to file a false police report. He referred to his employee ID for record retrieval. 

XXXX ) Why Citibanks handling appears improper under federal law a ) Nature of the dispute : goods not delivered as agreed, wrong delivery location Under CFPBs official interpretation of the billing error resolution rule, disputes about goods or services not delivered as agreed include, among others, delivery to the wrong location. 

This is exactly the nature of the dispute : shipments landed at addresses that were not mine, verified by photo evidence, and resulted in nonreceipt of the purchased goods. Therefore the applicable federal rule explicitly covers this scenario. 

b ) Obligations of the creditor to investigate and respond The Fair Credit Billing Act requires a creditor, upon receiving a written notice of a billing error from the consumer, to acknowledge the notice within XXXX  days and either correct the account or provide a written explanation after investigation within XXXX billing cycles or XXXX  days, before taking collection actions. 
Legal Information Institute Moreover, when the alleged billing error involves goods not delivered as agreed, the creditor may not assume the amount is correct unless it determines the goods were actually delivered. 
Legal Information Institute In short, if a dispute involves nondelivery or wrong delivery, the creditor must investigate and can not simply rely on the merchants demand for a police report or other unreasonable proof, especially when the consumer provides evidence of wrong delivery. 

c ) Prohibition on adverse collection action prior to proper resolution The statute restricts a creditor from closing or restricting an account solely because the consumer failed to pay a disputed amount before providing the required explanation. This protects consumers during the dispute process. 
Legal Information Institute In this case, I kept the balance at XXXX and relied on Citibanks assurances and guidance ; yet the dispute was closed and I was declared responsible despite ambiguity about whether documents were timely received, contradictory instructions, and documented evidence of misdelivery. 

d ) Evidence of conflicting or unclear communications from Citibank Representatives gave inconsistent instructions : XXXX said no need to worry or submit documentation because the case could be reopened ; another advised to send information ; yet later a representative claimed deadline was missed, and a supervisor insisted on proof that was not legally required or logically connected to the disputes nature. 

This pattern indicates a failure to provide a clear, consistent investigation process and to properly evaluate evidence of wrong delivery. 

XXXX ) Goodfaith efforts by the consumer I repeatedly contacted the creditor, followed directions, resubmitted documents in the required format, and refrained from making payments while the dispute was pending based on Citibanks guidance. 

I refused to provide a police report for a claim that would be false, which was requested by the merchant and later by the creditor. The request to use perjured documentation is unreasonable, unethical, and not required by the applicable federal rules covering wrong delivery disputes. 

XXXX ) Requested relief Given the above facts and applicable federal law : Reverse the charge and remove any liability and associated finance or late charges from my account for the Wayfair patio cover purchases, because the dispute involves goods not delivered as agreed, which the creditor must investigate and can not assume correct without determining actual delivery. 

Correct any reporting or account status that reflects me as responsible for the disputed amount. 

Require Citibank to comply with its obligations to properly investigate disputes that involve delivery to the wrong location or nondelivery, and to avoid shifting unreasonable requirements onto the consumer after accepting a dispute. 

Investigate possible violations of the FCBA and Regulation Z when creditor communications are unclear, contradictory, or improperly place the burden on the consumer for evidence not required by federal rules. 

Attachments and evidence Photo evidence of deliveries to wrong addresses. 

Chat transcript with Wayfair showing refusal to accept correct evidence, and insistence on police report. 

Email records of submissions to Citibank, including file format conversions. 

Notices from Citibank regarding dispute outcomes on XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/year>. 

Closing remark I acted in good faith to resolve a clear case of nondelivery to my address, provided evidence, followed Citibanks instructions, and was repeatedly told the case would be reopened or that evidence should be provided, yet the resolution denied my dispute. The federal statutes and CFPB rules were not properly applied to a dispute that explicitly falls within their coverage. I respectfully request CFPB review and relief based on the above facts and law. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative: On XXXX I made an online purchase for XXXX. I started the having second thoughts about it, and as I was looking up the product, I got a fraud alert from the credit card company. I automatically called them and ask them to stop the payment. This was less than XXXX to XXXX minutes after I had made the purchase. dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am filing a complaint against Citibank regarding a mishandled credit card dispute involving XXXX XXXX. On XX/XX/year>, I rented a vehicle from XXXX at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Upon arrival, XXXX charged me an additional {$12.00} without explanation. I was also given the wrong vehicle, which had been assigned to another customer at the same time, causing confusion and delay. When I returned to the counter, I was issued a second vehicle that had not been cleaned and still contained personal property from the prior renter. 

On XX/XX/year>, I contacted XXXX customer service to report the issues. An XXXX representative acknowledged the inconvenience and promised a partial refund for half a day 's rental. I was told by multiple XXXX agents over several follow-up calls that a refund had been issued to my Citi card . However, no refund was ever received. 

I disputed the charge with Citibank because XXXX failed to provide the agreed refund. Citibank later closed the dispute in favor of XXXX, stating that services were provided as intended, which is factually incorrect. In addition, Citibank claimed in a letter that they had previously requested necessary information from me and never received a response. This is false. At no point did Citibank contact me to request documentation or allow me to submit my evidence. I was never given an opportunity to submit audio recordings, emails, or correspondence with XXXX proving that a refund was promised and never processed. 

I contacted Citi through live chat and phone to appeal the decision, and I asked multiple times for the dispute to be reopened. I was told the case could not be reopened and that I needed to contact the merchant directly, even though I had already attempted to resolve the matter with XXXX for over a month without success. 

I have : -Audio recordings of XXXX representatives confirming that a refund was issued. 

-Call logs and emails to XXXX following up for the missing refund. 

-Proof that no refund has ever posted to my account. 

-Proof that the dispute was closed without an investigation and without allowing me to submit my evidence. 

My concerns : Citibank did not conduct a fair dispute investigation. 

Citibank closed the dispute based solely on a booking receipt from XXXX, which does not address the fraudulent or misleading refund issue. 

I was denied my right to submit documentation, which violates dispute investigation standards. 

Citibanks letter falsely claims I failed to respond, which is not true. 

I am requesting : -That Citibank reopen the dispute. 

-That Citibank review my evidence. 

-That Citibank contact XXXX for refund verification instead of relying on a booking receipt that does not resolve the actual issue. 

-Assistance in recovering the refund XXXX promised but never provided. 

I have made every good faith effort to resolve this directly with XXXX and Citibank. Citibank has mishandled this dispute and is attempting to close the case without due process or proper investigation. I am requesting CFPB assistance. 

Case : XXXX

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative: I have asked Citi Card to refund me {$4900.00} countless times over the last few years because they awarded a contractor {$4900.00} of my money for a service that was never provided to me. I have attached 40 pages of chronological events that took place that has extended over the last few years with Citi card. Initially I was given a temporary credit from Citi Card for about a 6- week time frame, but then they reversed their decision based on a hand type note from the contractor and some pictures the contractor took off the internet ; these are not pictures of my house. I refuted the claim, but they refused to credit me my money back. When the decision was made, I asked them what I needed to do to get a refund, and they said I needed some form of legal decision. I then promptly took the contractor to court and over a two-year time frame ( court process is slow ) I was able to get a decision stating the contractor was at fault and never entitled to the {$4900.00}. After I got the decision, I sent this to Citi Card and they said they will look into it. I started these calls in XX/XX/year>. I have spoken to at least 10 different dispute center representatives over the last 6 months. Every time I was told, we are working in getting your credit returned, it should not be much longer. After getting the run around, I asked what I needed to do and filed another complaint against XXXX XXXX as they had suggested. So I sent them all this correspondence again and finally received a letter form stating that too much time has expired. Citi Card statement is " Citi is at your side, before, during, and after every purchase ''. I don't think so. They basically misled me and found an easy way out when the contractor refused to credit them the {$4900.00}, so they figured a good customer would pay the bill which is exactly what transpired. The Contractor in question is now in prison and has no means to pay the court hearing decision. If Citi Card would have credited me the money in the first place and not taken the side of a criminal none of this would have transpired. {$4900.00} means a lot more to me than a multi-billion dollar credit card company, not to mention all the lawyer fees I have incurred. I want my refund from Citi Card.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative: XXXX charge {$140.00} Amex acct XXXX XXXX Case # Amex XXXX I received a credit of {$98.00} for the above purchase of airline ticket I submitted 2 NOT FIT TO FLY certificates from doctor and hospital indicating medical reasons as to no travelling XX/XX/XXXX - mid XXXX due to ongoing XXXX XXXX  at hospital XXXX XXXXXXXX reimbursed me FULL $ refund under # XXXX booking XXXX XXXX XXXX This was one way going on XX/XX/XXXX I had a return ticket bought on American Express XXXX XX/XX/XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX for {$140.00} Why was I given partial refund of {$98.00} and not {$140.00} I wish to dispute the remaining {$47.00} charge and want a refund of this amount I do not know why inconsistency and as a travel platform they should be my advocate with airline for full refund

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
An airline ticket was purchased using my American Express Delta Skymiles card ( confirmation code XXXX ), also the insurance box was checked to further protect if I had to cancel the flight. ( Scheduled for XX/XX/XXXX ) I canceled the airline ticket because I did not have to physically appear in court. Delta Airlines assured a refund to my credit card would happen. The credit for the {$530.00} airline ticket did not happen and I began disputing the charge when I noticed that it had not been removed from my credit card on or about XX/XX/XXXX. The charge was removed from my account and within a few weeks or less, the charge appeared again on my account. Called again to American Express to form another dispute, ( XXXX ) advised that it would be taken care of and not to worry. ( Continual process for over 4 times ) Tried to use my other American Express Card Hilton Honors, while on vacation in XXXX of XXXX for my Hilton honor points, and was told card declined, and account closed. Called American Express and was told that I disputed the charge " to late '' and that I would have had to dispute the account within a certain " timeframe ''. Advised the representative that I have continually been disputing this charge and was told by the representative that the charge was valid. I advised that I was told that there would be a credit issued because I canceled the flight and that credit has never happened. Contacted Delta airlines, and they stated they could not locate the flight information ( Confirmation XXXX ) despite the fact that I provided the confirmation number. Then was told if I had insurance, I would have received something for " XXXX ''. 

I was charged for a flight that I canceled, never issued a credit, or even so much as a voucher to use at a later date. Now, the American Express Hilton Honors credit card that had a {$0.00} balance is closed. The American Express Delta Skymiles Credit card, as of XX/XX/XXXX has a balance of {$850.00} from all of the late fees and interest fees American Express keeps charging me for a charge that was never refunded despite the continual disputes. Now, I keep getting text messages, stating " Your AMEX payment is due, '' " Keep your AMEX account from going further past due and avoid further negative impact to credit score '' " Pay {$40.00} to keep your account from becoming further delinquent. '' Now, my credit report is damaged due to American Express actions, and I have contacted and formed complaints with the credit reporting agencies. Now, I'm beginning my complaints with the CFPB.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am filing this complaint because American Express has repeatedly refused to properly investigate my billing dispute for a {$36000.00} charge to XXXX XXXX  for goods that were materially misrepresented. 

Transaction details : - Merchant : XXXX XXXX  - Date : XX/XX/year> - Amex Dispute Reference : XXXX Key facts : I attended an auction advertised as selling government seized property. Law enforcement later confirmed this claim was false advertising. The merchant also materially misrepresented the goods sold : - Jewelry sold as 18K gold was actually 14K gold ( certified appraisal ). 
- Diamonds sold as VS clarity were actually SI1SI2. 
- Tourmaline described as watermelon was standard bi-color. 
- Artwork described as original lithographs were inexpensive prints. 
- These discrepancies were confirmed by certified appraisals. 

I submitted extensive evidence to American Express including : - Certified appraisals proving misrepresentation - A law enforcement letter refuting the government seized advertising - Proof the auctioneer permanently surrendered his XXXX XXXX license for false advertising - A formal legal summary letter American Expresss handling : Amex originally denied my dispute citing only the merchants all sales final policycompletely ignoring the misrepresentation evidence. Under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ), all sales final terms do not excuse billing errors for goods not as described.

Their own Cardmember Agreement states billing errors include charges for goods and services you did not accept or that werent delivered as agreed. 

When I called to escalate, Amex agents claimed they cant investigate fraud or misrepresentation because they are just an intermediary, directing me to law enforcement. This is a misapplication of the FCBA, which requires card issuers to resolve disputes about goods not as described. 

After repeated escalation, Amex has now confirmed they are reopening the dispute under Not as Described, which is the correct classification.

I am filing this complaint to ensure CFPB oversight, as I want to ensure Amex fully and fairly investigates all evidence this time and complies with its obligations under the Fair Credit Billing Act.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Product : Credit card Company : American Express National Bank American Express improperly denied my dispute involving a charge obtained through merchant coercion and payment under duress. 

I disputed an {$8800.00} charge from XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX, MA ). My commercial vehicle was being detained by the merchant, who refused to release it unless I paid immediately while fees escalated daily.

The merchant initially refused credit card payment and demanded cash, despite advertising acceptance of credit cards. At the moment of payment, the merchant imposed new handwritten terms and added fees that were not part of the original invoice and conditioned release of my vehicle on signing those terms and surrendering my drivers license and credit card for copying.

I served the merchant with a written Payment Under Protest notice at the time of payment, expressly stating that payment was made solely to mitigate damages and secure release of my vehicle, not as consent to the charges.

American Express denied my dispute by treating the transaction as a voluntary, consented purchase and failed to evaluate it as a merchant-conduct and duress case. This ignores evidence of coercive billing practices and improper card acceptance conditions.

I am requesting CFPB assistance to require American Express to properly investigate this dispute under merchant-misconduct standards and protect my rights as a cardholder.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement

Now they are not resolving my issue and its not fair with me to pay for something that I did not authorize.Total amount is around {$2000.00}.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
On XX/XX/year>, my wife purchased a meal at XXXX XXXX XXXX for approximately XXXX. Instead, our credit card was charged nearly XXXX. 

I contacted XXXX XXXX XXXX, and they investigated the transaction. In writing, they admitted that the charge was entered incorrectly on their side. Despite acknowledging the error, they have not issued any refund. 

I then disputed the transaction with Citi. Under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ), I am entitled to dispute charges for goods/services not received as agreed or billed incorrectly. Despite providing documentation including XXXX XXXX own admission of error Citi denied my chargeback request and refused to issue a refund. 

I am therefore seeking CFPB assistance to enforce my rights under consumer protection law. Citi should have reversed this incorrect charge.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Dear CFPB representatives, I am writing to bring to your attention an issue I have encountered with City XXXX  and City regarding a misleading booking that was not resolved to my satisfaction. The booking was made in XXXX for a hotel in XXXX, XXXX, costing approximately {$1200.00} per night. The property was advertised with amenities such as Wi-Fi in all rooms, a bar, and a spa, none of which were available during my stay. Additionally, the Wi-Fi service was unreliable, and the establishment was more akin to an Airbnb, contrary to its advertised description as a hotel. Wifi only worked when you were standing by the door at desk. 

Despite expressing my dissatisfaction both during and after my stay, and providing evidence of the lack of amenities, and screenshots of the booking reservation showing the provided amenities, after 100+ emails, City XXXX offered only XXXX loyalty points as compensation. I find this insufficient given the nature of the complaint and the premium nature of the booking. I subsequently requested a refund equating to a third of the booking value, which was denied. 

After escalating the matter, I was informed by City that the booking is nonrefundable, effectively concluding the dispute. I believe this violates consumer protection principles as the services were misrepresented. Moreover, Citys administration of the dispute with City XXXX presents a conflict of interest, compromising the objectivity of the resolution process. 

I am particularly concerned that Citys policies may not align with the Fair Credit Billing Act and other consumer protection laws. This situation has caused me significant inconvenience and confusion due to the ineffective support and lack of clear, consumer-centric resolution procedures. 

I kindly ask for your guidance and intervention to ensure that my complaint is fairly addressed and that appropriate measures are taken to prevent such occurrences from affecting other consumers. Please let me know if any further information is required to support this inquiry. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

I signed up for the XXXX service. When the charge hits the credit card, it shows as " XXXX '' from XXXX. When I called American Express about the issue, the representative told me to " call XXXX. '' This is their issue, not mine. They should rectify their billing issues, not me.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Dear Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, I am writing to formally request your assistance in resolving a dispute I have with Wayfair regarding a defective mattress purchased in XXXX. The mattress was intended for my XXXX daughter, who only began sleeping on it independently this summer. After a few months of use, we discovered that the mattress has sunken in the middle and is no longer usable. 

Initially, Wayfair accepted my dispute and asked for supporting documentation within 72 hours. However, before I had a chance to gather and submit the necessary documentation, they denied my claim due to the length of time since the purchase. This was despite the fact that the product was unused for nearly 2.5 years, and the defect only became apparent when my daughter started using it. 

The mattress was purchased during the COVID-19 pandemic, and it seems the product was poorly made. I believe I am entitled to a refund or replacement for a defective product, regardless of the time frame, especially given that it was unused for such an extended period. I would appreciate your help in disputing this charge and ensuring that Wayfair addresses the defect in the product they sold. 

Thank you for your time and assistance in this matter. 

Sincerely, XXXX

I have contacted them several times to get this resolved but all they tell me is to wait 30 days and they will have it fixed. It never gets fixed. I disputed the credit bureaus and received letters from Barclay then stating it was being reported correctly and would not be taken off my report. I did not make these charges ; I live in Tennessee and these charges are in Indiana. I was going to hire an attorney but was told to contact you first. Thank you for your help.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
In XX/XX/XXXX, while I was in XXXX XXXX on an extended stay from XXXX to XXXX, an unauthorized fraudulent charge of approximately {$77.00} was made against a Wayfair XXXX for which I had applied in XXXX. I had applied for the card in order to get a discount while shopping on the Wayfair website, but never received the physical card and never made a charge against the account. 

Wayfair Citi XXXX contacted me in XX/XX/XXXX about a past due balance for a {$77.00} charge made in a sporting goods store somewhere in the United States. I told the agent that the {$77.00} charge was fraudulent. Because the card was compromised and the fact I had not received the card, I closed the account at that time. An investigation was conducted and a credit for the full amount was subsequently issued. I considered the matter closed. 

The charge reappeared a couple months later and I am now being billed for the original amount of the fraudulent charge plus exorbitant penalties and interest. I have spoken numerous times with Wayfair Citi XXXX billing agents and XXXX times with agents in the fraud investigation group to get the charge removed. Despite their promises to remove the charge and to correct the erroneous past due amounts on my credit reports, there have been no results and my XXXX XXXX has tanked from XXXX to the low-mid XXXX. I have written to several Citi agencies as well and gotten no response. 

I subsequently filed disputes with all XXXX major credit reporting agencies, but they responded that Wayfair Citi XXXX told them the charges are accurate. I am now at a loss on how to get my credit report and XXXX XXXX corrected.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
What happened? On XX/XX/XXXX, my travel plans were significantly disrupted due to the cancellation of my flights by the airline. The tickets were for a flight that was scheduled for XXXX XXXX The total cost of the XXXX tickets was {$2100.00}. Following the cancellation, I received a partial refund of XXXX for each ticket ( approximately {$650.00} for each ticket ) from the airline, but the remaining balance of $ XXXX ( {$880.00} ) was not refunded. 

I initiated XXXX disputes with Citibank for the remaining {$440.00} ( XXXX case for each ticket ) : Case XXXX Case XXXX Actions Taken : Both disputes were submitted to Citibank around the same time, detailing the same transaction for the same amount. 

Resolution of Case XXXX : Citibank initially resolved XXXX XXXX in my favor, and a credit of {$440.00} was applied to my account. They didn't resolve XXXX, so I sent correspondence to have them resolve it. 

Denial of Case XXXX : On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a letter from Citibank stating that Case XXXX was denied due to the amount of time that had passed. 

Appeal and Follow-up : I requested an appeal and sent multiple follow-up communications to Citibank expressing my concerns about the inconsistency in handling these identical cases. 

Reversal of Credit : After requesting an appeal, Citibank inexplicably reversed the resolution of Case XXXX, removing the {$440.00} credit from my account. This happened after I emailed them on XX/XX/XXXX when I asked them re-review the situation. 

Email Communications : On XX/XX/XXXX, I sent an email to Citibank requesting an evaluation of Case XXXX. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I sent an email expressing my frustration with the inconsistent handling of my disputes and requesting a review by a supervisor. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I sent another email demanding immediate resolution and reinstatement of the credit, and escalating the matter to a higher authority. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a letter from Citibank, which included my account statements and an audit worksheet but did not address the core issues or explain the inconsistency in their handling of the disputes. 

Current Situation : Despite my efforts and follow-up, Citibank has not provided a satisfactory resolution. The inconsistent handling of identical disputes has caused significant frustration and inconvenience. 

Requested Resolution : I am seeking the CFPBs assistance in resolving this matter.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Barclays XX/XX/XXXX response raises new billing-error and proof issues that were not resolved in its prior CFPB responses.

This complaint is limited to the XXXX XXXX account Barclays identifies as ending in XXXX and the balance Barclays continues to claim on that account. 

Barclays XX/XX/XXXX response is internally inconsistent and does not provide the documentary basis required to support the balance it says is valid. 

First, Barclays states that my XX/XX/XXXX billing statement reflected two XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX transactions on account ending in XXXX. However, the XX/XX/XXXX statement Barclays provided shows those transactions on card ending XXXX, not XXXX. The later XX/XX/XXXX statement on account ending XXXX still shows the disputed foreign purchase activity under card ending XXXX. Barclays has never provided the replacement-card or reissue history necessary to connect those card endings and prove the balance chain.

Second, Barclays XX/XX/XXXX response states that the XXXX XXXX foreign transactions were credited back on XX/XX/XXXX, while also stating that those same transactions were posted and credited back in the same billing cycle from XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX. Those two statements can not both be correct. This internal contradiction alone shows that Barclays has not provided a reliable account-level reconstruction.

Third, Barclays now says that no payments posted during the XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX billing cycle because three payments were returned : one for CUSTOMER ADVISES NOT AUTHORIZED and two for INSUFFICIENT FUNDS. Barclays also imposed a returned payment fee and later continued adding late fees and interest. I specifically dispute Barclays treatment of those reversals and all fees and interest that flowed from them unless Barclays can produce the documentary basis for each reversal.

The disputed items include : The XX/XX/XXXX payment reversal of {$6900.00} labeled CUSTOMER ADVISES NOT AUTHORIZED. 
The XX/XX/XXXX payment reversal of {$7400.00} labeled INSUFFICIENT FUNDS. 
The XX/XX/XXXX payment reversal of {$6600.00} labeled INSUFFICIENT FUNDS. 
The XX/XX/XXXX returned payment fee. 
All later late fees and interest that Barclays assessed on the balance after those reversals. 
Barclays conclusion that the XX/XX/XXXX XXXX XXXX transactions are established as valid, despite Barclays refusing to provide the alleged call recordings, transcripts, agent notes, or documentary evidence of authorization. 

This is a billing-error dispute under 15 U.S.C. 1666 and 12 C.F.R. 1026.13. Under those provisions, a billing error includes a failure to properly credit a payment, an accounting error, and a request for additional clarification including documentary evidence. If a creditor concludes that no billing error occurred, it must provide a written explanation after a reasonable investigation and, upon request, furnish documentary evidence of the consumers indebtedness. Barclays has not done that here.

This also implicates 15 U.S.C. 1643 ( b ), because Barclays and its law firm are attempting to enforce liability while Barclays still has not produced competent evidence showing that the disputed use was authorized or that the remaining balance was calculated correctly.

Barclays refusal to provide call recordings, transcripts, internal notes, payment trace information, bank return codes, and a line-by-line balance reconstruction is not a meaningful investigation. Instead, Barclays is asking me to accept conclusory statements such as the transactions remained valid and the balance remains valid, while at the same time refusing the very records needed to test those conclusions.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am filing this complaint regarding Citi 's repeated denial of my billing dispute ( Case XXXX ) for a charge of {$790.00} from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. Citi 's denial letter dated XX/XX/year> appears to rely primarily on the merchant 's refusal to issue a credit rather than evaluating the substance of my dispute. I have followed up multiple times on XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, and XX/XX/year> with no meaningful response or reconsideration. 

My dispute has two distinct grounds : 1. Forged / Unrecognized Signature on Merchant Documentation The merchant submitted paperwork to Citi that either does not contain my signature, or contains a signature I do not recognize as mine and that was never presented to me at the time of purchase. This documentation was not shown to me during the transaction and appears to have been fabricated or altered after the fact. I explicitly notified Citi of this in writing and offered to provide a signature sample for handwriting comparison. Citi did not pursue this. 

2. Material Misrepresentation and Deceptive Sales Practices The purchase was made in a high-pressure, tourist-facing retail environment. The merchant made verbal representations about the quality and authenticity of XXXX XXXX  that were material to my decision to purchase. The documentation provided contains no verifiable information about fiber composition, authenticity standards, or quality certification. Comparable XXXX products are widely available online for {$3000.00}. I was charged {$790.00}. 

Citi has not engaged with either of these arguments. Under the Fair Credit Billing Act and Visa dispute rules, authorization of a charge does not override material misrepresentation, nor does it validate documentation that the cardholder did not sign or agree to. The merchant 's refusal to issue a refund is not a valid basis to close a dispute Citi has an independent obligation to investigate under the FCBA. 

I am requesting that the CFPB require Citi to conduct a proper review of both the signature authenticity issue and the misrepresentation claim, and reverse this charge.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement

Shortly thereafter, I began to receive chargebacks of every single transfer, the oldest of which was 60 days old. I immediately contacted my banks, as well as XXXX, in order to inform them of this fraud. I also called XXXX XXXX, as most of the transfers originated from them. I was eventually able to get in touch with a gentleman named XXXX in their fraud department, who told me that there were several accounts from the same account holder that had been closed due to identity theft, and that an external investigation had already been started. He would give me no further detail. The position that XXXX, Citi, and XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX took was that I needed to work this out with XXXX, and that as far as they were concerned, I owed them the money. This has left my credit score in shambles, caused me extreme stress, and will likely force me into XXXX. I have kept every email, receipt, and letter involved in this matter and offered it to XXXX and they seemed disinterested.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
On XX/XX/XXXX, a charge in the amount of {$67.00} was posted to my account. This transaction was the result of a car break-in and the theft of my daughter 's wallet. It was reported to Citibank as unauthorized within approximately four hours of occurrence, and the account was promptly closed to prevent further misuse. The theft was reported to the XXXX Police and report # XXXX was opened. 

Citi denied my claim simply stating " Customer Responsible ''. 

Under the Fair Credit Billing Act and Regulation Z, I am entitled to : 1. A reasonable investigation of disputed charges, and 2. A written explanation of the creditors findings. 

Citis failure to provide such an explanation raises serious concerns regarding compliance with these obligations. 

I contacted Citibank again and asked them to re-open the case and provide justification for their denial. On XX/XX/XXXX I received another letter simply stating that Citibank " We are looking into your dispute ''. That was the last time that I heard from Citibank regarding this dispute. I have never received any further response and the case no longer shows as open on your website. 

I contacted CitiBank again on XX/XX/XXXX, once again asking them to reverse the XXXX charge. After several days they posted again that they were investigating my complaint but they referenced a comppletely different problem and did not show my issue as open. It appears that once again, nothing will be done about it. I need your help please. 

I am requesting the following : A complete written explanation of the investigation conducted, including the evidence relied upon in denying the claim Immediate reconsideration of the dispute Immediate reversal of the {$67.00} charge if Citi can not substantiate that the transaction was authorized For over a year, 1. Citibank failed to provide any meaningful written explanation for the denial. 
2. Citibank did not provide documentation showing why the transaction was considered authorized.

3. Citibank did not explain what evidence was reviewed.

4. Citibank has continued to hold me responsible for the fraudulent charge.

Under the Fair Credit Billing Act, consumers are entitled to a reasonable investigation and an explanation of the creditors findings when a billing dispute is denied. Citibank has failed to provide that explanation despite repeated requests.

The amount involved is small ( {$67.00} ), but the issue is important because consumers should not be held responsible for fraudulent transactions that were promptly reported, especially after the account was immediately closed due to suspected theft.

narrative:

I just received my statement yesterday on XX/XX/XXXX. The credit has been reversed again!
