dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I used a Citi personal credit card to purchase a new coffee machine online, from XXXX. The coffee machine that I received would not power on, so as soon as I discovered that, I filed a service request with XXXX. They sent their own service technician who confirmed that the machine is defective. I declined repair, because I didn't pay to buy a defective machine- I expected to get a brand new product that worked. My request to XXXX to replace the product was denied. So I disputed the charge with my credit card company, Citicards ( or Citibank ). Citicards has determined that I am responsible for the charge. They requested no clarification from me after I submitted the initial dispute with supporting documents for evidence, have provided no call back number or email to get in touch with them. I have no visibility into how they determined that I must be responsible, as they have provided no reasoning to back their determination. 

The precise details, dates, etc of the dispute that I filed are as follows. " On XX/XX/XXXX I purchased online from XXXX a countertop coffee machine. It was delivered on XX/XX/XXXX. On or around XX/XX/XXXX I plugged in the appliance for the first time, but it would not turn on. I submitted a warranty service claim with XXXX. On XXXX, XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX came, examined the machine and confirmed that it would not turn on. I refused repair, since my purchase contract was for a new appliance that works. I requested replacement on the same day, however XXXX 's employee XXXX XXXX responded on XX/XX/XXXX that the request is denied and that the appliance must be serviced. I find this unfair - I paid for a new appliance and it is normal and understood that the appliance that is sold will work as advertised. '' In addition, I also filed complaints against XXXX with the California XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX and XXXX, because I live in California and the machine was delivered there, as well as New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs, because XXXX XXXX is incorporated in New Jersey. The CA division has notified me that this is not the type of complaints that they handle. The NJ division has delegated the complaint to XXXX County Office of Consumer Affairs in XXXX, NJ, as they have jurisdiction.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
My biggest concern is American Express uses non-U.S. citizens to determine dispute status. These employees of American Express do not speak, read or fully understand English. How could a bank representative be expected to make a decision in accordance to Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) : Merchandise/Services Not Received regulation if they do not understand it. 

As an American Express cardholder I am appalled at the notion of an American Express employee making a decision that a charge for which the goods remain in XXXX, not shipped, or delivered to the cardholder in the United States should be forced to pay. 

What reasonable explanation could American Express provide for such actions. 

XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, Florida XXXX XXXX American Express XXXX XXXX XXXX New York, NY XXXX Ref : XXXX Transaction Amount : {$1000.00} Transaction Date : XX/XX/year> Dear XXXX XXXX XXXX For the disputed transaction as listed above. The crucial point is that delivery to a shipping agent doesn't automatically mean delivery to the cardholder. The cardholder is the ultimate consumer and the one who must receive the merchandise to fulfill the merchant 's delivery obligation.

When a cardholder initiates a dispute based on non-receipt, the merchant 's claim of delivery to the shipping agent does not resolve the issue. The cardholder 's non-receipt remains the central point of the dispute, and the cardholder retains the right to pursue a chargeback if the merchant doesn't resolve the matter satisfactorily. 
While the merchant may have delivered to the shipping agent, that does not guarantee delivery to the cardholder, and the cardholder is disputing based on their personal non-receipt.

The FCBA provides consumers with protections against billing errors, including being billed for merchandise not received.

The FCBA allows cardholders to dispute charges for merchandise that was not delivered, regardless of whether it was received by a shipping agent or another third party. 
The cardholder 's dispute is based on the merchant 's failure to fulfill their part of the transaction by delivering the merchandise to the cardholder. 
Delivery to a shipping agent is a step in the delivery process, but it doesn't absolve the merchant of their responsibility to ensure the merchandise reaches the intended recipient and lack of delivery confirmation to the cardholder 's address is a valid reason to deny a merchant its ability to force a cardholder to pay for goods not received as lack of the cardholder 's signature on any delivery receipt is a requirement. 
The merchandise was delivered to the shipping agent, not the cardholder, and therefore, the merchant 's obligation was not fulfilled. 
The specific chargeback is related to " Merchandise/Services Not Received '' ( e.g., Reason code C08 ). This reason code clearly indicates the basis of the cardholder 's dispute, is that the merchandise was not received despite the transaction being processed.

A cardholder has the right to dispute a charge when merchandise is delivered to a shipping agent but not received by the cardholder because of " Merchandise/Services Not Received '' claim under the protections of the Fair Credit Billing Act with evidence of their non-receipt. The merchant 's evidence of delivery to the shipping agent doesn't negate the cardholder 's dispute, as the cardholder remains the intended recipient. 

Respectfully,

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
CFPB COMPLAINT / FORMAL DISPUTE ESCALATION Company : American Express National Bank Consumer : XXXX XXXX : XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX XXXX XXXX, AR XXXX Accounts : XXXX XXXX XXXX ( ending XXXX / XXXX ) ; Business Gold ( ending XXXX ) Issue : Regulation Z Billing Error Investigation and Procedural Compliance COMPLAINT NARRATIVE This complaint concerns American Express 's repeated closure of documented billing disputes based solely on a " XXXX '' timing assertion, without addressing certified written notices, merchant coding irregularities, or previously established knowledge of the disputed activity. 

The issue presented is not whether a charge was processed through the payment network, but whether American Express complied with its investigation obligations under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ) and Regulation Z ( 12 C.F.R. 1026.13 ).

American Express has issued multiple form letters stating : " Because this dispute was reported more than 60 days after your statement date, we weren't able to investigate it. '' This statement mischaracterizes the regulatory framework.

I. THE 60-DAY PROVISION DOES NOT ELIMINATE INVESTIGATIVE AUTHORITY 12 C.F.R. 1026.13 ( b ) ( 1 ) defines when certain statutory protections are automatically triggered upon timely written notice. It does not prohibit investigation outside 60 days, nor does it remove creditor authority to evaluate billing errors. 


The repeated use of a categorical " unable to investigate '' position suggests a procedural policy rather than an individualized review of the dispute. 
The complaint requests CFPB review of whether American Express is : Treating the XXXX provision as a jurisdictional bar, Or using it to decline review despite documented written notice and knowledge.

II. EARLY KNOWLEDGE AND REPEATED WRITTEN NOTICE American Express received written notice of these disputed transactions in early XX/XX/year>. Additional certified correspondence followed. 

Once a creditor receives notice of a potential billing error, Regulation Z requires a reasonable investigation and written explanation of findings ( 1026.13 ( f ) ).

Despite this knowledge trigger, subsequent dispute closures in XX/XX/year> relied solely on the XXXX language without addressing : The certified mail receipts, The evidence submitted, Or the timeline of prior notice. 

The concern is whether internal records reflecting earlier notice were considered before closing the disputes. 

III. MCC MISCODING AND STATEMENT ACCURACY The merchants involved ( XXXX, XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ) operate as sweepstakes-gaming platforms. The transactions were processed under merchant category codes ( MCCs ) that did not reflect the restricted MCC 7995 typically associated with gambling activity.

Improper MCC coding and obscured trade names may constitute billing errors under : 12 C.F.R. 1026.13 ( a ) ( 3 ) ( incorrect amount or description ) 12 C.F.R. 1026.13 ( a ) ( 6 ) ( statement inaccuracies ) These issues are independent of whether an authorization code was generated. The periodic statement must accurately reflect the nature of the transaction. 

The complaint requests review of whether American Express evaluated the coding discrepancy as part of its investigation.

IV. FAILURE TO ADDRESS CERTIFIED MAIL NOTICE Formal written notices were transmitted via certified mail consistent with FCBA requirements. American Express 's dispute-closure letters do not reference : The certified mail tracking, The dates of delivery, Or the contents of the written submissions. 

Section 1026.13 ( f ) requires a written explanation of findings after investigation. A generic reference to " 60 days '' does not address the substance of a written billing error notice. The concern is whether the investigation obligation was bypassed rather than fulfilled.

V. DIGITAL WALLET PROVISIONING IS NOT DISPOSITIVE If reliance is placed on Apple Pay provisioning or the presence of an authorization code, that information alone does not establish that : The statement was accurate, The transaction description was proper, Or that no billing error exists. 

Provisioning demonstrates device enrollment - not necessarily accurate merchant classification or legal authority of the underlying charge. 

VI. INCONSISTENT APPLICATION OF THE 60-DAY POSITION American Express has , in prior instances, investigated disputes submitted beyond XXXX days. If the XXXX window is discretionary in practice, it should not function as a categorical bar in this instance. This raises a compliance consistency concern appropriate for supervisory review.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Summary of the XXXX There are XXXX ( XXXX ) unauthorized transactions that occurred within minutes of each other while I was traveling in XXXX, XXXX. These transactions are clearly related and should be cross-referenced as part of the same fraud case. 

Important : I authorized only XXXX legitimate purchase for {$250.00}. 

I did not authorize the additional XXXX transactions. 

My signature on the disputed receipts was forged. 

Detailed Narrative Timeline of the Day On the day of the incident, my husband and I were visiting XXXX, XXXX while on a cruise stop. While walking through the shopping area, we were approached by staff at a store called XXXX and invited inside to look at skincare products. Inside the store, we were assisted by a man who identified himself as XXXX. He represented himself as a dermatologist from XXXX, Florida who travels to locations such as the XXXX and XXXX to teach customers how to use the products. He also stated that the company worked with American Express to offer payment plans, which I have since learned was false. 

After a sales demonstration, my husband decided to purchase XXXX cream priced at {$250.00}. I handed my American Express card to the salesperson for this single purchase. The salesperson left for an extended period to process the transaction. When he returned, he presented a receipt showing {$250.00}, which I reviewed and signed. I specifically requested a copy of the receipt at that time, but he refused to provide a printed copy and stated it would be emailed to me. 

We then left the store and returned to our cruise ship. Later that evening, after we had already set sail, I received a fraud alert from American Express for a charge of approximately {$3500.00}. I immediately responded No to indicate the charge was unauthorized. At that time, I believed the alert had successfully stopped the transaction. 

The following day, upon reviewing my account more closely, I discovered that XXXX separate transactions had been processed in rapid succession totaling {$20000.00}, including the {$3500.00} charge. I did not authorize these charges, I did not sign for them, and I did not provide my card again after the single {$250.00} purchase. 

Timeline of Events While on vacation in XXXX, my husband and I entered a store called XXXX. 

A salesperson identifying himself as XXXX ( claiming to be a dermatologist from XXXX, FL ) assisted us. 

We agreed to purchase XXXX product for {$250.00}. 

I signed only a receipt for {$250.00}. 

The salesperson refused to provide a printed receipt and said it would be emailed. 

Later that evening ( after returning to the cruise ship ) : I received an American Express fraud alert for approximately {$3500.00}. 

I immediately responded No to indicate the charge was unauthorized. 

Upon reviewing my account the next day, I discovered XXXX total transactions had been posted in rapid succession. 

Why These Charges Are Fraudulent I authorized only XXXX {$250.00} purchase. 

The additional receipts provided by the merchant contain forged signatures. 

The merchants contact numbers do not connect. 

The salesperson made false claims about : Being aXX/XX/XXXX Offering American Express payment plans Pattern of Organized Scam : During my investigation, I discovered substantial evidence that this appears to be an ongoing scam targeting cruise passengers in XXXX and XXXX. In addition to the public consumer reports previously provided, I identified references indicating that an independent investigator has been engaged by affected parties and has been actively examining this individual and related businesses since at least 2024. The materials I located suggest the investigation is ongoing and focused on repeated allegations of misrepresentation, forged receipts, and unauthorized credit card charges tied to the same sales persona ( XXXX ) and associated storefront operations. 

While I understand American Express will conduct its own independent review, I am providing this information to demonstrate that my experience is consistent with a documented pattern reported by multiple unrelated consumers. I attached the relevant screenshots and links supporting these findings to the email I sent to American Express. 

Key details : Individual uses the name XXXX Phone number associated : ( XXXX ) XXXX Business name involved that we saw in-person : XXXX The fraudulent merchant aliases used for the charges : - Forever Young Non-Invasive Therapy - Pharmaciopy Skin Care Similar incidents have been documented publicly Supporting reports : https : XXXX https : XXXX https : XXXX? XXXX https : XXXX? XXXX https : XXXX? XXXX https : XXXX? XXXX Prior Contact with American Express I reported these charges immediately while they were still pending and have called repeatedly since the incident. 

However, the disputes continue to be reversed after the merchant submits receipts that I maintain are fraudulent and forged. 

Requested Resolution I respectfully request that American Express : Investigate these XXXX transactions as a single coordinated fraud case Permanently remove the unauthorized charges from my account Flag this merchant for potential fraudulent activity affecting cardmembers Provide written confirmation of the investigation outcome Fraud Detection and Alert Concerns I am also requesting a careful review of the authorization and fraud-monitoring process in this case. The disputed transactions represent highly unusual activity for my account profile, including : Multiple high-dollar charges in rapid succession Charges originating overseas in XXXX Transaction amounts far exceeding my single authorized {$250.00} purchase Notably, I did receive an American Express fraud alert for approximately {$3500.00}, and I promptly responded No to indicate the charge was unauthorized. Despite this explicit denial, the charge was ultimately posted to my account. 

Given my long tenure as a cardmember and my established spending patterns, I respectfully request clarification on : Why this cluster of transactions was not fully blocked as suspected fraud? 

Why the transaction I explicitly declined via the fraud alert was still permitted to post? 

Whether the merchants submissions were appropriately scrutinized given the timing and location mismatch?

American Express letter dated XX/XX/year>, denying my dispute but acknowledging the investigation.

The repeated denials, lack of resolution, and inadequate consideration of my documentation have caused significant stress and emotional burden. Please reference my previous complaint ( XXXX XXXX. XXXX ) for all prior documentation, including screenshots, communications, and supporting evidence.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am writing today to provide a detailed series of events that lead me to argue that XXXX XXXX is designed to fleece their clients. 
In XX/XX/year>, I was sponsored by my current brokerage company, XXXX. Shortly after, I started to get bombarded with companies that claim they can set appointments with potential clients. Most of them, I just ignored. 

XXXX XXXX sent me an invitation to attend a one-on-one zoom meeting. I reluctantly accepted ; they provided me with all the links I needed to find out more about their program. XXXX Website, rerecorded presentation with paid testimony and such. I was impressed if they could do what they promise, I would be able to stay busy talking to potential clients. One point they were pushing both on their website and in conversations was their guarantee. It was repeated that they offer a four-month guarantee to produce XXXX appointments. If they didnt, they stated they would work for free until they did. 
I became concerned and requested a meeting with my account representative towards the middle of the fourth month. At that point I had only received around XXXX appointments and about half did not meet with the qualifications that we set up together. I started asking the representative XXXX XXXX ) why we were not getting what was told I would be getting and asked how the guarantee works. He started getting nervous and said they dont really offer a guarantee. But reiterated that he would do everything to make sure I would get the appointments they ( XXXX  XXXX and every rep I spoke with during the sales process ) said I would get. He said he would check into the guarantee with management. A few days later, I was told that XXXX was terminated along with his promise to make it right. 

At this point I was assigned to XXXX, whom I discussed my concerns with, and he reassured me that he would make things happen but went on to say they dont offer that guarantee. He stated that he would stop the charge for {$900.00} ( ad Spend ) and the {$400.00} service fee. He said the guarantee was nowhere in the agreement. I asked for a copy that I could see, and he sent it to me. 
You see, they sent me an agreement to my email and pressured me to e-sign or lose the promotional rate. They were good at selling me on this service and earned my trust. I felt that if they offer the guarantee of XXXX appointments, I was sure I could do a mortgage for at least XXXX, which would have paid for the upfront cost of {$9500.00} and the $ 900 monthly ad but and the $ 400 monthly service fee that started after the fourth month. 
Besides, I made sure that I paid this on my credit card for added security as I know that or thought that my credit card company would add a layer of security if they were a scam or fraudulent. It became clear that they were a fraudulent company. When I contacted them about my concerns that they werent producing the appointments, they stopped all activity when I told them that they needed to honor the guarantee. This guarantee was both verbal and in writing on their website in XXXX places XXXX in their verbiage of the program and the FAQ. I will include a screenshot of this from their website. 
When they stopped all activity, I could no longer contact them, they closed their website and I was told they filed bankruptcy. Even if the bankruptcy part did or didn't happen, the fact that they closed shop leads one to believe that I am but one client that got scammed by them, how many others fell for the same scam. 
All total, I had paid close to $ XXXX on different credit cards. I have disputed it with my XXXX, and XXXX XXXX and they since have been resolved in my favor. Barclay 's originally found in my favor and credited me the {$6000.00} and {$900.00} transactions. They sent me a letter that they had found in my favor. Last month they turned around and put the charges back on my credit. I appealed the dispute. 

Barclays keep asking if I signed the agreement and authorization. My answer is Yes, when I thought they were a legitimate business, I did. For example, If I hired an air conditioning company to come in a replace my unit with a brand new name brand unit, signed and agreement and authorized the transaction, the company then disappeared and did not replace my unit but kept my money, by their ( Barclays ) logic I would not be entitled to recover via chargeback. 
I know that consumer protection laws are in place to protect people that have been defrauded, this is what gives us certainty, security and confidence in the market. Once we are no longer protected the markets will suffer. 



Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am submitting this complaint to formally dispute a charge in the amount of {$47.00} that was billed to my account on XX/XX/XXXX, through XXXX for an oil change service at XXXX. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I attempted to redeem the voucher at XXXX and was informed that it could not be used for an oil change. I was instructed to contact XXXX for a refund ; however, my attempts to reach XXXX through the app were unsuccessful, and I have not received any resolution from the merchant. 

I promptly filed a dispute with XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX, I received an email stating that a letter was available on my account. I did not receive this letter, and upon request, XXXX was unable to provide a copy. I was instructed to access the letter through paperless statements online ; however, there is no paperless option available on my account. Additionally, I spoke with three separate representatives, none of whom were able to explain how to locate or access this letter. Despite the lack of documentation or proper notice, the disputed charge was reversed and reapplied to my account.

I have since been informed that this matter is being classified as a billing dispute rather than fraud because the transaction was authorized. While I acknowledge authorizing the purchase, I did not receive the services paid for. This constitutes a valid billing dispute under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ), which protects consumers from charges for goods or services not delivered as agreed.

The core issue remains unresolved : I was charged for a service that was never provided, despite multiple good-faith efforts to resolve this matter with both the merchant and my financial institution. 

I am formally requesting the following : A complete and documented review of this dispute, including all findings and supporting evidence Copies of all correspondence, notices, and determination letters related to my account, including any documents referenced but not previously provided A permanent credit to my account in the amount of {$47.00} for services not rendered I respectfully request a prompt and fair resolution to this matter.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am filing a formal complaint against my credit card issuer ( XXXXCiti ) for refusing to reverse charges totaling approximately {$4000.00} to {$5000.00} plus interest charges placed on my XXXX card for XXXX Ads , as part of a broader {$32000.00} ad billing incident. These charges occurred during a period when my XXXX Ads account was compromised and misused, and even XXXX acknowledged the issue in writing but failed to refund the promised credits. 

To put this in perspective, my typical daily ad spend is between {$30.00} and {$50.00}. That has been consistent throughout my years of business. In XX/XX/year>, over {$11000.00} was drained from my account in just XXXX days due to malicious triggering of dormant keywords and targeted overrunning the ads. I have run my business for over 15 years, and I have never used personal credit cards to fund advertisinguntil this event. I only used personal cards such as the XXXX store card and the XXXX card because XXXX promised a refund for the invalid activity. I trusted them and took the risk. XXXX did not keep its word. 

I did not charge the full {$32000.00} to the XXXX cardthat amount was spread across multiple credit cards. However, XXXX/Citi handled my dispute the worst, refusing to acknowledge the problem even after receiving documentation that XXXX admitted system failures and promised credits. 

Between XXXX and XX/XX/year>, a hacker infiltrated my XXXX Ads account and began quietly embedding thousands of irrelevant and low-quality keywords. Many were in foreign languages or completely disconnected from my business. Then, between XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, those keywords were triggered all at once, burning through thousands of dollars in spend. XXXX flagged the issue but failed to stop it in timeand worse, never removed the embedded keywords. 

Importantly, there is no capability in the XXXX Ads user interface that would allow a business to voluntarily spend {$11000.00} in XXXX day, nor did I ever attempt to do so. XXXX imposes strict gatekeeping controls on daily budgets and spend thresholds. What occurred was not triggered by any user actionit was a system-level failure or compromise that completely bypassed normal advertiser limits. 

The damage was irreversible. My account was so deeply corrupted by those keywords that it never recovered. After trying unsuccessfully to fix it, I had to permanently close the account. I lost both the investment and my ability to advertise through that platform. 

Despite all of this, Citibank denied my dispute and even reported my account as delinquent to the credit bureaus, severely damaging my credit score. This was not a billing misunderstanding or dispute over valueit was platform abuse, automation failure, and mishandling, backed by the merchants own written admission. 

I am attaching the formal dispute letter I previously sent to XXXX as supporting documentation herein. 

Im seeking reversal of the charges placed on my XXXX card related to this incident, as well as removal of the negative credit reporting. The documentation, XXXX emails, and my formal dispute clearly support my claim.

narrative:

merchants failure to respond properly. Citibanks investigation was incomplete and relied solely on the merchants misleading statement. As a result, I was rebilled for a service that was not fully delivered. I am requesting that Citibank re-investigate the case and consider the failure to provide essential documentation as a material deficiency in the service.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
On XX/XX/year> I reported my credit card ending in # XXXX lost or stolen to Citi. I did this as I was missing an additional card I had requested and not received. I also had suspicions about a landscaping vendor I had hired to mow my lawn 4 times who seemed obsessed with having my credit card number on file so his app could schedule my lawn.This guy quoted me {$170.00} per mow.The only charge for {$170.00} was the first time and it escalated after he stored my credit card. Second charge {$190.00}, third {$210.00}, fourth {$220.00}. This vendor was XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX, essentially a one man show with an app. On XX/XX/year> I met XXXX XXXX in my driveway and reminded him that he had provided the 4 mows I had requested and that I would pay him with a different credit card as I had to report my Citi Card lost or stolen. I paid him {$220.00} using my XXXX credit card. I hired XXXX XXXX around XX/XX/XXXX to mow my lawn 4 times as my house was scheduled to be sold in aprox. 4 weeks and I believed I was very clear about 4 mows and the house was being sold. I actually breathed a sigh of relief getting rid of this guy who just kept increasing the bill as the grass grew less and less in XXXX On XX/XX/XXXX I noticed two charges on my new credit card ending in # XXXX, XXXX on XX/XX/year> from XXXX XXXX in the amount of {$230.00} and one on XXXX XXXX from XXXX XXXX in The amount of {$250.00}. I called Citi to report these unauthorized charges made on my Credit card ending in # XXXX that are now showing up on my new card # XXXX before I had even received my replacement card in the mail. I had reported card number # XXXX lost or stolen on XX/XX/year> to protect myself from the very vendor that was now still using my card on XX/XX/XXXX, and XX/XX/XXXX. He went to a house I no longer owned and mowed it twice and charging the very card number I reported stolen or lost. My suspicions about this vendor were confirmed as he intended to defraud me. The new owners confronted him on XX/XX/XXXX asking what he was doing there and he lied to them and told them that this was a gift from me the previous owner and he was there to mow the lawn. Citi responded by closing card number ending in # XXXX and reporting this vendor to the fraud department of Citi. They issued conditional credits on my account ending in # XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX Citi 's XXXX  department sided with the vendor and put these two charges on my new Citi credit card ending in # XXXX. 
I requested a second review of how I could be held financially responsible for unauthorized charges made on a credit card subsequent to the date I had reported it lost or stolen. Citi 's XXXX  department has insisted that although the charges are after reporting the card lost or stolen I am still responsible to pay these fraudulent unauthorized charges. I disagree and believe that I did everything I was supposed to do to protect myself from fraud. I am requesting a permanent credit from Citi totaling {$480.00} for these two fraudulent unauthorized charges.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:

Home Depot sent me a letter stating that they were conducting an investigation for these charges. I received a final letter from Home Depot letting me know that they have completed their investigation. The letter states that I am still responsible for these fraudulent charges. Not sure what went into the investigation to determine that I am still responsible for charges that I never made. 

I am writing today to continue my efforts in disputing these charges. There were no itemized items on the statement telling me what was actually charged. I dont know if these charges were purchased at the store or online. As you can see my information had been compromised. 

I am requesting that these charges be credited back to my account. I never purchased anything from Home Depot for that amount of money on that day in question. If these charges were purchased in the store, Im sure you have security cameras all over the store. If you go back to that day of the purchases and review the cameras, you will see that I was never in the store. Also, if these items were purchased on-line, where were these items shipped to?

I have been a valued customer of Home Depot for many years. I have always paid my bills on a timely basis. Please continue your review of this fraudulent charge and abate all charges and interest. 

I am concerned that this will impact on my credit rating, which is excellent.

narrative:

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
On XX/XX/year> I placed an order for a XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  ( sweater ) from a company -- XXXX -- I had no idea was overseas. Order # XXXX. The amount I was invoiced was {$31.00}, however, they charged my American Express card {$38.00}. I immediately emailed XXXX 's customer support and they wrote to me on XXXX saying they would credit me {$6.00}. They explained this was due to a system error. ( Note : XXXX has two websites XXXX XXXX  and XXXX XXXX  It took about a month to receive my shrug. I was annoyed, but again, I had no idea this company was overseas. I received the item on XX/XX/XXXX. Immediately, I was not happy with this item. It was itchy. The fabric was cheap. I wrote to them the very next day, on XX/XX/XXXX. I explained in my email to them that I was not happy with the quality of the item and they wrote me back on the same day asking me to open the package, take a photo of the item and send it back to them. I sent them a photo the same day. Three days later, on XXXX, I received a ridiculous email back from this company saying " sorry you're not satisfied with our product, but under the circumstances, we can not provide a refund because we didn't send the wrong goods ''. This is the most absurd thing I'd ever heard. The issue was NOT whether I got the right product -- I clearly did. But as a consumer, if I'm not happy with a product, I should be allowed to return it for a refund. I went back and forth with them about this -- explaining I couldn't use the sweater because the fabric was cheap, itchy and XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX -- and I then reached out to American Express to dispute my claim that same day, on XX/XX/XXXX. ( Note : I can provide all emails if you need them. ) Just this morning, on XXXX XXXX 15 days from the time I submitted my dispute, I heard back from American Express and they denied my dispute and rebilled my account for the cost of the shrug. American Express emailed me this morning saying they reached out to the merchant, who provided them with their cancellation and return policy. American Express also mentioned that this company ( XXXX ) issued me a credit of {$6.00} ( as if that was a corrective action on their part because of my dissatisfaction ) but what American Express was not realizing is that this {$6.00} credit had nothing to do with my dissatisfaction. This credit was because they originally and unjustly charged my American Express card an extra {$6.00} due to a " system error '', as they called it. 

I called American Express this morning, after receiving their email. I spoke with two different people -- XXXX and her supervisor, XXXX. ( I believe they were at a call center in XXXX. ) Both XXXX and XXXX insisted that the reason they wouldn't honor my dispute and credit my account for the {$31.00} is because I hadn't returned the item after telling the merchant I was dissatisfied with the quality of their product. I explained to both American Express agents that no person in their right mind would have returned an item, paying for additional shipping out of their own pocket, if the merchant told them that they would NOT issue a refund since they sent the correct item. Again, this whole thing is not about whether I receive the right item or not. I received the right item, but I was not happy with the quality, and for that, as a consumer, I should be refunded. Why would I spend additional money to ship this back to XXXX, or the XXXX, if they weren't going to give me my money back? No one would. 

I repeatedly made this point to American Express and they continued to tell me that my claim was being rejected. After 35 years with American Express, and feeling extreme disgust with the way they wrongly dismissed my case, I will be closing my account with them. I reiterated to both American Express agents that this isn't about the {$31.00}. It's about how dishonest this merchant ( Landscapet ) is being. If I was unhappy with the quality of their product, they should have offered me a return label to send my item back and refunded my account once they received the item. Again, I did not send it back to them because I have in writing from this company the fact that they could not justify a refund since " they sent me the right item ''. American Express, as the mediating party here, should have sided with me on that, but instead, they stupidly kept on holding over my head the fact that I never returned the item ... an item that had I returned, I would not have gotten a refund for. 

I'm very upset with this company for being so dishonest, but I'm even more upset with American Express for not defending their customer of 35 years in a transaction that was morally wrong and dishonest. I plan to pay off my balance and close my account, which I also told American Express. Sadly, they didn't even really care.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
On XX/XX/year>, I attempted to book a hotel room through the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. After selecting my preferred room, entering my credit card details, and clicking pay, I repeatedly received an error message stating, An error has occurred, please try again. Reload the page. I tried to complete the booking multiple times, but the error persisted. I assumed it was a technical issue and continued attempting to complete the reservation. However, after several attempts, I realized something was wrong when my credit card was charged for these failed transactions.

I immediately checked my credit card account and found that four charges from the hotel were posted to my account, despite not receiving any booking confirmation or reference number. Alarmed, I contacted Citi Card support to stop the payments. A representative, XXXX XXXX, informed me that the charges were still pending, and promised to file a dispute once the charges were fully posted. He also proactively helped me change my card number to prevent further unauthorized charges, which I greatly appreciated.

On XX/XX/year>, after returning from my trip, I noticed that the charges had been posted to my account. I contacted Citi again and spoke to another representative who incorrectly claimed that I was responsible for the charges since I willingly provided my credit card information. This was inaccurate, as I was attempting to book a room, not completing a legitimate transaction. Citi insisted that I first contact the hotel before they could proceed with my case, despite my explanation that I had not completed any booking and had no confirmation details.

I then filed a complaint with the hotel on their support page, but received no response. On XX/XX/year>, after waiting for two weeks without any acknowledgment, I called Citi again. This time, XXXX XXXX acknowledged the error and credited my account. However, on XX/XX/year>, I received three letters from Citi stating that their investigation was complete, but they could not reverse the charges due to merchant cancellation/return policy not followed issues.

When I followed up with XXXX XXXX at Citi on XX/XX/year>, she told me that no such letters were in their system, and the investigation was still ongoing. Confused by this inconsistency, I was told to wait another 4-7 business days for a resolution. By XX/XX/year>, still no update had been provided. 

On XX/XX/year>, after nearly three weeks of no communication, I escalated the case to XXXX XXXX, a manager at Citis dispute center. He asked me to send him the screenshots of the error messages I encountered while attempting to book. I complied and sent the images to XXXX. However, as of XX/XX/year>, the case was marked as resolved in Citis system, stating that I was responsible for the charges, and that the decision was made on XX/XX/XXXX, XXXXthe same day I sent the evidence.

Moreover, there were no letters or details provided as promised, and it was clear that Citi had not reviewed my evidence before making a decision. The entire process has been frustrating, with inconsistent responses, conflicting information, and no resolution to my case. Despite my efforts to provide proof of the failed transaction attempts, Citi seems to have disregarded my documentation, including the screenshots showing their websites errors.

I never completed a booking, and I received multiple error messages while trying to pay. It was clearly an issue on the hotel 's website, and not my fault. Citi 's mishandling of my case over a 4-month period, along with contradictory information and failure to follow up, has led to months of unnecessary frustration.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I used my American Express credit card to pay a total of {$4000.00} for XXXX XXXX XXXX parts ordered from a supplier on XXXX. The payments were made as follows : {$660.00} on XX/XX/year> {$840.00} on XX/XX/year> {$1200.00} on XX/XX/year> {$1200.00} on XX/XX/year> In my request for quotation, I required that the parts have 100 % defect-free black powder coating with no blemishes of any kind. The supplier sent a sample in XX/XX/year>, but the surface treatment was terrible, and the powder coating was flaking and peeling, and the part looked to be painted. I immediately told the supplier that the sample was unacceptable and that it would need to be sandblasted and refinished. 
The supplier then gave me a written promise that if the production parts had the same surface problem, they would remake the entire order for free and cover the shipping costs. I have attached that screenshot. I proceeded with the full order only because of this explicit written promise. 
During production, the supplier sent me videos and photos of the parts. In response to one of those videos, I commented that the parts looked good and that I looked forward to seeing them in person. Those comments were made remotely based only on the photos and videos the supplier had sent. They were not a final acceptance of the finished product, as I had not yet received or physically inspected the actual parts. 
The parts arrived on XX/XX/year>. Every single piece had flaking and peeling powder coating on the corners and edges because no proper surface preparation was done. The defects were only clear after I physically unboxed and handled the parts. I contacted the supplier the same day through XXXX chat and told them the parts had the same issues as the sample. 
On XX/XX/year>, I asked for the promised full free remake. The supplier refused to provide a full remake and offered only a partial solution. I opened a formal dispute with XXXX XXXX XXXX the same day. XXXX said they would respond within three business days, but gave no answer for more than nine days. XXXX only responded after I filed with American Express. 
I then filed disputes with American Express. American Express has closed all of the disputes and rebilled the charges to my account. They determined that my earlier comments on the suppliers production photos and videos ( looks good and look forward to seeing them in person ) mean I accepted the parts. However, those comments were made before I had the finished items in hand and before I could see or test the coating 's adhesion. American Express is also requiring proof that I returned the parts to XXXX, which was never part of the suppliers promise. I submitted the suppliers written guarantee, photos, and videos of the defects, but American Express has not resolved the issue fairly.

I am requesting full credits for all four disputed charges.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Dear Citibank Support Team, I am writing to formally dispute a XXXX XXXX XXXX transaction for {$78.00} that first appeared on my Citibank credit card on XX/XX/XXXX. This charge was contested as fraudulent because my card was compromised and used without my permission, yet my credit card was not closed following the dispute. It is important to note that best practices in fraud cases recommend closing compromised cards to prevent recurring fraudulent charges. Citibanks failure to do so left my account vulnerable. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, the same unauthorized {$78.00} charge reappeared on my account. Under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) and other relevant consumer protection statutes, Citibank is obligated to block reoccurring fraudulent charges once it has been made aware of unauthorized billing activity. The fact that the merchant was able to re-bill my account despite the initial dispute indicates Citibanks failure to take appropriate action to protect my account from further fraudulent transactions. 

Again, on XX/XX/XXXX, I was billed a third time by XXXX XXXX XXXX for {$78.00}, even after disputing the XX/XX/XXXX charge. This demonstrates negligence on Citibank 's part, as I had already notified the bank of this issue. Under the Electronic Fund Transfer Act ( EFTA ), Citibank is required to investigate and prevent recurring fraudulent charges after receiving notice from the cardholder. By allowing the fraudulent charge to reappear, Citibank has not complied with these statutory obligations. 

I have attached a statement where Citibank declared the {$78.00} charge valid, yet the bank has been unable to provide any documentation to substantiate this claim. As per the FCBA, Citibank is required to supply documentation validating disputed transactions, which has not been done in this case. 

I am requesting that Citibank correct my account balance to reflect {$0.00} and reverse the {$78.00} charge. According to the terms of my cardholder agreement, I am not responsible for fraudulent transactions, and I expect Citibank to resolve this issue in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement

I contacted American Express later that evening on XXXX XXXX, and explained the situation, I provided my credit card charge for gas to fill the vehicle on XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX, my boarding pass for my flight back to XXXXXXXX XXXX, my receipt for leaving the parking garage on XXXX XXXX, the credit card charge for the parking fees on XXXX XXXX, the original paid reservation for the Budget Car rental, and the disputed receipt charges. 

I also explained to American Express that there were employees present at the XXXX XXXX rental return lane to check in the vehicle I rented. 

On XXXX XXXX I received a secure message from American Express with a message that included that I used the rental vehicle for XXXX and violated the terms of the rental agreement. 



I contacted the American Express executive office and explained the situation, and my disagreement and how surprised I was that American Express would make false statements such as this. 

I was asked to provide supplemental proof of my flight over the phone such as an additional ticket from XXXX XXXX that I boarded on my XX/XX/XXXX return flight to XXXXXXXX XXXX. I explained to the American Express executive representative that I have already provided my boarding pass, and no airline I have ever flown provides an additional ticket for all passengers who board the flight. 

I contacted XXXX XXXX on XXXX XXXX and received an email from them stating they confirmed my flight from XXXX to XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX was complete. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I received an additional message from American Express that they were adjusting my credit card charge for the disputed amount, but this could change based on a response from XXXX XXXX XXXX 

American Express seems to be having difficulties making a final determination on the truth and what really happened which is why I am sending this to your office for review. 

I have included an email from my current employer with instructions on where to be on my first day at my new place of employment, which began on Monday XX/XX/year>. 

I have receipts from gas purchases made from my commute from my home in XXXXXXXX XXXX to my place of employment in XXXXXXXX XXXX, and also receipts of local purchases made with my credit card from the Caf at my place of employment from XX/XX/year> to XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX 

In addition, I have included additional credit card charges from local restaurants I have visited and retail stores I visited in Arizona. 

If I am in Arizona, where I live and work, provide proof of me being there, why would there be any doubt of that, and why would XXXX XXXX XXXX and American Express say I returned a vehicle on XXXX XXXX in XXXX, XXXX when I was in XXXX

narrative:
I noticed a negative mark on against me on my credit report today. 
I noticed it was Citibank/home depot . I have never been late, so I called and they told me we did not pay XXXX. I told them I did not pay XXXX because of hurricane helena impacting us. We were displaced from our home and could not move back in for weeks. They told us we could miss 2 months of payments at Citibank, so we did. They marked us late when they should have been helping my family. I was also in the hospital and could not make the payment. Citbank said there is no record of them talking to us, so they could not remove it. I told them that is not our fault. The phone cut off because of the bad weather and I did not call back, but thought they had us taken care of. Surely they can remove this late mark. The BIGGER problem was a charge for {$1600.00} which was ALL interest. That charge showed up on my card in XXXX. They told me I had not paid an item off in the 2 yrs given so they added interest on. I asked them which item did I buy that I had to pay off? They told me they had no record of an item but it was {$3600.00}. I told them that is a fraud charge as I never bought anything for {$3600.00}. I called Home Depot to see if they could look up what someone bought for {$3600.00} and they said nothing is on record. They told me at home depot to file a fraud charge with the credit card company and it will go away because they have no record of anything purchased for {$3600.00}. Also, Citibank could not tell me what it was but thought it was a computer. Home Depot does not sell computers, so this is a big mistake. That being said, they need to credit my account the {$3600.00} + the {$1600.00} interest fee. Which will also remove the {$1600.00} interest charge. Please remove asap.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
On XX/XX/XXXX I placed an order on a website called XXXX for shoes. I had previously contacted the individual seller on the site requesting US men size 15s, of which they responded with a picture of shoes which I took for them in size 15. As a result I went ahead and ordered shoes, selecting size 12 as it was the largest available size. I included in the comments attached to the order my desire for size 15s and not 12 ( please see the " order '' attachment ). I also immediately messaged the seller via the platform ( please see the " DM '' attachment ) expressing my desire for size 15s. The seller responded once and then ignored my messaged and went ahead and shipped me size 15 shoes nearly one week later.

In the interim I had contacted the platform multiple times ( please see the attached text file ) expressing my concern that the seller was ignoring my messages and I was going to receive a useless size 12 shoe. The platform assured me this would be handled, even stating the : XXXX Thank you.XXXXThank you so much for waiting. Upon checking, I can confirm that the seller has indeed been unresponsive. Please dont worry as I have submitted a report to contact the seller and urge them to respond to you as soon as possible. 

XXXX XXXX assured that once a report is filed against a seller, they are prompted to take action quickly, as continued inaction may lead to penalties or even a possible ban from the platform.

Despite all of this, the seller went ahead a few days later and shipped me size 12 shoes on XX/XX/XXXX. 

I feel the seller did not have size 15s and did not want to forfeit the sale so they sent me size 12s, despite knowing it was against my wishes. The platform railroaded me when I sent this to them, and now Citi has closed my dispute siding with the seller for unknown reasons.

I agree that I messed up ordering size 12s and assuming a picture of shoes that do not show the size meant they had size 15s. However, it was clear on the order I wanted size 15s, in addition to the messages I sent them.

Despite having this information and me contacting the site/seller nearly half a dozen times pleading to not send me size 12s, I received size 12s. I do not feel it is right that I should have to pay for these shoes, as I did all I could to avoid them sending me the size 12s.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am submitting this statement regarding improper services provided by a moving company and the subsequent mishandling of my dispute by Barclays. 
I entered into a written contract with the moving company for services that included a specific, agreed-upon receiving/delivery date. I possess both the written contract and a receipt confirming these terms. Without my consent, and without providing any notice to me, the moving company unilaterally changed the contracted receiving date. 
When I contacted the company to question this change, I was informed that the altered date was what it was, and I was accused of lying despite my possession of written documentation showing the originally agreed-upon date. At no point did the company acknowledge responsibility for the change, nor did they provide any advance notice or reasonable explanation for altering the contract terms. 
As a result of this issue, I submitted a formal dispute to Barclays. Barclays failed to follow up with me during the dispute process and ultimately closed the case without providing any notification. According to Barclays own stated policy, customers are to receive a letter explaining the reason a dispute is closed. I did not receive any such letter, explanation, or notice. 
In summary, I received no proper notification, no follow-up communication, and no written explanation regarding the closure of my dispute. Both the moving company and Barclays failed to adhere to basic contractual and procedural standards, causing me financial harm and significant inconvenience.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am filing this complaint against Barclays ( Financing XXXX ) for mishandling valid billing disputes under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ).

On XXXX XXXX XXXX, I purchased computer parts from XXXX using the above-mentioned cards. The items were shipped via XXXX  and marked as delivered, but were stolen from my property shortly after delivery. I have security camera footage showing the theft and filed a police report. 


Timeline : XX/XX/year> : Items stolen immediately after XXXX delivery ; I filed a dispute with Barclays within the required timeframe. 
XXXX XXXX  : Barclays temporarily credited my accounts during investigation. 
XX/XX/year> : Barclays re-billed me despite the evidence, stating that XXXX and XXXX confirmed delivery. 
XX/XX/year> : I submitted formal dispute letters citing FCBA, which protects consumers when goods are not received in usable condition. Both banks denied my disputes again. 
XX/XX/year> : I filed a complaint with the CFPB ( Case # XXXX ). Barclays maintained their positions. 
XX/XX/year> : I filed with the OCC ( Case # XXXX ). OCC referred the complaint back to CFPB as Barclays have > $ XXXX in assets. 


Why This Violates FCBA : Both XXXX and Barclays equate merchant shows delivered with consumer received the goods. This is not correct. I never physically received the merchandise. FCBA ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ) defines a billing error to include items not accepted by the consumer or not received.

Both banks ignored video proof, the police report, and my timely dispute submissions.

They closed my disputes by shifting liability onto me, even though I had no chance to take possession of the goods.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I applied for a Citi business credit card and did not receive or activate the physical card until late XX/XX/year>. I did not have possession of the card at any time during XXXX or early XX/XX/year>. 

Between XX/XX/year> and XX/XX/year>, 16 unauthorized retail transactions were posted to the account, before I had received or activated the card. These charges were not authorized by me and total {$1500.00} : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX immediately reported these charges as fraudulent and clearly stated that I did not have possession of the card at the time of the transactions. Citi has repeatedly stated that I informed them I was in possession of the card when the charges occurred. That statement is false. I never said that, and I did not have the card. 

I have made extensive efforts to resolve this issue, including : Sending an email to the Citi fraud address provided to me Calling Citi at least a dozen times Sending a certified letter, which Citi claims they did not receive, despite my having the USPS return receipt proving delivery ( The letter they received is attached to this complaint ).

Despite these efforts, Citi continues to classify the fraudulent charges as customer responsible and has not corrected its records or properly investigated the fraud. I have also filed a police report documenting the unauthorized transactions.

I am requesting CFPB assistance to require Citi to correct the inaccurate claim that I had possession of the card, properly investigate the fraud, and remove my responsibility for these unauthorized charges.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Id like to file a complaint against Barclays ( GAP ) Mastercard for looking out for their companys best interest instead of myself ( the consumer ). 


I ordered seven clothing items from Old Navy on XX/XX/XXXX ( order # XXXX ) for a total of {$110.00}. My order was delivered on XX/XX/XXXX with five out of the seven items missing. My package was taped shut like it had been tampered with and someone haphazardlywrapped the package in shipping tape. There was also an internal XXXX  XXXX ( shipping ) HUB sticker inside my resealed package showing someone had been in my package between the ( GAP ) warehouse and myself. 
After speaking with customer service reps on XX/XX/XXXX and having my missing items claim denied, a GAP representative told me my only course of action was to dispute the charge with my credit card company but otherwise the case is closed and I'm out {$76.00}. They had no interest in the pictures I took of my package. A customer service rep alluded to me that since I have a GAP card, and GAP will do the 'internal " investigation with my disputed charge, I don't stand a chance at a refund. This is a conflict of interest. I filed with the BBB and the case was eventually closed without a resolution. I insisted this was a shipping issue, not a credit issue. 
It was reiterated to me continuously from customer service reps from GAP that they can not and will not offer a refund ; I can only dispute the charge. 

Enter Barclays : - I talked with GAP Barclays credit card customer service on XX/XX/XXXX to initially dispute the charge @ XXXX. I talked to XXXX to review the missing items. 
- I returned a missed call & message on XX/XX/XXXX @ XXXX and spoke to both XXXX & XXXX to again review the missing items. 
- I returned a missed call & message on XX/XX/XXXX @ XXXX and spoke to XXXX. XXXX alerted me I would receive a credit for my missing order in the amount of {$59.00}. I told her that was not the full amount and she insisted it was only a partial refund but I would receive the entire {$76.00} back. 
- I missed a call on XX/XX/XXXX but no message or number was left. 

Today, XX/XX/XXXX, I received a letter in the mail from Barclays GAP Mastercard stating my case has been closed and was decided against me in part because of no communication on my part. This is untrue. I have phone logs of myself calling and talking to their representatives. They also notified me they would be taking back the {$59.00} they credited me on XX/XX/XXXX. 
The proof Old Navy/GAP provided were tiny screenshot printouts of my account charges, a fragmented sentence stating based on weight all items were included, and a tracking number showing the package was delivered. They absolutely closed the claim without sufficient evidence since my claim was for missing items. 
- I spoke to XXXX from Barclays today ( XX/XX/XXXX ) who confirmed the case was closed and I can only mail or fax a letter to ask for my case to reopened. I have XXXX interest in continuing the charade that this will be taken care of appropriately. 

It certainly questions the relationship between Barclays GAP ( store ) credit card and a GAP owned company. Especially after the customer service rep mentioned/threatened this would happen. How exactly did they find in their own favor with scant evidence? I was not and am not protected in any of these situations. GAP knew by pushing me to dispute the charge, there was no way they would be found at fault when theyre investigating themselves. They unilaterally decided my case wasnt valid without any recourse. 

Its an extreme conflict of interest when they decided themselves that they didnt do anything wrong.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
We purchased a floor lamp from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/XXXX, for {$790.00} and added on a Worry Free Warranty from XXXX company for {$14.00}. When the lamp arrived via XXXX, the package was damaged ( photo attached ), and the heavy base and all the hardware to assemble the product were missing. We contacted XXXX, who said they would file a claim with the shipper and offered a refund of {$150.00}, which they said was the cost of the base or a replacement product. Based on the poor quality of the packaging, we did not feel a replacement would arrive intact either. We expected our {$790.00} to be refunded based on the warranty purchased from XXXX, advertised as Protects shoppers for product and delivery issues. XXXX is saying that the product was shipped from XXXX with a ripped box, which is not logical. Why would FedEx accept a broken box? Worry XXXX ended up not being the case. 
After multiple emails with both XXXX and XXXX, we contacted Citi bank, who issued the credit card we used to buy the lamp. Citi initially issued a conditional credit for {$790.00} to our account on XX/XX/XXXX, but then reversed the credit and closed the dispute on XX/XX/XXXX. Initially, Citi said we should return the product. We had told XXXX they could pick it up for a return, but we didnt feel we should have to pay the high cost of sending a heavy floor lamp back to XXXX XXXX XXXX never agreed to ship back the lamp at their cost. If they really filed a claim with XXXX  like they said they did, it shouldnt matter and XXXX should honor the warranty. We tried 3 more times with Citi and supplied the additional information attached here, and every time, they closed the issue as customer responsible.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
To AMEX : Urgent Complaint Unauthorized Charges and Unauthorized Payment Deduction by American Express On XX/XX/year>, my credit card was the target of unauthorized charges. The merchant name listed was XXXX XXXX. After conducting my own investigation, I determined that this is a live-streaming platform located in XXXX XXXX XXXX I have never, under any circumstances, used any services from this company, nor do I have any connectionpersonal or professionalto anyone affiliated with it. 

Upon reporting this fraud to American Express, the disputed amount was initially refunded to me. However, on XX/XX/year>, I was alarmed to find that these exact same four charges had reappeared on my account statement. I immediately contacted American Express. The representative assured me that the charges would once again be placed into dispute. I acted in good faith and waited for the resolution promised to me. 

On XX/XX/year>, I discovered that over {$5000.00} had been withdrawn from my debit card and applied to my American Express accountwithout any prior notification to me and without my consent. Only upon logging in to my account did I realize that American Express had unilaterally and wrongfully treated this amount as a legitimate charge, directly contradicting my previous fraud report and my explicit position that these charges were unauthorized. 

This conduct is unacceptable, unconscionable, and potentially unlawful. It is a blatant violation of consumer rights and could constitute unauthorized debit activity under applicable banking and credit regulations. {$5000.00} is a significant sum, particularly in the current economic downturn. I reside in the XXXX XXXX, where mass layoffs by major corporations have already placed tremendous financial pressure on households. For American Express to demand payment for transactions I did not authorize, did not benefit from, and have explicitly disputed is indefensible. 

I can provide verifiable proofnearly two years of passport entry and exit recordsconfirming that I have never been to XXXX XXXX. I have never authorized any individual or entity to use my credit card in any capacity. These transactions are fraudulent, and the withdrawal of {$5000.00} from my debit card without my consent is an additional breach of trust and consumer protection laws. 

I demand an immediate reversal of this unauthorized payment and a full reinstatement of the disputed funds to my account. I further request a complete written explanation of how and why American Express processed these charges against me after they were already identified as fraudulent. 

Failure to resolve this matter promptly will compel me to pursue all available remedies, including further complaints to the CFPB, escalation to relevant state and federal regulators, and the initiation of legal action if necessary. 

Return my funds immediately.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative: In XXXX of XXXX, I opened up a Home Depot/CitiBank credit account to purchase a quartz countertop for my condo. This account/credit card was only opened up to participate in the 24 month XXXXero interest promotion that Home Depot was offering. In doing so, monthly statements were sent paperless and my payment was set on Autopay every month from my checking account. I never carried this card in my wallet, nor was it accessible to any other person at any time. It was always kept in a home safe drawer. This being the case, I did not look at my account balance, fully knowing that there were no other purchases by myself & the balance due was paid every month on auto pay & was strictly for my countertop. I did not use this card at any other time and paid off the balance of {$5900.00} for this countertop in XX/XX/XXXX. Fast Forward to XX/XX/XXXX when I finally looked at my account for final payoff & discovered that there were 3 separate charges that I did not make, or approve, in XXXX of XXXX. ( see attached statements ) These purchases were : XX/XX/XXXX = {$1100.00} - Hardware electrical & lighting XX/XX/XXXX = {$590.00} - Hardware Gift Card Mall Kitchen & Bath Fees XXXX XXXX {$2500.00} - Hardware Gift Card Mall Kitchen & Bath Fees I immediately called Home Depot credit services on XX/XX/XXXX and began the worst 6 months of harassment and support from a Credit Card company that I have ever encountered. This has caused me months of stress, duress, and most importantly has affected my outstanding credit score of XXXX. It is currently in the XXXX- ALL due to this fraud. Upon this discovery, and payoff of what my original balance for my countertop was, I canceled any further auto payment due to the fraudulent charges being the only remaining charges listed. In doing so, late/no payments for what I know to be fraudulent charges is the cause. I did not present my phone logs at the time ( didnt realize at that time that I would need to ) of the initial dispute on XX/XX/XXXX and therefore have been harassed by almost each and every customer support person I have dealt with. I have been called a liar and told that although everyone claims to have my recorded voice approving someone by the name of XXXX on 3 separate purchases, they cant prove it to me. Initially, I was told that I was called 3 times for 3 purchases ( shown above ). These purchases were NOT approved by me, nor did I receive a phone call. I was told that I was a liar by XXXX because my XXXX XXXX XXXX  birth name is XXXX. He does not go by that name, he goes by his middle name and this XXXX  name is only on his birth certificate. I would NEVER approve of anyone by that name knowing it would be fraud as he has no need to go to Home Depot as a college student. After gathering my phone records for those dates - XX/XX/XXXX & XXXX of XXXX and several harassing phone calls by & to Citibank and Home Depot, I finally reached a helpful security advisor named XXXX who gave me an email to send the phone logs. I also included screenshots of the only 2 phone calls whos phone numbers I did not recognize on XX/XX/XXXX or XX/XX/XXXX further showing & proving that I was never called by anyone from Home Depot asking for approval for someone to use my credit card. XXXX said someone would review these logs and get back to me asap. Explaining to her the prior conversation with XXXX, She asked me if I wanted to file/report XXXX, who was extremely rude, harassing, and called me a liar over the phone. I said yes. She was apologetic for that behavior and we ended the call. At this point, I was told by everyone I spoke with that the calls for approval were made to me on the XXXX and XXXX of XX/XX/XXXX and they could not share with me proof of this - only that it sounded like my voice. No proof to me whatsoever. Even though, I had the phone logs from my cell phone number to prove that I did not receive a legitimate call from Home Depot or CitiBank. After a month of not hearing back, I emailed twice after asking for a response. I was on my honeymoon for a month and out of the country so I couldnt call. I finally called the Home Depot Security response number once again and I spoke to someone for 25 minutes once again explaining my claim. I was then redirected to XXXX once again, who claimed to have never spoken with me before. Although I knew this was false, I did not argue that, I just wanted someone to help me. I asked if anyone had looked through my phone logs that I sent by email to XXXX, and she claimed she never saw it. I resent it again to the same email and after back and forth, she THEN changes the day & claims that the actual approval call made by Home Depot to me was actually made on XX/XX/XXXX. This NEW date was NEVER told to me before, it was always stated to be on the days the fraud charges were made on the XXXX and XXXX of XXXX, XXXX. I instantly knew someone was trying to cover this up because # 1 - why would an approval call be made to me AFTER the charges were made? Before undergoing the proposed treatment plan, the XXXX vet did not tell me that the severity of my pet 's health condition was associated with a high probability of XXXX  or the guarded prognosis despite proposed treatment. I only found out about the high chance of death despite treatment on XX/XX/XXXX when my pet passed away at vet XXXX  during the morning of XX/XX/XXXX less than XXXX XXXX  after the initial vet visit.
