XXXX personnel advised me that I must sign the document to proceed forward with the treatment plan and did not provide me a copy of this document on XX/XX/XXXX. I also did not obtain a copy of the discharge report until XXXX sent me via email the discharge report on XX/XX/XXXX, the day after the XX/XX/XXXX vet visit. On XX/XX/XXXX, I spoke to my pet 's treating vet on a recorded phone call, where she told me that she wasn't shocked by my pet 's XXXX  and told me for the first time that " abrupt XXXX  happens all the time with a XXXX like this and can die no matter what you do. '' My vet also told me for the first time that guarded prognosis meant that there was a XX/XX/XXXX possibility as to XXXX  or getting her better again and told me that she could have done a better job of preparing me for the possibility of XXXX. 
XXXX is refusing to refund the {$960.00} charge on XX/XX/XXXX, and I've disputed the XX/XX/XXXX XXXX vet charge with AmEx on XX/XX/XXXX for dissatisfaction of services with XXXX. Attached is my correspondence to AmEx, dated XX/XX/XXXX, stating XXXX ( XXXX ) points for my dissatisfaction with XXXXXXXX XXXX vet services on XX/XX/XXXX, including supporting documentation. 
During the AmEx dispute process, AmEx rebilled my account but stated multiple different and inconsistent reasons that even conflict with AmEx 's own classification description of the XXXX charge as an AmEx SERVICE CHARGE, dated XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, and XX/XX/XXXX. These rebill statements are attached. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, AmEx rebilled my account, writing that " [ T ] he merchant has provided us with complete charge details for the charge in question, and signed document indicating that the risk involved in treatment and the results can not be guaranteed. '' On XX/XX/XXXX, AmEx rebilled my account, writing that " [ Y ] our inquiry is associated with a tort claim ( unrelated to the service provided/delivered ) which is specifically excluded from consumer credit law. '' On XX/XX/XXXX, AmEx rebilled my account, writing without explanation " [ W ] e 've determined that you're responsible for the amount in question. '' On XX/XX/XXXX, AmEx rebilled my account - less than XXXX hours after speaking with AmEx supervisor, XXXX- writing that " [ M ] erchant has provided us signed agreement which indicates that all terms and conditions were agreed, which may be XXXX XXXX or may cause XXXX. '' On XX/XX/XXXX, AmEx rebilled my account, writing that " [ Y ] our inquiry is associated with a tort claim ( unrelated to the service provided/delivered ) which is specifically excluded from consumer credit law. '' I also spoke with the following AmEx personnel regarding this dispute, including AmEx supervisors from XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX, who refused to answer my questions regarding the dispute process and provided me with inconsistent reasons from the above AmEx documentation regarding this dispute. In addition, AmEx supervisors engaged in lengthy communications with me- not to discuss the evidence and documentation that I had to support my reasons for dissatisfaction of XXXX services - but to try to convince me that my account must be rebilled that was based only on documentation submitted by XXXX. 
On XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, I spoke with XXXX and AmEx supervisor, XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX advised me that AmEx received documents from XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, and that documentation received by AmEx by XXXX contains privileged information that I'm unable to review regarding this dispute. When I asked XXXX why I was unable to review this information exchanged between AmEx and XXXX, she declined to answer. From XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX, AmEx supervisor XXXX called me to discuss this dispute. In appearances of propriety, XXXX feigned interest in listening to my dispute and attempted to sympathize and relate to me by telling me that he also has XXXX dogs. However, XXXX then attempted to XXXX  me into telling me that our conversation was on a recorded line and that the vet visit pertained to my pet and not me, and that the charge will stand. I responded that the charge involved my dissatisfaction of services because XXXX failed to provide me with information associated with the high risk of death before undergoing services, and that I was ultimately the person making the decision about the proposed treatment. XXXX also repetitively asked me what I wanted and I stated that I wanted full credit. Although I told XXXX that I will close my AmEx account if AmEx did not credit my account, he then asked again if I wanted to close my AmEx account with him during our conversation as a passive aggressive manipulation tactic. XXXX XXXX arrogant conduct during our conversation clearly indicated that he did not care whether I closed my AmEx account and was extending pseudo interest and lip service for the appearance of propriety. XXXX was also feigning interest in hearing the basis of the dispute to only attack my reasons on why I was entitled to credit. Although XXXX told me that the charge will be rebilled to my account, he then attempted to backpedal and said to await the outcome of the reopened dispute. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, I spoke with another AmEx supervisor, XXXX. Without reviewing my XX/XX/XXXX correspondence and supporting documentation regarding the dispute, XXXX told me that the charge will be rebilled to my account. XXXXXXXX XXXX illogical reasons on trying to convince me why the charge will remain were so extreme and absurd that I took notes during our conversation and the following are my notes from our call : I had to know what the doctor was thinking at the time of physical examination of my pet and know that what the doctor was thinking following physical examination, without her telling me about the high risk of XXXX, was the same as what was written down on the proposed treatment plan that treatment " may cause XXXX  '' before I signed ; it did not matter whether the doctor made it clear or not about the high risk of XXXX  before signing the document because I signed the document. I requested a copy of the recorded call with XXXX, but he refused and told me that recorded calls were only for internal AmEx purposes. XXXX then unilaterally excused himself from the call and told me that he was hanging up. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, AmEx rebilled my account stating that the charge involved a tort and not a service. However, AmEx 's own website information and classification for the subject vet charge classifies the charge as a BUSINESS SERVICE. Attached is the AmEx website information on classifying the XXXX charge as a BUSINESS SERVICE. 
Due to rebilling of my account of the XX/XX/XXXX XXXX charge, I closed my AmEx account and advised that I will pay the remaining balance, but will not pay the XX/XX/XXXX XXXX charge of {$960.00} for dissatisfaction of services. 
Also on XX/XX/XXXX, I spoke with XXXX  and requested to speak with dispute team personnel working on my dispute and their names and contact information. Ira advised me that I could not speak with dispute team personnel working on the dispute. I also request the calling location for AmEx supervisors and whether they are located in the United States.

My experience with the AmEx dispute process shows lack of transparency, inconsistency, and lack of thoughtful or meaningful customer service.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I disputed a credit card charge with Citi in XX/XX/year> because the merchant charged me twice for the same item. The total purchase amount was XXXX XXXX XXXX, and the merchant processed the same transaction twice. 

At the time of purchase, the merchant informed me that my Citi credit card was declining. I have a screenshot showing the charge pending on the Citi card ; however, on the merchants side the transaction continued to show as declined. Because of this, I completed the purchase using another XXXX card ending in # XXXX, for which I also have a receipt. 

Approximately three days later, I discovered that the Citi card had successfully processed and posted, resulting in a duplicate charge for the same item. I submitted receipts clearly showing that the merchant was paid twice for a single purchase and explained this during multiple calls with Citi. 

Due to currency exchange conversion, the duplicate charges posted as {$130.00} on the XXXX card ending in # XXXX and {$130.00} on the Citi card ending in # XXXX. The small difference in cents is the result of exchange rate fluctuations at the time of processing and does not represent separate purchases. 

After months of delays, repeated reclassification of the dispute, and inconsistent handling by multiple agents, Citi sent me a letter stating that they determined I am responsible for the charge. The letter does not explain why clear evidence of a duplicate charge was disregarded, nor does it address the receipt documentation I submitted. 

Throughout this process, each agent has stated that my dispute was placed into a different category, and the category has been changed multiple times. I have spoken with several supervisors, all of whom stated they understood and agreed with my dispute, yet no resolution has been reached. My most recent conversation was with XXXX ( Reference ID # XXXX ). 

I believe Citi failed to conduct a reasonable investigation and issued a determination that directly contradicts the evidence provided. Under the Fair Credit Billing Act, duplicate charges are billing errors, and card issuers are required to properly investigate disputes before holding a consumer responsible.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Summary of Events XX/XX/XXXX Due to the layout of XXXX XXXX XXXX in XXXX, XXXX at the behest of an airport employee took a commercial taxi from XXXX XXXX  to XXXX  XXXX to meet my brother who arrived on a different airline. I was told by the airport employee that the airport shuttle between terminals only came every XXXX minutes. I had never been to this airport before. The taxi ride was approximately five minutes long if that.

Upon arriving at XXXX XXXX the taxi driver informed me that the ride cost XXXX pesos, approximately XXXX US dollars. As I had just arrived in XXXX and did not have any local currency, I attempted to pay with US dollars, but the taxi drive told me he was unable to accept US dollars. I then provided the taxi drive my American Express platinum card to pay for the ride. The taxi driver showed me that the wireless handheld billing device said XXXX pesos prior to me handing him my credit card. The taxi driver proceeded to attempt to c

harge my credit card but implied that he was having connectivity issues with the device, and the charge wasnt processed. The taxi driver did not hand my credit card back. The taxi driver again entered XXXX pesos on the wireless handheld billing device and attempted to charge my credit card but continued to imply that he was having connectivity issues, and he got out of the taxi. The taxi driver proceeded to walk closer to the airport terminal as if they were trying to connect to the terminals Wi-Fi signal. At this point I also got out of the taxi as well and after a couple minutes of this attempting to get a wireless signal persisting, I then took my credit card back from the taxi driver and handed them XXXX US dollars which was more than sufficient to cover the cost of the taxi ride.

Additionally, on this date I paid for a private transportation vehicle from XXXX XXXX XXXX to our resort in XXXX XXXX XXXX which cost XXXX US dollars. 
I returned to my home in XXXX, Virginia on XX/XX/XXXX. 

XX/XX/XXXX While reviewing my credit card charges via the American Express app I noticed two separate charges for {$480.00} on XX/XX/XXXX, however in some documents American Express lists the transaction date as XX/XX/XXXX. One of the charges is from a merchant titled XXXX XXXX  and the other is from a merchant titled XXXX XXXX, both for {$480.00}. 
I contacted American Express customer service and provided a representative the same explanation as above for what occurred on XX/XX/XXXX. I stated to the American Express representative that neither of these charges were amounts that I authorized any merchant to bill to my credit card nor did I take two separate taxi rides or authorize my credit card to be charged through multiple merchants. I took one taxi ride and handed one individual, the taxi driver, my credit card to charge XXXX pesos as agreed to and displayed on their handheld billing devices screen. The representative acknowledged understanding what occurred and submitted disputes for both of the charges. 

XX/XX/XXXX I received secure messages notifications from American Express via email with the messages themselves on their website regarding these two disputed charges.

The letter for the dispute against the merchant XXXX XXXX stated M

erchant has provided supporting documentation showing that your Card was submitted as the form of payment for the transaction. Therefore, without proof of different payment method and/or a cash receipt, we are unable to pursue further. The letter for the dispute against the merchant XXXX XXXX stated In response to our inquiry, the merchant has provided the attached support indicating that your American Express card ending XXXXwas provided for payment of the disputed amount. We are providing you with an opportunity to review the merchant 's response. If you still question the charge, please provide us with proof indicating that it was paid by other means, and we will be glad to reopen the investigation.

Attached to each secure message letter was the corresponding receipt from the merchants XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX. The timestamp in the receipts is XXXX and XXXX, one minute and fourteen seconds apart. Each receipt has a graphical representation of XXXX XXXX XXXX on the second page. Each receipt contains an image of a signature which is not my signature nor anything remotely close to it. 

XX/XX/XXXX I contacted American Express and was routed to the fraud services department. I again explained to the representative exactly what occurred on XX/XX/XXXX and recapped the disputes I filled on XX/XX/XXXX. I explained to the representative the issue with the multiple charges existing in the first place, with the different merchant names, with the timestamps of the receipts, and with the forged signatures. 
I explained to the representative how I have been an American Express member since XXXX and they have 14 years of signatures they can compare the ones on their receipts to in order to see that it is not my signature authorizing these charges.

The representative stated because I provided the merchant with my credit card that meant that I authorized these charges to be billed. To this I again explained that I did not take multiple taxi rides with different merchants, I did not authorize my credit card to be billed multiple times, nor did I authorize the amount that was billed each time and that I only authorized a very lower amount to be billed to my credit card one time. The representative continued to state because I provided the taxi
driver my credit card meant I was liable for the charges. I explained to the representative that based on this logic it meant I could never hand my credit card to any employee anywhere again. No matter what unauthorized fraudulent charges or illegal activity they conducted once having my credit card or if they charged an entirely different amount or multiple different amounts that under this rationale, I would be financially liable. Basically, under this rationale if I handed a bartender my credit card and they charged multiple amounts grossly higher than they said they would that I would be liable for the charges simply because I presented my credit card to them to pay an entirely different amount.

The representative asked me if I had a receipt for the cash payment I made to the taxi driver. I explained that no, I did not have a receipt for the cash payment just the same as the taxi driver did not provide me with a receipt for either of the unauthorized and fraudulent charges which they billed my credit card for. Nearly nobody when paying cash for a taxi receives a receipt unless possibly if traveling for business and then they would probably use a credit card for invoicing purposes.

The representative reopened the disputes for reinvestigation. 

XX/XX/XXXX I again received secure messages notifications from American Express via email with the messages themselves on their website regarding these two disputed charges.

This time the letter for the dispute against the merchant XXXX XXXX stated In response to our inquiry, the merchant has provided the attached support indicating that your American Express card ending XXXX was provided for payment of the disputed amount. We are providing you with an opportunity to review the merchant 's response. If you still question the charge, please provide us with proof indicating that it was paid by other means, and we will be glad to reopen the investigation.

This time the letter for the dispute against the merchant XXXX XXXX stated We have reviewed all the supports once again. The merchant has provided attached signed record of charge for your reference. In the absence of any proof of cash payment we will not be able to investigate further. We truly want to help you with the resolution, but we are bound by decisioning based on merits of paperwork and here merchant commitment was fulfilled. 

XX/XX/XXXX I made one final attempt to contact American Express to discuss these disputes. I requested to speak with a customer service supervisor. I again explained everything that occurred on XX/XX/XXXX and my previous interactions with American Express employees. I explained the discrepancies with the receipts, the fraudulent signatures, and everything else as stated above. 
I also highlighted this sentence from the second XXXX XXXX dispute letter The merchant has provided attached signed record of charge for your reference which stated that American Express was using forged signatures as their rationale for me being financially liable for the charge. Once more I explained to the representative how I have been an American Express member since XXXX and they have 14 years of signatures they can compare the ones on their receipts to in order to see that it is not my signature authorizing these charges and clear signs that fraud was committed.

Yet again the representative stated because I gave the individual my credit card that it meant I authorized these charges, and I was liable for them due to the merchants providing these receipts. Immediately upon a transaction being submitted American Express system knows if the charge was made via the cards chip, the tap feature, being swiped, or in the information was manually entered. These receipts submitted by the merchants confirm nothing beyond charges having been processed and provide XXXX evidence to their validity. Never mind that I did not take two taxi rides, nor I did not authorize the exorbitant charges for the amount that was billed by the merchants which was one taxi driver from a criminal organization.

American Express, a U.S. registered business is facilitating international illegal credit card fraud and profiting from it through the transaction fees which American Express charge merchants for processing payments. American Express at a minimum is complicit in allowing this illegal credit card fraud to be charged against the credit card for which they are the supporting financial institution. Once becoming aware of the illegal activity and doing nothing to remedy it through means available to them. Then American Express is explicitly involved in supporting and profiting from illegal credit card fraud.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
On XXXX, XXXX, a {$100.00} cash transaction was unauthorized taken from one of my Citi credit card and saved to another Citi credit card. It gave me a highest alert because this is a cash advantage transaction with the interest rate up to XXXX XXXX or more, I immediately realized that this is a fraud transaction which I have not done and also have unauthorized anyone to do it. I immediately contacted Citi Bank Dispute Department to start the dispute. Until now I have filed at least 5 continuous disputes to Citi Bank Dispute Department, and also have repeatedly requested assistance from a personal banker form the local Citibank branch office to assist me to deal with this issue. We together have talked to at least 20 agents including a supervisor to dispute this transaction. Unfortunately, in the whole process of the past 4 months, these agents of Citi Bank Disputes Department have showed their extremely unprofessional working manners and very neglect working attitudes towards my disputes, their ignorant responses to my disputes have really surprised me : My phone calls have not only been transferred to different agents, I have been asked to repeatedly tell my experience again and again, but also I have been given different answers, responses and resolutions each time about the fraud including amount and the interests. They have not worked as a well-known professional financial institute employees, but a group of unlicensed and unauthorized street liars! 

Especially after so many investigation, Citibank Dispute Department still have neither addressed my concerns nor found out who really made this fraud transaction, but reported the past due amount and interest to XXXX, XXXX and XXXX to seriously damaged my credit score and reputation, which have caused my credit score dramatically to be dropped down from XXXX to XXXX. 

After several agents and a local branch office supervisor suggested me to pay {$100.00} to avoid bad credit in different credit bureaus in the investigation process, I had to pay {$100.00} the fraud amount. However, not as those agents promised on the phone, the interests would be removed from my credit card, they have been remained on my credit card and be counted as past due, unpaid, etc ... Until today, it still has continuously caused my credit scores to be dropped down. Those agents sounded like a group liars. 

In the past 4 months, regarding this fraud, I only received one mail from Citibank Dispute Department after the investigation finished which gave me a negative decision. Then, no matter how bad I requested, I have not received any mails to tell me the decisions. I can't trace the process at all. Each phone call made me so upset and consumed my valuable time unlimited. 

Today I just saw the result posted in my account that the investigation has been finished and no credit has been refunded to me. It means that the real fraud is not been addressed, but my money and reputation have been stolen and damaged. 

Thinking back the whole process in the past 4 months, I feel that this decision is very unacceptable, very unprofessional, very incapable, very irresponsible and very disrespectful to my credits ' safety and benefits. Can Citibank agents really be capable to handle these dispute cases? I doubt that. The whole process is a big mess! 

Dear investigator, I need your intervene to investigate this fraud traction and the investigation process how Citi Bank has made to my case. Thank you! 

XXXX XXXX XX/XX/XXXX

Attempted to dispute the charges through CitiBank, which handles the credit card that XXXX has charged. Using their mobile app, I accidentally put in XX/XX/XXXX instead of XX/XX/XXXX as the correct date of service on my dispute request. Being new to the XXXX and it being my first time to dispute a charge, I did not realize that withdrawing the dispute means taking the financial institutions ( CITIBANK ) right for the dispute. I mistakenly withdrew the dispute for the intention of just correcting the date ( which happened only few seconds after dispute submission ). Called citibank for the honest mistake and asked for assistance for correction and dispute resubmission but was told by their supervisor that they have no power to do anything about it anymore. 

Researched online and discovered that CFPB might be able to help. I work as a healthcare practitioner as well and was told by co-workers that they have heard others experienced the same issue with XXXX too. Hopefully this report not only can help me with my dispute, but also stop the company from taking advantage of people through unethical misinformation which burdens people who deserve better and honest healthcare services.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Good Evening : I am a Citi AAdvantage credit card customer. Citi has improperly denied my fraud dispute for a fraudulent {$6700.00} charge arising from Identity Theft to XXXX, XXXX ( XXXX, GA ) dated XX/XX/year>. 

Account : Citi AAdvantage Card old card ending in XXXX ( replaced by XXXX ) Cardholder : XXXX XXXX Disputed Charge : {$6700.00} Merchant : CharterUp, XXXX Transaction Date : XX/XX/year> This transaction was not authorized by me. At the time of the fraud, I lived in XXXX, Texas. The individual who committed the identity theft is someone I had known from prior business dealings ( his name is XXXX XXXX, also using the alias XXXX XXXX ). He previously obtained access to my credit information and used my Citi card information without my knowledge. 

I initially filed a dispute with Citi and received a conditional credit. Citi later reversed the credit, stating the dispute had been incorrectly processed as a purchase dispute. I then followed up and filed a fraud dispute. Citi denied the fraud dispute on the basis that I knew the person who used the card which disqualified this as a fraud dispute. Most recently, in the face of completely contradictory evidence, they denied the last appeal because they claim I " authorized the transaction ''. I did not. Let me say that again. I. Did. Not. Authorize. This. Transaction. The Merchant, itself, verified so in the phone call I had with them and the email they sent afterwards. All were sent to Citi 's Dispute Teams repeatedly and most recently to Citi 's Executive Team. 

The merchant, XXXX, XXXX, confirmed in an email that they knew they were not speaking to me ( the actual cardholder ) and that they did not speak with me to obtain authorization for the transaction. I provided this email thread to Citi multiple times. I have also provided the associated Police Report, an additional Police Report and another Police Report Number, for which I have not yet received the full report, from XXXX XXXX yet showing this person 's ongoing efforts in stealing my identity and defrauding me, to Citi 's Dispute Teams, and most recently to Citi 's Executive Team.

Despite this, Citi has refused to treat this as an unauthorized transaction and has re-billed the {$6700.00} to my account.

I believe Citis handling of this matter violates : Regulation Z, 12 C.F.R. 1026.12 ( b ) liability for unauthorized use of credit cards ; and Regulation Z, 12 C.F.R. 1026.13 creditor obligations to investigate and
resolve billing errors.

Knowing who committed the fraud does not make the transaction authorized. I am requesting that the CFPB require Citi to : 1. Reverse the {$6700.00} XXXX, XXXX charge ; 2. Reverse any interest or fees resulting from this charge ; 3. Restore my account to the status it would have had absent this fraud ; and 4. Correct any negative or inaccurate credit reporting arising from this transaction.

5. Provide compensation commensurate with the amount of emotional torture Citi 's failure to act has had as a negative impact on my life and on my time 6. Ensure that those people working in the Dispute Teams have a full mastery of the XXXX language, as to be able to correctly read and interpret Police Reports and Emails, and determine the difference between " authorized '' and " unauthorized '', and fully understand what " Identity Theft '' means on a Police Report, so that claims are correctly filed. Additionally, ensure employees on Dispute Teams know and understand Citi 's own internal differentiation between a Purchase or Fraud Claim, as to not reject disputes multiple times for erroneous reasons.

I have attempted all other means by which to ask Citi to more carefully review the evidence provided to them, including Police Reports and Email chains from the Merchant who admits not having my authorization to run the transaction. All of my attempts have been denied and/or ignored.

I now ask the CFPB to look into this situation and ask Citi to review the fraudulent Identity theft purchase once again. I have filed with the XXXX XXXX  XXXX and the FTC. I am now adding the CFPB to prompt Citi to take appropriate action to protect their cardholders from unauthorized transactions and months ' long fights to have their credit restored. This has negatively impacted my credit score, cost me interest on a fraudulent purchase and entirely too many days of emotional suffering in efforts of simply saying that it is not " OK '' for my " supposed '' bank to support the illegal activities of a known Identity Thief.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
On XX/XX/2022, I signed up for a free 7-day period trial offer from the online streaming merchant XXXX. This free trial was cancelled before the 7-day period, it was cancelled on XX/XX/2022. I received both a welcome email and an email stating that my free trial had been cancelled ( by me ) before charges incurred ( this is noted on XXXX 's system that they did in fact received the cancellation and it is noted in the attached email and a customer representative confirmed this information ). Nine ( 9 ) months later I am being charged for transactions/services, for a total of {$480.00}, that I never authorized. I contacted my banking institution, American Express, before contacting XXXX regarding the fraudulent transactions to report them as fraud. American Express at first were able to remove the transactions as they said this was an open and pending investigation and said they were going to be removed while the investigation was completed. A month later the charges reappeared on my statement, and when I contacted American Express about it, they said that the charges were valid and not fraudulent as the merchant, XXXX provided documentation stating that they were done by me. 

I proceeded to contact XXXX regarding these transactions to try to understand why I am being charged for something that I never signed up for. In speaking with them, XXXX informed me that they could not do anything regarding the refunds, because this was now with my banking institution [ American Express ], and they could not help me. 

I asked XXXX for proof of the sign-up, such as welcome emails, IP addresses, time stamps and other substantial information of where the services originated and anything else they may have, but they said they can not and will not provide any additional information since they have provided all proofs and documents to my banking institution [ American Express ] , but not to me. 

In many occasions I have contacted American Express requesting these documents but they refuse to provide them, since they have claimed that the documents are private and can not be provided to me, even though according to both the merchant and American Express I made the transactions. 

My feeling is that they are hiding information and they are manipulating User 's information in order to generate revenue. Unfortunately, my banking institution is siding with XXXX even though the information has not been provided to me. I am seeking a reimbursement in the total of {$480.00}. 

I have reported XXXX to the XXXX, the Federal Trade Commission and my State Attorney General regarding these fraudulent transactions since American Express does not want to help me. If you notice on the XXXX website, XXXX has a pattern, history and precedent of doing this type of activity and washing their hands by saying you never cancelled, and you re-initiated these services. I also want American Express to do a proper investigation where they collect information from both sides not just from the merchant.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am submitting this complaint regarding a billing dispute for a non-refundable hotel booking that I was unable to use due to circumstances that made lawful travel impossible.

I booked a hotel stay through XXXX for a stay at XXXX XXXX XXXX in XXXX XXXX. The total charge was {$3900.00}, billed to my Barclays credit card. 

Planned Travel Date : XX/XX/year> Approximately 7 days prior to departure, I discovered that my passport did not meet the entry requirements for travel. I made immediate and repeated attempts to secure a passport renewal or expedited appointment but was unable to obtain one due to lack of available passport appointments despite repeated efforts.

Because I could not meet legal entry requirements, I was unable to travel as planned. This was not a voluntary cancellation but a situation where travel was not legally possible despite reasonable efforts.

-I contacted XXXX to request a waiver, credit, or any accommodation given the circumstances. XXXX declined, citing the non-refundable nature of the booking, though they did offer a partial goodwill credit of {$300.00} which does not reflect the full loss.

-I filed a claim with XXXX XXXX for the insurance we purchase. The claim was denied on the basis that passport-related issues are excluded, despite the fact that I was unable to travel due to legal entry requirements.

-I filed a billing dispute with Barclays , asserting that the charge should be reviewed due to the circumstances preventing lawful use of the service. The dispute was denied.

-I then submitted a detailed second-level appeal to Barclays on XX/XX/year>, including supporting documentation and a full explanation of the situation. Barclays denied the appeal and upheld the charge.

I believe that Barclays failed to conduct a reasonable and fair investigation of my dispute. This was not a standard voluntary cancellation. I was prevented from legally traveling despite making good faith efforts to resolve the issue. As a result, I was unable to use the purchased service at all.

Given these circumstances, I believe the charge should have been reconsidered under principles of fairness and consumer protection, particularly where services can not be rendered or used due to circumstances outside the consumer 's control.

Due to my habit of consistently making minimum payments on my accounts and trusting that my finances were in order, I did not immediately detect the fraudulent activity. It was only after a thorough personal financial review that I became aware of these unauthorized transactions.

Since discovering this issue, I have taken steps to secure my accounts and report the fraud to the appropriate institutions. I am requesting assistance from the CFPB in addressing the financial damage and helping to resolve any disputes that may arise as a result of this identity theft. However, their investigation concluded that the account was NOT opened fraudulently and they were still holding me responsible for the charges. After calling XXXX XXXX again, they verified that the application had a different address, social security number, drivers license number, D.O.B., and that the purchase was for a stackable washer/dryer purchased in XXXX, PA. dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
XX/XX/XXXX Formal Dispute for the charge under the Fair Credit Billing Act.- Account Ending in XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Card Type : JetBlue Plus Card Claim/Dispute Reference Number : XXXX Claimant XXXX XXXX XXXX Merchant XXXX XXXX Date : XX/XX/XXXX Amt- {$770.00} To whom it may concern, Jetblue Barclays Bank is in violation of the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) by failing to conduct a reasonable investigation or provide the evidence used to deny my claim. By Law, JetBlue Barclays must provide a " Written Statement of Results '' and copies of all documents that XXXX  provided to " prove '' this case with supporting documentation showing I was aware that this item was final sale and it was visible on the product page, checkout page or receipt. 

XXXX XXXX XXXX filed chargeback with JetbluePlus Barclays . The reason was that the merchant ( XXXX ) refused to allow me to return an item. The merchant ( XXXX ) claimed the item was a Final Sale. The merchant XXXX XXXX  ) has failed to provide any evidence supporting this item as being marked final sale. 
XXXX XXXX XXXX received a decision letter from JetBlue Barclays that her case was closed in favor of the merchant ( XXXX ) The reason was that the customer ( XXXX XXXX XXXX did not return the item within 15 days of receiving it!! The denial letter did not mention anything about the item being Final sale. The supporting documentation attached proves otherwise This is a circular argument. I can not return an item if the merchant ( XXXX ) refused to provide return authorization. Merchant ( XXXX  ) did not prove any final sale status regarding this purchase. 
Barclays ignored the factual context that proves the purchase was legitimate PROOF- Attached are several screen shots of the product page and receipt showing the lack of any final sale warning! 
Attached you will also find all the correspondence with the merchant ( XXXX ), Seel.com ( extra Insurance purchased by the customer ( XXXX XXXX ) and the denial letter JetBlueBarclays sent to XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX. 

JetBlue Barclays has failed to resolve this after receiving all of the supporting documents.

Below is a very detailed description of what has transpired over the last 3 months with supporting documents and correspondence My first call to Barclays was on XX/XX/XXXX to file a formal chargeback against XXXX for refusing my return request for a purchase I made on XX/XX/XXXX and received on XX/XX/XXXX ( XXXX order # XXXX ) I explained to the Barclays representative the details of the claim and offered to provide all the supporting documentation and proof. I was informed that a case was going to be opened and the merchant will be notified and they will have 90 days to answer the claim and there was no need to send any proof at that time. The Barclays representative provided me with my claim number and advised that I will be notified ( by phone ) when the merchant responds to the claim. 
After 30 days of not hearing anything, I called Jetblue Barclays back and provide my claim # and inquired about an update to my case. I was told that the merchant has not responded yet and that the merchant ( XXXX ) had 90 days to respond. I was informed that if XXXX did not respond within the 90 days, I would win the chargeback automatically. I was also told that I would get a call from Barclays dispute department before a decision was made. 

About XXXX weeks after that 2nd call to Barclays, I received a 15 page letter in the mail dated XX/XX/XXXX - Claim ID- XXXX. This letter states that the this matter would not be pursued further due to the merchant not violating the terms of the agreement THIS LETTER STATES THAT THE REASON THIS DISPUTE IS UNACCEPTABLE IS BECAUSE THE CARDHOLER ( ME ) DID NOT RETURN THE ITEM WITHIN THE 15 DAY WINDOW.

THERE IS NOT ONE THING IN THIS 15 PAGE LETTER THAT MENTIONS ANYTHING ABOUT THE ITEM BEING FINAL SALE!!!! THE REASON THAT WAS SENT CONTRADICTS EVERYTHING I CLAIMED AND PROVIDED PROOF FOR!! 

After I got this letter, I called Barclays again and explained all of this. I asked why my claim was rejected when I filed this claim for the main reason being that I was denied the ability to return this item and got a letter saying I lost the case because I failed to return the item? I spoke to XXXX XXXX who advised me to fax a letter and all my proof which I did that night. 

I called a few days later and was told there is nothing else they can do AFTER getting my fax with ALL THE PROOF! 

Since then, I spoke to several managers at Barclays, several escalation team members and was still being told that I lost the chargeback after reviewing all of the proof that I had sent over however I spoke to a woman name XXXX and she admitted on that call that she did not have a copy of the letter that was sent to me on XX/XX/XXXX from Barclays and also did not have any of my supporting documents that was sent over but she still told me my request to re open the case was denied. 

I then contacted the " Office of the President '' at Barclays and spoke with XXXX XXXX. 
I explained everything from beginning to end. She told me she had no way of accessing anything on my case and will request my case to be re-opened. She called me back 2 weeks later and advised that I was still denied. XXXX XXXX at the Office of the President failed to provide me with a required written explanation or evidence for the denial. XXXX XXXX also mentioned that she did not have any access to any of the paperwork regarding my case but XXXX XXXX still told me that my 3rd request to re-open my case was denied and XXXX XXXX told me she was unable to provide any further information. 

Barclays is legally required to provide a formal response WITH EVIDENCE EVIDENCE meaning it needs to be proven that this item was final sale and the Customer ( XXXX ) was aware of the item being final sale ( must be visible on the actual product page of the exact item purchased ) I proved that this was not Final sale with all of the screenshots submitted. 

I am just baffled that after speaking to several different people at Barclays and providing all the supporting documentation, I have not gotten this resolved. This is unacceptable. 


Summary of Claim On XX/XX/XXXX, I ordered a ski suit on XXXX ( order # XXXX ) which was delivered to my home on XX/XX/XXXX. The ski suit was {$690.00} and I also purchased XXXX XXXX XXXX for {$14.00}. The suit did not fit me and the zipper was also broken. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I tried to process a return on XXXX and the return page did not work, The live chat did not work either so I emailed customer service and explained that I was having trouble processing a return through the website and I requested a return label to be sent to me. 


The response I got was that I was unable to return the item due to the final sale policy which made no sense because THIS ITEM WAS NOT MARKED FINAL SALE. 

There was no mention of FINAL SALE on the product page or the check out page and I sent photo evidence to them. 



This is RIGHT FROM XXXX.COM WEBSITE REGARDING SEEL PROTECTION which I purchased. 


I requested XXXX to send me proof where it says final sale and this is the response I got from their XXXX XXXX. 

XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX So I am being told that even though the item had no mention of FINAL SALE, XXXX claims to have official order records on their end that override the fact that this item was not marked final sale on the website? XXXX was unable to provide any proof after several requests. 

When I asked XXXX for their internal order records showing this item was final sale, XXXX kept responding from a Digital Concierge I responded to the digital concierge that I had purchased the Worry Free Seel protection insurance since the Zipper was stuck also. I was told that I had to contact XXXX XXXX directly. 


After receiving this response, I contacted XXXX This is the response I got from them I asked the merchant ( XXXX ) to clarify what they said and the response I got was asking me to rate my experience. This is unacceptable and wrong So I never got any proof from XXXX or Barclays. But I have all the proof to prove everything I am claiming. I am requesting my money back immediately! 

XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX NY XXXX Copy of Denial Letter from Jetblue Plus Barclays on XX/XX/XXXX is attached! 






JetBlue Plus Barclays failed to conduct a reasonable investigation- which is a violation of the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ). 

Thank you XXXX XXXX XXXX

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
During my travels abroad, I encountered an overbilling issue at a hotel, which was duly acknowledged by the hotel staff. Subsequently, I received correspondence from the hotel extending apologies for the error, alongside a request from their banking institution for Citi Bank to intervene and rectify the situation. Regrettably, my initial claim with Citi was rejected, followed by a subsequent rejection of the second claim. I've been informed verbally that the third claim was approved ; however, as of yet, I haven't received any written confirmation from Citi. 

The frustration arises from the apparent reluctance of Citi to address a customer 's legitimate concern, despite clear communication from the hotel owner acknowledging the overbilling and their bank 's explicit request for Citi 's involvement. Despite possessing undeniable documentation supporting the validity of my claim for a credit, Citi continues to dismiss my credit requests, which is deeply concerning. Furthermore, the bank has informed me of their action in crediting Citi with the amount owed to me following Citi 's belated outreach to them. Yet, I find myself unable to obtain a transparent and honest response from Citi regarding the resolution status. 

Throughout approximately XXXX to XXXX communications, the consensus among Citi 's lower-level staff unequivocally supported the legitimacy of my claim. However, there seems to be a discrepancy between their acknowledgment and the decisions made at higher echelons, resulting in continued rejection of my claims. 

This persistent discrepancy is not only disconcerting but also indicative of a systemic issue within Citi, reflecting a lack of commitment to fulfilling customer promises and an apparent deficiency in oversight mechanisms.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Product : Credit card ( XXXX XXXX XXXX issued by Citibank , N.A . ) Issue type : Billing error dispute / foreign transaction Summary of complaint : I am submitting a complaint regarding Citibank, N.A.s handling of a disputed foreign transaction on my XXXX XXXX XXXX credit card. 
The disputed charge involves an apparent currency denomination error and inconsistent merchant identification arising from a transaction in XXXX. At the time of purchase, I requested that the charge be processed in XXXX pesos, but it posted in U.S. dollars. I was not provided a receipt despite requesting one. 
I submitted a written dispute within the required time period and provided all information required under the Fair Credit Billing Act and Regulation Z, including account identification, amount, and reasons for believing a billing error occurred. A Citi representative later acknowledged that my dispute was timely. However, Citi denied the dispute solely because I could not provide a receipt, even though the merchant did not provide one and Citi did not request transaction documentation from the merchant. 
The dispute falls within the statutory definition of a billing error, including an incorrect amount, improper identification, and a request for clarification and documentary evidence. The process applied appears to impose an evidentiary burden on the consumer that is impractical in foreign transactions where merchants fail to provide receipts. 
I request CFPB review of whether Citibanks investigation and resolution of this dispute complied with the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ) and Regulation Z ( 12 C.F.R. 1026.13 ), and whether its practices provide consumers a meaningful opportunity to contest incorrect charges.

Desired resolution : Review of Citibanks handling of this dispute for compliance with FCBA and Regulation Z, and appropriate corrective action.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
RE : Citibank Billing Dispute Resolution Compliance Failure Citibank Reference Number : XXXX Original Disputed Amount : {$1100.00} | Credited Amount : {$1000.00} Remaining Unresolved Balance Gap : {$19.00} plus all accrued interest and finance fees I am filing a formal complaint against Citibank for a billing error calculation failure under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ). Citibank recently sent me an official letter under Reference Number XXXX stating they received a credit from the merchant for {$1000.00} and marked my fraud investigation as " complete. '' However, the original fraudulent international transaction total charged to my card was {$1100.00}. 

Due to international merchant arbitrage and foreign currency conversion fluctuations, the merchant 's manual partial refund left a {$19.00} balance gap on my account. Citibank 's automated system has since reversed my temporary conditional credit, leaving my subsequent statement balance completely incorrect and exposing me to backdated interest charges on a fraudulent transaction. 

The international merchant is a master manipulator who never manufactured or delivered the custom merchandise, committed a severe data breach by leaking another customer 's ID into my file ( Exhibit C ), and has already been proven fraudulent. I demand that Citibank conduct a manual adjustment to completely wipe out the remaining {$19.00} balance gap, eliminate all associated interest fees or finance charges, and fully clear my account of this merchant 's fraudulent footprint.

So coming to you as they denied my request for a refund and I do believe this is a obligation the owe me as my credit card company to protect me from fraud and this was straight up fraud from a company that isn't living by their promise for refund.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
XX/XX/year>, {$430.00} XX/XX/year>, {$430.00} I am filing this complaint against American Express for their refusal to investigate and dispute unauthorized, illegal surcharges imposed by a merchant ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ), despite evidence of systemic fraud and violations of XXXX XXXX  XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX ). 

The merchant has been systematically adding a 5 % credit card surchargewhich exceeds the legal limit and Amex 's own merchant agreementand a 4.5 % sales tax that is manually added to handwritten notes but hidden as a " Custom Amount '' on the official Square POS transaction. This deceptive practice was designed to prevent the discovery of these illegal fees. 

When I contacted American Express to dispute these charges ( some dating back 9 months ), the representative refused to assist with any transactions older than 60 days. However : This is not a simple billing error ; it is a documented merchant compliance violation and unauthorized transaction amount. 

The merchants use of " Custom Amount '' to hide illegal fees constitutes deceptive conduct, which should toll the discovery period for these disputes. 

By refusing to escalate this to their Merchant Compliance Department or open an investigation into these systemic illegal charges, American Express is failing to protect its cardmembers as required under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) and is allowing a merchant to profit from prohibited surcharging practices.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am a cardholder with American Express. My use of my Amex card on XXXX XX/XX/2024 was to hire an attorney on XXXX, a website. I hired an attorney to file one XXXX  for me at the XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. The attorney name given as XXXX XXXX, charged {$100.00} and XXXX added {$17.00} to make a total of {$120.00}. The attorney requested my card details to pay the XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX the {$250.00} fee to examine the XXXX application, the examination to be sure no one else had filed same or similar. 
I became concerned that the attorney XXXX XXXX ' seemed not to know how to do the XXXX  application in an efficient manner, so I requested to cancel the work. The attorney refused to cancel the work and so I had to let her proceed per terms on XXXX website. Nevertheless, as soon as the attorney had finished, I telephoned American Express on a recorded line and asked American Express to cancel my card and issue a new number. I explained that I had an uncomfortable feeling about the attorney and did not want to risk fraud on my card. The American Express representative declined to cancel my card but informed me that cancelling the card was not needed because at American Express, 'if it is fraud, then the cardholder is never required to pay for it '. 
Unbeknownst to me, the attorney XXXX XXXX ', without my permission or specific authorization, proceeded to use my card details to process an addition 24 charges at the XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX over the next several months ( XX/XX/2024 XXXX 2024 ). The money from my card was used to fund XXXX work and applications at the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX for other persons XXXX and to pay the attorney over and over. 
I became aware of this fraudulent activity when my subsequent American Express statements arrived, and I noted unauthorized {$10000.00} in charges. I reported this to American Express and filed a dispute with American Express. American Express later informed me that they had investigated and had decided to deny my dispute without explanation to me. 
In the meantime, I telephoned the Chargeback coordinator at the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, someone I will call 'Ms Chargeback Coordinator ' She informed me that she had recognized this fraud already, and had prepared a spreadsheet on all these fraudulent unauthorized charges. Furthermore, Ms. Chargeback Coordinator informed that she had sent the {$10000.00} back to American Express as chargeback, and then Ms. Chargeback Coordinator of USPTO.gov deleted the relevant accounts in the USPTO.gov computer. 
Unfortunately, the American Express people did not accept the {$10000.00} but instead returned the {$10000.00} to XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX with an email stating NO DISPUTE EXISTS ON THIS CASE or words to that effect. The fact is that American Express had closed the dispute incorrectly by this time, after an inadequate investigation ( despite seeing the note by Ms. Chargeback Coordinator on the portal, such note representing the merchant USPTO.gov attesting that the charges on my card were unauthorized and fraud by XXXX XXXX The {$10000.00} was thus returned to XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX by American Express in error. 
The result at the time of this report is that XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Chargeback Coordinator informs me she can not send me a treasury check directly for this {$10000.00} because her USPTO.gov accounts are closed. Furthermore, although Ms. Chargeback Coordinator and I together have joined in a series of conference calls with American Express, the American Express people tell us they are only Merchant Service people and unable to communicate directly to the Fraud investigators at American Express. 
Thanks to Ms. Chargeback Coordinators persistence however, she obtained a promise from the American Express representative that they would discuss the case with the American Express Investigative team and someone would reach out to me, the cardholder, by Mon ( XXXX XX/XX/2024 ) or Tues ( XXXX XX/XX/2024 ). 
Meanwhile, my XXXX score has begun to drop because American Express has posted the {$10000.00} to my account. Even though my American Express account has a note saying this {$10000.00} is under dispute and not required to be paid by me at this time, the credit reporting company XXXX picked up the over limit listing on my account and reduced my XXXX score XXXX points. 
The file numbers for this case at American Express are : XXXX and XXXX. 
Thank you.

However, this matter is so outrageous, I forwarded my formal demand for arbitration directly to their legal counsel. I received the identical form rejection letter dated XX/XX/23 refusing to do anything to remedy the obviously fraudulent bill. 

The facts I charged a XXXX XXXX  to my XXXX XXXX card. I declined all extras. I tried to pay a cash toll, but the attendant told me my XXXX  transaction already was charged. 
I tried to pay the $ XXXX total of tolls online after my rental as I always do, but the XXXX  website rejected my payment because it was already paid. 
There was NO XXXX  on the windshield, although some tape remained from something that was once attached. XXXX had left an unlocked, unprotected XXXX  hidden in the back of the closed center console which evidently was able to communicate with the toll facility. 

My investigation with a XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  representative informed me that while she had seen many rental car XXXX  scams THIS was a first. XXXX charged me 15x the actual toll.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
i booked a hotel stay through the american express travel portal using my american express credit card. on the travel portal, the specific hotel i booked did not state that an additional {$27.00} per night ( 4 total nights = {$100.00} ) would be charged. the email i received from american express after booking did not state i would be charged this extra fee, neither does the webpage where the booking occurs state there is an additional nightly fee. 

after my stay, i noticed the additional fee was charged. i contacted the hotel to remove the fee and they said the fee should have been disclosed by american express. i called american express and was advised to file a dispute with the merchant, which i did. american express rejected my claim and ruled in the favor of the merchant. 

following the failed dispute, i called the american express XXXX XXXX and filed a complaint with them. i've provided 2 forms of evidence showing i was never quoted this additional nightly expense. 1 ) screenshot of the american express travel portal where to this day if you try to book that hotel again it does not list an additional fee, and 2 ) email confirmation from american express that does not state an additional nightly fee is due. 

the dispute has been rejected under the grounds that i received adequate notice that i would owe the hotel that additional nightly fee. 

american express did not adequately disclose this fee and is now forcing me to pay it. they have rejected my dispute and additional complaint i filed with them. 

please help me.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
I am filing a complaint regarding Citibanks handling of a disputed credit card charge for {$550.00} for travel booked through Citi XXXX on XX/XX/2025, for XXXX XXXX XXXX 

I booked accommodation at XXXX XXXX in XXXX XXXX, XXXX, through Citi XXXX. Upon arrival late at night in a foreign country with my two children, we were unable to enter the property because the required access code was never provided. We were locked out in the rain and unable to reach the property management despite multiple attempts. As a result, we were forced to travel across town and walk from hotel to hotel - again in the rain at night with two exhausted children- in order to book emergency lodging at another hotel for the night. 

The following day, after spending much of the day calling Citi five times, a Citi XXXX agent finally informed me that the property had actually sent the access instructions to Citi, but Citi did not forward them to me. When we eventually accessed the accommodation, however, it was materially misrepresented and uninhabitable, in poor conditions, dirty, and with a strong sewage smell, raising health and safety concerns. I documented this with photographs and notified Citi immediately before vacating the property. I was forced to find alternative accommodation twice in less than 24 hours of my trip.

Despite these facts, Citi has refused to issue a full refund, incorrectly treating this issue as a cancellation subject to the propertys cancellation policy, instead of material misrepresentation and breach of contract as I repeatedly informed them. Citi has also made it extremely difficult to escalate the issue despite multiple calls over two weeks. They never let me talk to a supervisor directly, instead assigning an unreachable supervisor based in XXXX, XXXX, at a ridiculous time difference from XXXX, who never contacted me despite multiple requests. I am requesting that Citibank properly review this matter as a failure to deliver the contracted service, issue a full refund for the accommodation, and address the lack of meaningful escalation and resolution.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:

A fraud alert was raised and the credit card was cancelled, 6 of the 7 transactions were removed from my account except for one. I called 4x at different dates to resolve the last transaction that I was charged fraudulently, but after talking to the supervisor on the 4th call ( lasting at least 20-30 min per call ), all the previous calls done prior were actually not in the system. Every phone call I made, I always asked for confirmation and/or emails for proof that I called to resolve my dispute, but I never received confirmation code for the calls nor email. The {$210.00} + charge remains on my account and I was told that it would remain that way until 2 billing cycles from the 3rd time I called. Hence the 1st 2 phone calls were useless.

Why would a customer take the burden for paying a fraudulent charge when I have made several attempts to address it? Why would I be responsible to pay it while the investigation is ongoing?

Why I never received confirmation such as email or text about telephone encounters done many times to resolve the issue?? The level of difficulty resolving problems with this credit card is incomparable to any credit card company experience I had and I always pay full every month without miss. It is very frustrating.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
On XX/XX/XXXX I received an email from a merchant ( XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX ) for a purchase of {$140.00} I did not make. I immediately got on the phone with Citi, the issuer of my Best Buy VISA card, and let them know of the fraudulent charge. While I was on the phone with the representative, they informed me that my card was being used again, fraudulently, as I was speaking with them. They immediately closed my card and issued a new one. After an investigation, Citi issued a credit for the fraudulent charge a week later, on XX/XX/XXXX. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, Citi reinstated the charge, ( later ) citing the merchant 's evidence of this being a legitimate charge. I have not received this evidence, but was told it was coming to me in the mail. Regardless of the so-called evidence, it was a charge that was not made by me. As I explained to the Citi representatives, I was on the phone with them while another fraudulent charge was being made. Additionally, I had multiple previous fraud attempts, a few days prior, on cards from a different issuer ( XXXX ), including XXXX for the same merchant ( XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX ). 

XXXX correctly, and immediately, recognized these transactions as fraudulent and resolved them without me having to take further action. With Citi, I had to initially wait for over a week for an " investigation '' to complete before I was credited back the {$140.00} charge. Then nearly a month later I'm blindsided by seeing that very same charge reinstated in my account, without any formal communication from Citi as to why. I had to call them to learn that the merchant disputed my original claim, and I still have yet to receive any documentation whatsoever. 

I understand that banks will have different policies and procedures when dealing with fraud. I'll admit that I have limited understanding on how banks are regulated in this regard. However, the stark difference between how XXXX and Citi handle the same case shows the difference between pro-consumer and anti-consumer corporate behavior. I've already spent several hours of my time with Citi over one simple, clear-cut case of fraud. And I still sit here waiting, frustrated and angry, for Citi 's justification of their enablement of credit card fraud.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
On XX/XX/year>, I was expecting delivery of an item purchased through XXXX for {$3900.00}. The XXXX tracking later showed the package as delivered, however I never received the item. 
After contacting XXXX, the delivery driver admitted that on XX/XX/year> he handed my package to an unknown individual who was walking on the sidewalk near my residence. The individual was not me, did not live at my address, and the identification did not match my name or delivery address. Despite this, the driver allowed the individual to sign for and take possession of my package. 

XXXX conducted an internal investigation and interview with the driver. In a written email, XXXX management confirmed that the driver admitted to misdelivering the package to a random person and stated that the driver was disciplined for failing to follow delivery and identification procedures. 

Because the tracking shows delivered, XXXX has denied my refund request despite the item never being received. XXXX has also declined reimbursement beyond the {$100.00} insurance limit. Barclays has denied my dispute based solely on the delivery scan, despite written confirmation from XXXX that the package was misdelivered and lost. 

The item was never received by me, and the carrier has provided written confirmation that delivery was made in error to an unauthorized individual. 
Barclays denied my billing dispute solely based on a delivery scan, despite written confirmation from XXXX management that the package was lost/misdelivered and never received. I contacted the carrier as instructed and provided their written admission of loss. Barclays is refusing to consider the carriers final determination and is incorrectly shifting responsibility back to the carrier after fulfillment failure.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
An {$800.00} dispute was submitted to Citi due to a canceled purchase that the vendor confirmed would be refunded. Despite this confirmation, the vendor has failed to provide a refund after an entire month. I submitted evidence to Citi, including vendor confirmation and receipts, but the dispute was decided in the vendor 's favor. 

When I contacted Citi, I mentioned that evidence had been provided and asked for confirmation of receipt. According to Citi, they never received it, despite multiple emails sent to their designated address with the case number in the subject line. I then asked the representative to verify if evidence had ever been received from me for any dispute, regardless of outcome. The representative confirmed that evidence had never been receivednot once. 

This reveals a disturbing pattern : either Citi 's dispute email address is not actively monitored or is not functioning properly. Worse yet, Citi appears to close cases without allowing proper information to be received. I've contacted the disputes department multiple times but have never received clear answers about processing times or proper procedures for reopening cases. Additionally, during my entire relationship with Citi, I've never been asked to provide additional information, suggesting that proper investigations are never conducted. 

I receive conflicting informationfrom " cases can not be reopened '' to " the case has been reopened ; feel free to send evidence '' to " the case will take XXXX weeks to reopen, and evidence can only be submitted when prompted. '' This indicates that Citi lacks a proper process for handling customer disputes and consistently sides with vendors when it offers the path of least resistance, even in cases involving fraud or incorrect charges like mine. I've escalated this matter to their XXXX relations team but have still not received a response.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Citibank Billing Disputes department repeatedly closed my dispute without informing me of merchant response to my dispute. 
My dispute was somehow escalated to Citi Executive Response team to no avail, which did nothing more than the same as their Billing Disputes department. 

On XX/XX/XXXXXXXX2025, XXXX placed an UNauthorized charge of {$29.00} on my Citi Double cash credit card presumably for a " subscription ''. 
A full 4 months later ( a quarter of a year ), Citi is still denying refund of that UNauthorized charge, and merchant fails to respond to my email requests for refund. 

On XX/XX/XXXXXXXX/25, I purchased an automotive VIN report from XXXX website. 
XXXX presents its website as a seller of XXXX reports, then underhandedly, deceptively, and crookedly inserts subtle hints about subscriptions. 
But that does NOT mean that purchasers of their VIN reports have purchased a subscription. 
Vinseeker terms and services state nothing about subscription only mention that they MAY charge for access to website. 

There is NO mention of subscription on home page of Vinseeker.com which only states Get Your VIN Report On next Payment screen is clearly stated {$1.00} Vehicle History Report with a 1 superscript after {$1.00} Vehicle History Report. 
In lower right hand of that same screen is Plan Details box with the same 1 superscript, which does NOT link a subscription plan CLEARLY with the Report being purchased. 
Stated at " Plan Details '' is " After your 7 day trial, you will be billed {$29.00} plus applicable sales tax per month and your plan will be limited to running 30 reports per month ''. 

Nowhere on Vinseeker.com is there any link for purchasing any such " Plan ''. 

This is where Vinseeker.com deceptive marketing scam comes into play. 
If XXXX intends for Report purchasers to sign up for free trial subscription, then that should be clearly stated upfront in the following manner : {$1.00} XXXX XXXX Report * * Requires signup for 7 day free trial subscription billed at {$29.00} following trial period. 
Contact support by phone or email to cancel subscription before end of trial to avoid additional charges. 

Vinseeker.com deceptive scam can be further seen on third screen for entering payment method. 
Only there under " 100 % Guarantee is there stated in tiny print Your order includes our initial certified report plus our volume discount package... 
But nowhere is that package referred to as a SUBSCRIPTION or as a " Plan ''! 
And nowhere is it stated that the package is a 7 DAY FREE TRIAL THAT MUST BE CANCELLED OR ADDITIONAL CHARGE OF {$29.00} WILL INCUR! 

Below that is an image box Order Summary. 
To left of Order Summary text is {$1.00} with a red trial price tag. 
HOW MUCH MORE DECEPTIVE CAN IT GET!!!! 

Nowhere in that box is stated anything about volume discount package much less monthly subscription or subscription " Plan ''. 

Below the box buried in other text is {$1.00} trial for 7 days, then $ XXXX/month There is nothing that links trial to Report price or " Subscription '' or " Plan ''. 

This is a SCAM pure and simple. 
But Citicard has repeatedly denied my dispute without providing any details, any merchant response, or reason for the denial. 
I have had the Citi Double Cash card for well over a decade, have had other Citi cards before that, and currently have a Citi Custom Cash card as well. 
But Citi could care less about what a loyal customer I am, how I always autopay my bill in full every month, or how many thousands upon thousands of dollars of purchases I've made with my card. 

Citi simply settles cardholder dispute in favor of merchant, which I suspect could very well be a scam on Citi 's part to save money dealing with disputes.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement
narrative:
Transaction Details Merchant : XXXXXXXX XXXX  XXXX XXXX Transaction Date : XX/XX/year> Disputed Amount : {$670.00} Barclays Decision Letter Date : XX/XX/year> Nature of Complaint I am filing this complaint against Barclays Bank Delaware for failing to conduct a proper investigation into my fraud dispute and for improperly denying my chargeback claim. Barclays issued and then reversed a provisional credit of {$670.00} based on an inadequate investigation that did not address the documented evidence of fraud I presented. 

Detailed Description of Events Step 1 : The Fraudulent Purchase On XX/XX/year>, I purchased what was advertised as a power generator on XXXX, paid through XXXX, for {$670.00}. Immediately after completing the purchase, I identified the following fraud indicators : The sellers XXXX account had been created the day before my purchase The account had XXXX reviews The identical generator was listed by legitimate sellers at three times the price I was charged The stated delivery window exceeded one month Step 2 : Immediate Cancellation Attempt I contacted the seller immediately to cancel the order. The sellers only response was a single word : denied. The seller refused to cancel the transaction. 

Step 3 : XXXX  Failure to Investigate I attempted to report the fraud to XXXX, but their platform did not offer a fraud reporting option. I was forced to select item not received as the closest category. The fraudulent seller responded by submitting four XXXX tracking numbers to XXXX, which XXXX accepted as proof of delivery and immediately closed my case without verifying the delivery address. 

Step 4 : The Fraudulent Delivery Scheme Packages Delivered Elsewhere Upon investigating the XXXX tracking numbers, I determined that all four packages were delivered to a veterinary clinic in my town NOT to my address at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX, VA XXXX. This is a documented fraud scheme in which sellers ship worthless items to a real address ( not the buyers ) to generate legitimate tracking records. 

Additional evidence of fraud : The purchased item was a power generator. The seller declared each of the four packages at approximately five pounds. A power generator can not be shipped in four five-pound packages. 

Step 5 : Barclays Inadequate Investigation and Improper Denial I filed a fraud claim with Barclays, which issued a provisional credit while investigating. On XX/XX/year>, Barclays reversed the provisional credit and denied my claim, stating the transaction was valid based on my previous purchase history with the merchant. 

This determination is factually and legally insufficient for the following reasons : 1. My prior purchase history with the XXXX platform has no bearing on whether a specific third-party seller committed fraud. Barclays improperly conflated the payment processor with the fraudulent merchant. 

2. Barclays did not address or investigate the documented evidence that packages were delivered to a third-party address, not mine. 

3. Barclays did not require XXXX or XXXX to produce the actual XXXX delivery address confirmation only the tracking status, which shows delivery to a different address entirely. 

4. As a XXXX XXXX XXXX holder, I am entitled to chargeback rights for non-receipt of goods. I received nothing at my address.

Relief Requested I respectfully request that the CFPB require Barclays to : 5. Reopen and conduct a proper investigation of my fraud claim 6. Require XXXX XXXX produce XXXX delivery documentation showing the actual delivery address not merely the tracking status 7. Reinstate the provisional credit of {$670.00} pending a proper investigation 8. Process my chargeback under XXXX  applicable reason codes for non-receipt of goods Supporting Evidence Available XXXX  tracking records showing delivery address ( not my address ) Written statement from the XXXX  XXXX  confirming receipt of four packages containing bricks Screenshots of the sellers XXXX account showing account creation date and XXXX reviews Written exchange with seller showing refusal to cancel Barclays denial letter dated XX/XX/year> ( Reference XXXX ) I have acted in good faith at every stage of this dispute. I ask the CFPB to ensure that Barclays fulfills its obligation to conduct a fair and thorough investigation, and to hold it accountable for reversing a provisional credit without adequate cause.

dispute_type: Problem with a purchase shown on your statement

After the package did not arrive, I disputed the charge with Citi. Citi issued a conditional credit while it investigated. XXXX then told Citi the order had been delivered. On XX/XX/2026, Citi responded that it no longer had any recourse to obtain credit for me because the timeframe to resolve the dispute had passed, and that its investigation remained closed.

My complaint is that Citi closed the dispute without adequate proof that I received the goods, then refused to reconsider it based on timing even though I continued to contest that the merchandise was never delivered to me. I am asking Citi to reopen and fairly review the dispute, explain exactly what evidence it relied on, and correct any billing or credit reporting
consequences related to this transaction.
